nodes.label: Agentic AI-driven enterprise architecture: a foundational fr

nodes.label: Agentic AI introduces a new paradigm in enterprise architecture by enabling auto
nodes.text: Agentic AI introduces a new paradigm in enterprise architecture by enabling autonomous, communicative, and goal-driven agents that operate across distributed systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Building upon generative AI, agentic frameworks deliver scalable, self- orchestr
nodes.text: Building upon generative AI, agentic frameworks deliver scalable, self- orchestrating architectures capable of optimizing performance, fortifying cybersecurity, and enhancing operational resilience.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This paper presents a foundational architecture that integrates Agentic AI princ
nodes.text: This paper presents a foundational architecture that integrates Agentic AI principles into enterprise systems, offering a unified approach to intelligent orchestration, security, and adaptability.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This paper explores the convergence of agentic and generative AI through recent
nodes.text: This paper explores the convergence of agentic and generative AI through recent frameworks, emphasizing Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication protocols, intent-based coordination via the Agent Communication Protocol (ACP), and integration with LLM-powered reasoning engines.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic architectures automate complex workflows, detect and respond to cyber th
nodes.text: Agentic architectures automate complex workflows, detect and respond to cyber threats, and simulate failure scenarios through decentralized, intelligent agents.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The proposed framework incorporates event-driven communication, vectorized memor
nodes.text: The proposed framework incorporates event-driven communication, vectorized memory, and optional blockchain-backed verification to support trust, transparency, and traceability across agents.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The result is a composable, adaptive infrastructure that redefines how enterpris
nodes.text: The result is a composable, adaptive infrastructure that redefines how enterprise systems achieve agility, security, and continuity.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: When implemented with robust governance and AI oversight, Agentic AI powered by
nodes.text: When implemented with robust governance and AI oversight, Agentic AI powered by A2A emerges as a transformative force for high-performance, resilient enterprise design.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: 1. Introduction
nodes.text: 1. Introduction
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) represents a new paradigm in enterprise arc
nodes.text: Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) represents a new paradigm in enterprise architecture, one that extends the capabilities of generative AI by enabling autonomous, communicative, and goal- driven agents that operate collaboratively across distributed systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These agents interact through Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication protocols and i
nodes.text: These agents interact through Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication protocols and intent-based coordination models, such as the Agent Communication Protocol (ACP), to dynamically plan, reason, and execute complex enterprise tasks.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: When combined with Generative AI technologies, particularly large language model
nodes.text: When combined with Generative AI technologies, particularly large language models (LLMs), Agentic AI delivers self-orchestrating, intelligent architectures that optimize performance, strengthen cybersecurity, and enhance operational resilience.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This shift from traditional, centralized AI to distributed, agentic ecosystems m
nodes.text: This shift from traditional, centralized AI to distributed, agentic ecosystems marks a foundational evolution in how enterprises design scalable and secure systems.Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to a class of AI techniques that produce novel content such as text, images, or code from learned patterns in large datasets.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These technologies (including LLMs) have rapidly advanced and found diverse ente
nodes.text: These technologies (including LLMs) have rapidly advanced and found diverse enterprise applications, from automating customer service with chatbots to generating software code and business insights [1].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In recent years, organizations have begun integrating generative AI into enterpr
nodes.text: In recent years, organizations have begun integrating generative AI into enterprise architectures at an unprecedented pace.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: According to a 2024 global survey, 65% of businesses report regularly using AI (
nodes.text: According to a 2024 global survey, 65% of businesses report regularly using AI (often generative models) in their operations, nearly double the percentage just ten months prior [1].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This surge in adoption underscores generative AI’s growing role as a foundationa
nodes.text: This surge in adoption underscores generative AI’s growing role as a foundational component of modern enterprise architecture, enabling new forms of data-driven innovation and process optimization.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic AI builds upon these capabilities, transforming passive generative model
nodes.text: Agentic AI builds upon these capabilities, transforming passive generative models into autonomous agents capable of continuous collaboration, learning, and adaptation across enterprise workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Leveraging agentic and generative AI for scalable and secure enterprise architec
nodes.text: Leveraging agentic and generative AI for scalable and secure enterprise architectures is now a critical focus in both research and industry practice.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Enterprises today face mounting pressure to strengthen cybersecurity, achieve IT
nodes.text: Enterprises today face mounting pressure to strengthen cybersecurity, achieve IT scalability, and ensure operational resilience, areas where these AI paradigms offer powerful, complementary solutions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For example, recent studies highlight that generative models can enhance securit
nodes.text: For example, recent studies highlight that generative models can enhance security by improving threat detection and vulnerability assessment beyond the capabilities of traditional tools [2].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: At the same time, industry surveys show that nearly 73% of companies are priorit
nodes.text: At the same time, industry surveys show that nearly 73% of companies are prioritizing AI above all other digital investments, with 90% of C-suite leaders specifically applying AI to bolster aspects of operational resilience (e.g., in finance and supply chain), including early experiments with generative AI [2].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic AI extends these capabilities by introducing distributed intelligence wh
nodes.text: Agentic AI extends these capabilities by introducing distributed intelligence where agents collaborate autonomously, share contextual insights, and orchestrate cross-domain actions through secure A2A messaging.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This agentic coordination improves system scalability and resilience by minimizi
nodes.text: This agentic coordination improves system scalability and resilience by minimizing single points of failure and enabling adaptive recovery from disruptions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Indeed, integrating AI into enterprise architecture is increasingly seen as key
nodes.text: Indeed, integrating AI into enterprise architecture is increasingly seen as key to scaling operations: analysts note that generative AI may require fundamentally new architecture designs (e.g., more fluid data integration and specialized infrastructure) to support enterprise-wide deployment at scale.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic frameworks expand this vision by incorporating modular micro-agents, eve
nodes.text: Agentic frameworks expand this vision by incorporating modular micro-agents, event-driven architectures, and decentralized control layers, supported by vectorized memory, LLM reasoning, and blockchain-backed traceability to ensure both autonomy and accountability.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In summary, the ability to securely harness AI at scale is now recognized as a v
nodes.text: In summary, the ability to securely harness AI at scale is now recognized as a vital driver of enterprise performance, cybersecurity posture, and business continuity.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The convergence of agentic and generative AI carries profound implications for d
nodes.text: The convergence of agentic and generative AI carries profound implications for digital transformation and the future of IT strategy.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Generative AI is not just a new tool but a catalyst for reimagining business pro
nodes.text: Generative AI is not just a new tool but a catalyst for reimagining business processes and services across the entire value chain.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: It can accelerate enterprise digital transformation by automating complex tasks
nodes.text: It can accelerate enterprise digital transformation by automating complex tasks and enabling data-driven decision- making at a previously unattainable speed and scale.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In the realm of cybersecurity, fast-emerging generative AI developments are pois
nodes.text: In the realm of cybersecurity, fast-emerging generative AI developments are poised to drive a new wave of defensive capabilities: for instance, deploying AI to proactively identify fraud, analyze threats, and respond to incidents can augment human analysts and improve security outcomes [3].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Within agentic architectures, these defensive capabilities are enhanced further
nodes.text: Within agentic architectures, these defensive capabilities are enhanced further through cooperative agents that monitor threats, negotiate remediation workflows, and enforce policy compliance autonomously, significantly reducing response latency and human dependency.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: More broadly, organizations that embrace agentic and generative AI-driven archit
nodes.text: More broadly, organizations that embrace agentic and generative AI-driven architectures are positioning themselves to be more agile, intelligent, and innovative.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Enterprise architecture is evolving toward AI-centric models in which intelligen
nodes.text: Enterprise architecture is evolving toward AI-centric models in which intelligent systems are deeply embedded into core business functions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This trend suggests that AI will become a foundational element of future enterpr
nodes.text: This trend suggests that AI will become a foundational element of future enterprise infrastructures, seamlessly supporting everything from real-time analytics to adaptive operational workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic AI strengthens this foundation by enabling dynamic orchestration where a
nodes.text: Agentic AI strengthens this foundation by enabling dynamic orchestration where autonomous agents execute tasks, exchange insights, and continuously optimize enterprise performance.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In effect, generative AI is helping to usher in “AI-first” architectures, enabli
nodes.text: In effect, generative AI is helping to usher in “AI-first” architectures, enabling enterprises to transform their operations and competitive strategies for the digital age.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The next wave of enterprise transformation is being shaped by the rise of agenti
nodes.text: The next wave of enterprise transformation is being shaped by the rise of agentic and modular AI ecosystems, where organizations compose intelligent agents and pre-trained generative models via open platforms and marketplaces.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This trend reduces entry barriers and accelerates innovation by allowing enterpr
nodes.text: This trend reduces entry barriers and accelerates innovation by allowing enterprises to adopt models with domain-specific capabilities, such as legal summarization, anomaly detection, or regulatory compliance, and extend them into collaborative agentic workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In parallel, multi- agent systems are gaining adoption, wherein multiple generat
nodes.text: In parallel, multi- agent systems are gaining adoption, wherein multiple generative AI agents coordinate to perform complex enterprise tasks.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For example, one agent may monitor compliance risks, another handle predictive a
nodes.text: For example, one agent may monitor compliance risks, another handle predictive analytics for operations, and a third synthesize business reports.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Through A2A and ACP-based coordination, these agents form an interconnected agen
nodes.text: Through A2A and ACP-based coordination, these agents form an interconnected agentic layer that delivers scalable, context-aware decision intelligence across departments.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Additionally, AI observability tools are now integrated into deployment pipeline
nodes.text: Additionally, AI observability tools are now integrated into deployment pipelines to detect hallucinations, model drift, or bias in real time.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These trends indicate a growing expectation that enterprise architectures will e
nodes.text: These trends indicate a growing expectation that enterprise architectures will evolve beyond static AI integrations into orchestrated, self-optimizing AI environments, moving from reactive analytics to proactive, autonomous intelligence.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This transition from generative to agentic AI represents a structural redefiniti
nodes.text: This transition from generative to agentic AI represents a structural redefinition of enterprise systems: from centralized automation to decentralized cognition.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Despite its promise, integrating agentic and generative AI into enterprise envir
nodes.text: Despite its promise, integrating agentic and generative AI into enterprise environments also presents significant challenges and opens research questions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Security risks remain a foremost concern: generative AI systems can be susceptib
nodes.text: Security risks remain a foremost concern: generative AI systems can be susceptible to adversarial inputs and manipulation, potentially creating new attack vectors (such as prompt injection or deep fake generation) that threaten enterprise data and systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Moreover, these models often produce outputs that are not fully reliable; they m
nodes.text: Moreover, these models often produce outputs that are not fully reliable; they may generate incorrect or misleading information (“hallucinations”) or reveal sensitive data from their training corpus [4].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Such behaviors raise serious ethical and bias issues: if a model embeds biased p
nodes.text: Such behaviors raise serious ethical and bias issues: if a model embeds biased patterns from training data, its decisions could unfairly discriminate or violate compliance requirements.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic architectures introduce additional complexities around agent communicati
nodes.text: Agentic architectures introduce additional complexities around agent communication governance, message integrity, and decision transparency.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Ensuring A2A message validation, access control, and runtime explainability beco
nodes.text: Ensuring A2A message validation, access control, and runtime explainability becomes critical to maintaining trust and compliance in distributed systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Another critical issue is data governance and regulatory compliance.
nodes.text: Another critical issue is data governance and regulatory compliance.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Enterprises must devise new governance frameworks to control AI systems from mon
nodes.text: Enterprises must devise new governance frameworks to control AI systems from monitoring model outputs to managing the quality and provenance of training data to meet legal standards and maintain transparency.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Ensuring proper oversight of generative and agentic models is non-trivial, espec
nodes.text: Ensuring proper oversight of generative and agentic models is non-trivial, especially as regulations (e.g., data privacy laws and AI ethics guidelines) rapidly evolve.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Finally, there are substantial scalability and performance challenges in deployi
nodes.text: Finally, there are substantial scalability and performance challenges in deploying these models at enterprise scale.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: State-of-the-art AI workloads require enormous computational resources and effic
nodes.text: State-of-the-art AI workloads require enormous computational resources and efficient orchestration layers capable of managing agent collaboration, vector retrieval, and real-time decisioning.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In short, issues of security, bias, governance, and scalability represent key ba
nodes.text: In short, issues of security, bias, governance, and scalability represent key barriers and research gaps that must be addressed to fully realize the combined potential of agentic and generative AI in enterprise settings.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Given the significance of these opportunities and challenges, the purpose of thi
nodes.text: Given the significance of these opportunities and challenges, the purpose of this review is to examine how Agentic AI can be leveraged to design enterprise architectures that are scalable, secure, and resilient while incorporating the generative capabilities of LLMs for adaptive reasoning and automation.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: We aim to synthesize the latest research and industry developments to offer insi
nodes.text: We aim to synthesize the latest research and industry developments to offer insights into performance optimization techniques for AI-driven systems, including architecture design patterns that support distributed agents and real-time collaboration.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: We also explore AI- enhanced cybersecurity frameworks, detailing how agentic and
nodes.text: We also explore AI- enhanced cybersecurity frameworks, detailing how agentic and generative systems can strengthen digital defenses through intelligent detection, automated response, and continuous risk learning.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Additionally, the review discusses strategies for ensuring operational resilienc
nodes.text: Additionally, the review discusses strategies for ensuring operational resilience in agentic architectures, including self-healing design, event- sourced recovery, and autonomous fault management.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: By consolidating current knowledge and identifying remaining gaps, this article
nodes.text: By consolidating current knowledge and identifying remaining gaps, this article establishes a foundational framework for Agentic AI-driven enterprise architecture, highlighting best practices and emerging solutions that balance performance, security, and resilience.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Ultimately, the paper demonstrates how Agentic AI, powered by A2A communication,
nodes.text: Ultimately, the paper demonstrates how Agentic AI, powered by A2A communication, LLM reasoning, and decentralized intelligence, redefines enterprise architecture for the next era of intelligent, secure, and adaptive systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: 2. Theoretical framework for agentic ai in scalable, secure, and resilient enter
nodes.text: 2. Theoretical framework for agentic ai in scalable, secure, and resilient enterprise architectures
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: As Agentic AI becomes a foundational layer in enterprise architecture, organizat
nodes.text: As Agentic AI becomes a foundational layer in enterprise architecture, organizations are shifting from isolated AI experiments to embedding autonomous agents and LLM-driven reasoning at the core of business systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This section outlines a theoretical framework to integrate agentic and generativ
nodes.text: This section outlines a theoretical framework to integrate agentic and generative AI into enterprise architectures in a scalable, secure, and resilient manner.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The framework is defined by key components, foundational assumptions, and potent
nodes.text: The framework is defined by key components, foundational assumptions, and potential real-world applications grounded in current research while identifying areas for future evolution.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The proposed framework comprises several key components that enable robust deplo
nodes.text: The proposed framework comprises several key components that enable robust deployment of agentic AI systems, where autonomous agents interact through structured protocols to optimize performance and enhance enterprise-wide intelligence:
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: AI-Driven Security and Autonomous Defense
nodes.text: AI-Driven Security and Autonomous Defense
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agents: Incorporating agentic intelligence into cybersecurity enables proactive,
nodes.text: Agents: Incorporating agentic intelligence into cybersecurity enables proactive, decentralized defense capabilities.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Generative AI-powered agents can continuously analyze telemetry data, detect sub
nodes.text: Generative AI-powered agents can continuously analyze telemetry data, detect subtle anomalies, and simulate threat scenarios, going beyond static rules or signatures [5, 6].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These autonomous security agents, informed by LLMs, operate as first responders
nodes.text: These autonomous security agents, informed by LLMs, operate as first responders within the architecture, identifying suspicious behavior, coordinating containment actions, and escalating critical threats.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For example, in a Zero Trust environment, an Access Policy Agent could evaluate
nodes.text: For example, in a Zero Trust environment, an Access Policy Agent could evaluate behavioral context using real-time A2A messages from monitoring agents, and dynamically approve or deny access without human involvement.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This model augments human analysts, reduces mean time to response (MTTR), and he
nodes.text: This model augments human analysts, reduces mean time to response (MTTR), and helps implement autonomous cyber resilience across the enterprise [7].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Performance Optimization via Distribution
nodes.text: Performance Optimization via Distribution
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agent Orchestration: Deploying agentic AI at scale requires performance-aware or
nodes.text: Agent Orchestration: Deploying agentic AI at scale requires performance-aware orchestration, where agents run efficiently, share context, and invoke LLMs only when necessary.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The framework emphasizes optimized model deployment via Agent-level caching and
nodes.text: The framework emphasizes optimized model deployment via Agent-level caching and prompt optimization, Infrastructure-aware routing (e.g., invoking lighter agents at the edge, heavier models in the cloud), and hardware acceleration (GPUs, TPUs, Inferentia).
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Micro-agents perform domain-specific tasks (e.g., data summarization, anomaly de
nodes.text: Micro-agents perform domain-specific tasks (e.g., data summarization, anomaly detection) and offload compute- intensive reasoning to centralized LLMs only when high-confidence responses are needed.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This approach maintains low latency while optimizing costs [8].
nodes.text: This approach maintains low latency while optimizing costs [8].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Cloud-native tools such as event buses (e.g., Kafka, AWS EventBridge) allow scal
nodes.text: Cloud-native tools such as event buses (e.g., Kafka, AWS EventBridge) allow scalable coordination between agents, while vector stores (e.g., pgvector, Pinecone) provide persistent memory for context-aware reasoning.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This ensures that agent interactions remain performant, asynchronous, and horizo
nodes.text: This ensures that agent interactions remain performant, asynchronous, and horizontally scalable, supporting thousands of parallel transactions across departments.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Operational
nodes.text: Operational
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Resilience through Agent Collaboration and Failover:
nodes.text: Resilience through Agent Collaboration and Failover:
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Enterprise systems must be resilient by design, particularly under increasing de
nodes.text: Enterprise systems must be resilient by design, particularly under increasing dependency on autonomous agents and AI workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This framework embeds fault-tolerant multi-agent patterns, where critical agents
nodes.text: This framework embeds fault-tolerant multi-agent patterns, where critical agents (e.g., Risk Monitoring, Payment Resolution) include backup failover agents and redundancy.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic AI enhances resilience by predicting potential failures via LLM-based fo
nodes.text: Agentic AI enhances resilience by predicting potential failures via LLM-based forecasting agents, triggering automated incident response playbooks, and self-healing agent behaviors, where faulty agents are automatically quarantined and replaced by replicas.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Furthermore, the architecture supports cross-environment resilience by integrati
nodes.text: Furthermore, the architecture supports cross-environment resilience by integrating agents across hybrid clouds, edge, and on-premises systems with secure A2A protocols [9, 10].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For instance, a Finance Agent operating at the cloud edge can communicate secure
nodes.text: For instance, a Finance Agent operating at the cloud edge can communicate securely with central compliance agents using encrypted, intent-driven messages.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: As enterprises span regulatory domains, federated learning is introduced as a me
nodes.text: As enterprises span regulatory domains, federated learning is introduced as a mechanism for agents to collaboratively update models without exposing raw data.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This approach enhances privacy, supports compliance, and allows scalable learnin
nodes.text: This approach enhances privacy, supports compliance, and allows scalable learning across regions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: 2.1 Assumptions
nodes.text: 2.1 Assumptions
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In designing this agentic AI-driven enterprise framework, several foundational a
nodes.text: In designing this agentic AI-driven enterprise framework, several foundational assumptions are made about the enterprise context and the current trajectory of AI adoption and regulation.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These assumptions reflect the shift from centralized generative AI experimentati
nodes.text: These assumptions reflect the shift from centralized generative AI experimentation to distributed, autonomous, and agentic system design focused on performance, security, and resilience:
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Rapid Enterprise AI and Agentic Adoption: It
nodes.text: Rapid Enterprise AI and Agentic Adoption: It
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: is assumed that enterprises are rapidly adopting AI, especially generative and a
nodes.text: is assumed that enterprises are rapidly adopting AI, especially generative and agentic AI, across business functions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Surveys indicate a surge in enterprise AI investment and optimism; for example,
nodes.text: Surveys indicate a surge in enterprise AI investment and optimism; for example, AI spending grew over 6× from 2023 to as companies moved from experimentation to execution, and 72% of IT decision-makers expect broader generative AI adoption in the near future [11].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This widespread adoption implies that the framework must support large-scale mul
nodes.text: This widespread adoption implies that the framework must support large-scale multi-agent deployments, agent orchestration, and inter- agent communication (A2A) across a variety of use cases and domains.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: We also assume that organizations view Agentic AI not only as a technological en
nodes.text: We also assume that organizations view Agentic AI not only as a technological enabler but as a strategic advantage offering intelligent autonomy, adaptive reasoning, and proactive optimization embedded into enterprise systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Heightened Cybersecurity Risks in Agentic
nodes.text: Heightened Cybersecurity Risks in Agentic
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Environments: This framework assumes a threat landscape where AI, while providin
nodes.text: Environments: This framework assumes a threat landscape where AI, while providing powerful defense mechanisms, also introduces new security risks, particularly in distributed agentic ecosystems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Threat actors are already leveraging generative AI to launch more sophisticated
nodes.text: Threat actors are already leveraging generative AI to launch more sophisticated attacks (e.g., phishing, impersonation, and deepfakes).
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In agent-based architectures, new attack surfaces emerge, such as compromised ag
nodes.text: In agent-based architectures, new attack surfaces emerge, such as compromised agents behaving maliciously, adversarial prompts targeting LLMs within agents, and manipulation of agent-to-agent communication pathways.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In parallel, enterprises must address risks like data leakage through LLMs, unva
nodes.text: In parallel, enterprises must address risks like data leakage through LLMs, unvalidated agent decisions, and cross-domain message spoofing.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Therefore, security and governance must be embedded into the agent lifecycle, in
nodes.text: Therefore, security and governance must be embedded into the agent lifecycle, including runtime validation, intent logging, identity authentication, and real- time access controls.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: We assume that enterprises will require AI-native cybersecurity solutions, inclu
nodes.text: We assume that enterprises will require AI-native cybersecurity solutions, including autonomous threat detection agents, secure A2A protocols, and automated incident response playbooks [12].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These elements are critical to address the velocity, scale, and complexity of AI
nodes.text: These elements are critical to address the velocity, scale, and complexity of AI-powered cyber threats.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Regulatory Compliance and Responsible
nodes.text: Regulatory Compliance and Responsible
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agent Governance:
nodes.text: Agent Governance:
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Enterprises operate in an increasingly regulated environment where AI deployment
nodes.text: Enterprises operate in an increasingly regulated environment where AI deployment is expected to comply with laws, ethics, and governance standards.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The framework assumes that enterprises are accountable for the actions and decis
nodes.text: The framework assumes that enterprises are accountable for the actions and decisions made by autonomous agents, making auditability, transparency, and explainability essential.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The framework anticipates the need to comply with evolving standards such as GDP
nodes.text: The framework anticipates the need to comply with evolving standards such as GDPR, HIPAA, SOX, and upcoming AI-specific legislation (e.g., EU AI Act, U.S.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: NIST AI RMF).
nodes.text: NIST AI RMF).
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: It also assumes that enterprises in healthcare, finance, and government will be
nodes.text: It also assumes that enterprises in healthcare, finance, and government will be subject to enhanced scrutiny regarding how agents are trained, how LLMs are used within agents, and whether agent behavior aligns with Responsible AI principles.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: To support this, the architecture includes mechanisms for agent behavior logs an
nodes.text: To support this, the architecture includes mechanisms for agent behavior logs and decision traceability, bias detection across LLM-powered outputs, data access governance across agent tasks, and formal validation of agent workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In short, this framework assumes that trust in AI systems encompassing fairness,
nodes.text: In short, this framework assumes that trust in AI systems encompassing fairness, security, privacy, and regulatory alignment is not optional.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: It is a non-negotiable foundation for scalable and sustainable adoption of agent
nodes.text: It is a non-negotiable foundation for scalable and sustainable adoption of agentic AI in the enterprise [13].

nodes.label: 2.2 Potential Applications of Agentic AI in Enterprise Systems
nodes.text: 2.2 Potential Applications of Agentic AI in Enterprise Systems
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: By combining the components and assumptions described above, the proposed Agenti
nodes.text: By combining the components and assumptions described above, the proposed Agentic AI framework can be applied to several enterprise domains where autonomous, intelligent agents orchestrate decisions, automate operations, and adaptively respond to real-time stimuli.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Unlike isolated generative AI solutions, agentic systems enable distributed reas
nodes.text: Unlike isolated generative AI solutions, agentic systems enable distributed reasoning, collaborative execution, and scalable intelligence through secure Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication and LLM- enabled reasoning [14].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Below are three high- impact application areas that highlight how this agentic f
nodes.text: Below are three high- impact application areas that highlight how this agentic framework advances enterprise cybersecurity, performance, and resilience:

nodes.text: Detection and Response:

nodes.label: Agentic AI significantly elevates enterprise cybersecurity by enabling proactive
nodes.text: Agentic AI significantly elevates enterprise cybersecurity by enabling proactive, real-time threat management through autonomous detection and coordinated response agents.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Within the framework, generative models (e.g., GANs, LLMs) power agents tasked w
nodes.text: Within the framework, generative models (e.g., GANs, LLMs) power agents tasked with analyzing network logs, behavioral patterns, and threat intelligence feeds across distributed environments.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These agents collaborate through A2A messaging protocols to validate anomalies,
nodes.text: These agents collaborate through A2A messaging protocols to validate anomalies, escalate incidents, and trigger automated containment actions [15].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For instance, a Threat Intel Agent may detect phishing behavior and notify a Res
nodes.text: For instance, a Threat Intel Agent may detect phishing behavior and notify a Response Agent, which then executes predefined playbooks to isolate the endpoint, all without human intervention.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: LLMs embedded in agents help synthesize and prioritize alerts, while adversarial
nodes.text: LLMs embedded in agents help synthesize and prioritize alerts, while adversarial learning techniques improve threat prediction.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: By dynamically adapting to new threat vectors, agentic security systems reduce d
nodes.text: By dynamically adapting to new threat vectors, agentic security systems reduce detection latency and response time from hours to seconds.

nodes.label: 2.3 Future Research and Improvements
nodes.text: 2.3 Future Research and Improvements
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: As agentic and generative AI systems become embedded in enterprise architecture,
nodes.text: As agentic and generative AI systems become embedded in enterprise architecture, several critical research areas must evolve to support scalability, governance, and trust.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Future studies should explore how to scale multi-agent ecosystems using optimize
nodes.text: Future studies should explore how to scale multi-agent ecosystems using optimized infrastructure such as distributed compute layers, vector databases, and AI accelerators to support real-time collaboration between autonomous agents.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Similarly, more efficient orchestration of agent-to-agent (A2A) communication, d
nodes.text: Similarly, more efficient orchestration of agent-to-agent (A2A) communication, dynamic memory sharing, and model loading strategies will be required to maintain performance under enterprise-scale workloads.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Beyond infrastructure, new frameworks are needed to govern agent behavior, valid
nodes.text: Beyond infrastructure, new frameworks are needed to govern agent behavior, validate inter-agent protocols like ACP (Agent Communication Protocol), and ensure transparency and fairness in autonomous decisions.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Research should address how to build explainable, auditable agent actions, prote
nodes.text: Research should address how to build explainable, auditable agent actions, protect against adversarial threats in decentralized environments, and enforce responsible AI principles such as ethical constraints, bias mitigation, and runtime observability.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: As enterprises adopt these architectures, continuous feedback from real-world de
nodes.text: As enterprises adopt these architectures, continuous feedback from real-world deployments will be essential to iteratively refine governance mechanisms, reinforce cybersecurity postures, and advance resilient, self-regulating agentic systems.

nodes.label: 3.1 Types of Data Sources
nodes.text: 3.1 Types of Data Sources
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Modern agentic AI-driven enterprise architectures rely on a diverse ecosystem of
nodes.text: Modern agentic AI-driven enterprise architectures rely on a diverse ecosystem of data sources to empower autonomous, communicative agents and their underlying generative models.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These data sources fuel decision-making, behavior modeling, and real-time respon
nodes.text: These data sources fuel decision-making, behavior modeling, and real-time response across distributed systems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Each agent tasked with operations such as threat detection, financial forecastin
nodes.text: Each agent tasked with operations such as threat detection, financial forecasting, or customer interaction requires access to high-quality, contextually rich data.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: The following data types are foundational to this architecture:
nodes.text: The following data types are foundational to this architecture:

nodes.text: Data (Databases):

nodes.text: Highly organized information stored in relational databases and data warehouses, including ERP, CRM, and financial systems, remains essential for grounding agents in transactional truth.

nodes.label: While structured data comprises just 10 to 20% of enterprise data [18], it provi
nodes.text: While structured data comprises just 10 to 20% of enterprise data [18], it provides schema- defined facts that support validation, rule enforcement, and deterministic logic in agentic workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agents interacting with enterprise systems often use structured data as anchors
nodes.text: Agents interacting with enterprise systems often use structured data as anchors for verifying AI-generated insights or executing compliance-related tasks.

nodes.text: Unstructured

nodes.text: Text and Documents:

nodes.text: Unstructured data, including documents, emails, transcripts, social media content, and multimedia, makes up 80 to 90% of enterprise data and serves as the primary substrate for LLM-powered reasoning [19].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Generative and agentic systems derive contextual understanding, tone, and domain
nodes.text: Generative and agentic systems derive contextual understanding, tone, and domain-specific knowledge from these sources.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agents equipped with NLP pipelines can extract signals from PDFs, meeting notes,
nodes.text: Agents equipped with NLP pipelines can extract signals from PDFs, meeting notes, or knowledge base articles, enabling sophisticated semantic searches and contextualized conversations.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In agentic architectures, these inputs allow agents to operate more like human c
nodes.text: In agentic architectures, these inputs allow agents to operate more like human collaborators, processing ambiguity and nuance in natural language.

nodes.text: IoT Sensor Data: Edge-based streaming data

nodes.label: from Internet of Things (IoT) devices provides real-time telemetry critical to o
nodes.text: from Internet of Things (IoT) devices provides real-time telemetry critical to operational decision making.

nodes.text: This includes equipment logs, environmental monitors, and wearable sensors.

nodes.text: The semi-structured nature of IoT data (e.g., JSON, time-series) allows autonomous agents to detect anomalies, trigger alerts, and optimize processes at the edge.

nodes.text: IDC estimates over 40 billion IoT devices will generate 175 zettabytes of data by 2025 [20], and agentic AI frameworks must ingest and reason over this data continuously to support resilience, predictive maintenance, and adaptive control loops.

nodes.text: Cybersecurity and System Logs: Cyber and

nodes.text: As of 2025, more than 100 zettabytes of data, over half of the world’s data, is projected to be stored in the cloud [22].

nodes.label: In agentic systems, this enables horizontally scalable agents to retrieve, proce
nodes.text: In agentic systems, this enables horizontally scalable agents to retrieve, process, and contextualize enterprise-wide knowledge through vector embeddings, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and streaming analytics.

nodes.text: For example, a Customer Experience Agent might combine behavioral sequences with product knowledge to craft hyper-personalized engagement strategies.

nodes.label: 3.2 Integration of Data Sources
nodes.text: 3.2 Integration of Data Sources
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In an Agentic AI-driven enterprise architecture, integrating diverse data source
nodes.text: In an Agentic AI-driven enterprise architecture, integrating diverse data sources is foundational for enabling intelligent, context-aware, and autonomous agent behavior.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agents depend not only on individual datasets, but on their ability to reason ac
nodes.text: Agents depend not only on individual datasets, but on their ability to reason across modalities, detect interdependencies, and retrieve relevant knowledge at inference time.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This requires a unified, scalable, and secure integration fabric, one that suppo
nodes.text: This requires a unified, scalable, and secure integration fabric, one that supports both data interoperability and dynamic access control.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Agentic frameworks benefit significantly from multi-modal AI techniques, which c
nodes.text: Agentic frameworks benefit significantly from multi-modal AI techniques, which combine structured data (e.g., transactions), unstructured content (e.g., documents), and real-time streams (e.g., IoT telemetry) to enhance LLM-powered agents’ reasoning capabilities [23].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For example, a Maintenance Agent might combine time-series equipment data with t
nodes.text: For example, a Maintenance Agent might combine time-series equipment data with technician reports and inventory records to generate a predictive alert or action plan.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Integrating modalities enhances not only the accuracy but also the explainabilit
nodes.text: Integrating modalities enhances not only the accuracy but also the explainability of agent decisions, as each inference can draw upon multiple, verifiable inputs.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Modern architectures increasingly rely on vector databases, embedding frameworks
nodes.text: Modern architectures increasingly rely on vector databases, embedding frameworks, and semantic indexing to create unified representations across data types.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Text documents, SQL rows, log files, and metadata are encoded into vector spaces
nodes.text: Text documents, SQL rows, log files, and metadata are encoded into vector spaces that agents can query dynamically.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This enables advanced retrieval- augmented generation (RAG), where agents, rathe
nodes.text: This enables advanced retrieval- augmented generation (RAG), where agents, rather than relying on static model memory, pull up-to- date information from enterprise knowledge bases to ground their responses.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This fusion of structured and unstructured data supports both precision and flex
nodes.text: This fusion of structured and unstructured data supports both precision and flexibility in generative workflows [24], especially in mission-critical areas such as compliance, security, or customer engagement.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Moreover, agentic systems benefit from emerging data fabric architectures, which
nodes.text: Moreover, agentic systems benefit from emerging data fabric architectures, which provide a semantic layer across siloed systems and enforce consistent data access rules.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These fabrics allow agents to maintain secure communication and policy-aligned c
nodes.text: These fabrics allow agents to maintain secure communication and policy-aligned coordination across domains essential for preserving enterprise security and compliance posture.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: A particularly important innovation in integration is federated learning, which
nodes.text: A particularly important innovation in integration is federated learning, which allows agents to collaboratively train models across decentralized data environments without centralizing sensitive information.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This is crucial in sectors such as healthcare and finance, where data residency
nodes.text: This is crucial in sectors such as healthcare and finance, where data residency and privacy are non-negotiable [25].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: By enabling edge- resident agents (e.g., in hospitals, branches, or remote devic
nodes.text: By enabling edge- resident agents (e.g., in hospitals, branches, or remote devices) to update shared models locally while exchanging only weights or gradients, federated approaches preserve both data privacy and model quality [26].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This also reinforces operational resilience: even if a node becomes unavailable,
nodes.text: This also reinforces operational resilience: even if a node becomes unavailable, agents in other environments continue learning and functioning independently [27].
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: In practice, agentic architectures orchestrate A2A interactions across shared me
nodes.text: In practice, agentic architectures orchestrate A2A interactions across shared memory systems, event buses, and secure APIs to exchange insights and trigger workflows.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: For example, a Customer Service Agent interacting with a user might query a Bill
nodes.text: For example, a Customer Service Agent interacting with a user might query a Billing Agent, which then retrieves structured payment records while cross-referencing recent email exchanges from unstructured sources.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: This modular, composable integration allows for dynamic agent collaboration and
nodes.text: This modular, composable integration allows for dynamic agent collaboration and creates an adaptable infrastructure that scales across lines of business, geographies, and data silos.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Ultimately, well-integrated data ecosystems are critical enablers of the agility
nodes.text: Ultimately, well-integrated data ecosystems are critical enablers of the agility, security, and resilience promised by agentic AI.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: As organizations evolve toward this architecture, the focus will increasingly sh
nodes.text: As organizations evolve toward this architecture, the focus will increasingly shift toward embedding intelligence at the integration layer, allowing agents not only to access and combine information but to reason over it autonomously and act within governed boundaries.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.text: This cloud-edge topology allows agents at the edge (such as in factories, hospitals, or retail stores) to perform latency- sensitive inference while cloud agents handle centralized coordination, global state updates, and model retraining [29].

nodes.text: The secure coordination between these agents using protocols like Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) or A2A orchestration ensures business continuity even when connectivity is intermittent, making the architecture resilient by design.

nodes.label: Despite the growing body of literature on AI in enterprise systems, current fram
nodes.text: Despite the growing body of literature on AI in enterprise systems, current frameworks are insufficient to meet the evolving demands of scalability, resilience, and security in generative AI environments.

nodes.label: Legacy architecture methodologies such as TOGAF, ITIL, or siloed AI integration
nodes.text: Legacy architecture methodologies such as TOGAF, ITIL, or siloed AI integration models fail to account for the continuous learning cycles, distributed agentic behaviors, and dynamic performance characteristics that define modern enterprise AI.

nodes.label: These models often result in fragmented implementations where AI capabilities ar
nodes.text: These models often result in fragmented implementations where AI capabilities are layered on top of legacy infrastructure rather than being natively embedded.

nodes.label: Similarly, traditional AI deployment strategies emphasize model training or infe
nodes.text: Similarly, traditional AI deployment strategies emphasize model training or inference accuracy but rarely address the complexities of integrating these systems across heterogeneous IT landscapes with shared data pipelines and governance layers [31, 32].

nodes.label: The literature remains fragmented across subdomains, and risk controls, complian
nodes.text: The literature remains fragmented across subdomains, and risk controls, compliance enforcement, and optimization strategies are often treated as isolated concerns.

nodes.label: This fragmentation leads to architectural trade-offs that can undermine scalabil
nodes.text: This fragmentation leads to architectural trade-offs that can undermine scalability or create governance blind spots [33].

nodes.label: Our proposed agentic framework addresses this critical gap by integrating genera
nodes.text: Our proposed agentic framework addresses this critical gap by integrating generative AI into a cohesive, multi-layered enterprise architecture, one that incorporates AI security, performance orchestration, multi-agent governance, and operational continuity into a unified model.

nodes.label: Specifically, the framework supports cross-agent communication via protocols suc
nodes.text: Specifically, the framework supports cross-agent communication via protocols such as A2A and ACP, integrates federated learning to preserve privacy, and enables zero-trust, self-healing system behavior.

nodes.label: It also embeds resilience mechanisms capable of anticipating and responding to f
nodes.text: It also embeds resilience mechanisms capable of anticipating and responding to failures or attacks in real time.

nodes.text: By merging architectural rigor with AI-native design, the model moves beyond piecemeal enhancements to offer a next-generation theory for enterprise AI enablement, one that bridges agility and trust at scale [34, 35].

nodes.label: The proposed framework establishes a new foundation for secure, scalable, and in
nodes.text: The proposed framework establishes a new foundation for secure, scalable, and intelligent enterprise systems.

nodes.label: This paper presents a comprehensive framework for integrating Agentic AI and Gen
nodes.text: This paper presents a comprehensive framework for integrating Agentic AI and Generative AI into enterprise architecture, enabling organizations to achieve unprecedented levels of scalability, security, and operational resilience.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Unlike traditional models that rely on static, rule-based systems, the proposed
nodes.text: Unlike traditional models that rely on static, rule-based systems, the proposed architecture leverages modular AI agents, data fabric orchestration, and secure communication protocols such as A2A, ACP, and MCP to deliver real-time, autonomous, and explainable AI capabilities.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: These agentic systems are designed to collaborate across domains such as securit
nodes.text: These agentic systems are designed to collaborate across domains such as security, compliance, optimization, and customer experience, allowing enterprises to shift from reactive operations to proactive, self-healing ecosystems.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: Generative AI plays a pivotal role within this architecture, not only enhancing
nodes.text: Generative AI plays a pivotal role within this architecture, not only enhancing predictive accuracy and cybersecurity but also enabling continuous learning, multi-modal integration, and synthetic simulation of threats and failures.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: By embedding LLM-augmented agents into core enterprise functions and leveraging
nodes.text: By embedding LLM-augmented agents into core enterprise functions and leveraging real-time pipelines and vector stores, the model supports low- latency, high-reliability operations across distributed cloud-edge environments.
nodes.author: Prince Kumar

nodes.label: From invoice validation in receivables to autonomous fraud detection in payables
nodes.text: From invoice validation in receivables to autonomous fraud detection in payables, this framework demonstrates how generative and agentic intelligence can be operationalized across verticals, transforming siloed legacy systems into cohesive, intelligent platforms.

nodes.text: Looking ahead, this shift toward agent-based, modular architectures will redefine the enterprise technology landscape.

nodes.text: Enterprises will increasingly curate AI components, foundation models, orchestration protocols, and compliance agents from evolving marketplaces and integrate them via AI and API gateways into their operational stacks.

nodes.label: This modularity supports not only technical agility but also enables scalable AI

nodes.text: At the same time, the rise of adversarial AI and AI- powered cyber threats calls for a parallel advancement in generative AI-driven defense systems.

nodes.text: As attackers adopt more sophisticated tools, enterprise architectures must be fortified with resilient, continuously learning AI layers capable of preempting zero-day attacks and supporting automated recovery.

nodes.label: Business Architecture Copilot: Agentic AI-enabled Business C
Business Architecture (BA) plays a significant role in aligning business and IT.
nodes.text: Business Architecture (BA) plays a significant role in aligning business and IT.

nodes.label: Current methods in BA artefact generation and maintenance are largely manual, st
nodes.text: Current methods in BA artefact generation and maintenance are largely manual, static, and time-consuming, which limits their effectiveness in dynamic and complex decision-making environments.

nodes.label: Large Language Models (LLMs) have showcased their potential in automating the ge
nodes.text: Large Language Models (LLMs) have showcased their potential in automating the generation of BA artefacts through customised prompting techniques.

nodes.label: However, they face a key challenge due to the tendency of LLMs to hallucinate, r
nodes.text: However, they face a key challenge due to the tendency of LLMs to hallucinate, resulting in outputs that may be inaccurate or inconsistent.

nodes.label: Agentic AI offers an alternative approach to LLM-based BA modelling, due to its
nodes.text: Agentic AI offers an alternative approach to LLM-based BA modelling, due to its ability to make autonomous decisions, adapt through learning, and process data in real-time.

nodes.label: To investigate their potential, this research project proposes an Agentic AI-ena
nodes.text: To investigate their potential, this research project proposes an Agentic AI-enabled business architecture modelling approach (BA Copilot) that leverages AI agents to automate the generation of BA artefacts.

nodes.label: Our work employs an agentic system, implemented using the AutoGen 0.2 framework,
nodes.text: Our work employs an agentic system, implemented using the AutoGen 0.2 framework, integrated with an Enterprise Knowledge Graph (EKG) to semantically contextualise organisational knowledge.

nodes.label: The proposed system enables AI agents to extract, reason over, and organise busi
nodes.text: The proposed system enables AI agents to extract, reason over, and organise business architecture artefacts.

nodes.label: The key outcomes of this research project are the Agentic AI-enabled Business Mo
nodes.text: The key outcomes of this research project are the Agentic AI-enabled Business Modelling Architecture (ABMA) and an agent-based capability modelling process, designed to enhance decision-making in modern enterprise contexts.

nodes.label: By incorporating the knowledge graph and reasoning capabilities through LLM in o
nodes.text: By incorporating the knowledge graph and reasoning capabilities through LLM in our design, we aim to facilitate user trust in the AI-generated BA artefacts as well.

nodes.label: The current progress of this project involves designing ABMA and implementing a
nodes.text: The current progress of this project involves designing ABMA and implementing a prototype within the scope of business capability modelling (BCM), producing promising preliminary results.

nodes.label: We plan to evaluate and extend this work to other BA artefacts and develop a too
nodes.text: We plan to evaluate and extend this work to other BA artefacts and develop a tool that practitioners can use based on our findings.

nodes.label: Capability modelling, Enterprise knowledge graphs, Agentic AI, Business Architec
nodes.text: Capability modelling, Enterprise knowledge graphs, Agentic AI, Business Architecture, Decision Making

nodes.label: 1. Introduction
nodes.text: 1. Introduction

nodes.label: Organisations are increasingly interested in the digitalisation of their organis
nodes.text: Organisations are increasingly interested in the digitalisation of their organisational processes and workflow [1].

nodes.label: This requires organisations to fundamentally change their business models and op
nodes.text: This requires organisations to fundamentally change their business models and operating environment by adopting contemporary data and digital technologies, such as AI/ML [2].

nodes.label: Despite the potential influence of data and digital technologies on BA methods,
nodes.text: Despite the potential influence of data and digital technologies on BA methods, a key challenge for organisations is identifying the capabilities, tasks and decisions that can be augmented or supported via AI.

nodes.label: This draws our attention to the BA methods within the enterprise architecture (E
nodes.text: This draws our attention to the BA methods within the enterprise architecture (EA) discipline [3] and how they can support organisations’ digital technology-enabled transformations.

nodes.label: BA, according to TOGAF [4], is “a description of the structure and interaction b
nodes.text: BA, according to TOGAF [4], is “a description of the structure and interaction between the business strategy, organisation, functions, business processes, and information needs”.

nodes.label: BA supports various ER2025: Companion Proceedings of the 44th International Conf
nodes.text: BA supports various ER2025: Companion Proceedings of the 44th International Conference on Conceptual Modeling: Industrial Track, ER Forum, 8th SCME, Doctoral Consortium, Tutorials, Project Exhibitions, Posters and Demos, October 20-23, 2025, Poitiers, France *Corresponding author eesha.oaj@student.uts.edu.au (E.

nodes.label: Oaj); asif.gill@uts.edu.au (A.
nodes.text: Oaj); asif.gill@uts.edu.au (A.

nodes.label: Gill); madhushi.bandara@uts.edu.au (M.
nodes.text: Gill); madhushi.bandara@uts.edu.au (M.

nodes.label: Bandara); troach@capsifi.com (T.
nodes.text: Bandara); troach@capsifi.com (T.

nodes.label: business stakeholders in decision-making by representing and analysing data arou
nodes.text: business stakeholders in decision-making by representing and analysing data around entities that govern an enterprise, such as business strategies, goals, capabilities, pain points, business processes, applications, and rules and regulations [4].

nodes.label: Key BA artefacts such as capability maps, value stream maps, and application por
nodes.text: Key BA artefacts such as capability maps, value stream maps, and application portfolios provide crucial information for IT investments, performance analysis, and resource utilisation [5].

nodes.label: However, current BA artefacts are static; they become outdated quickly and are u
nodes.text: However, current BA artefacts are static; they become outdated quickly and are unable to keep up with the evolving business decision-making [6].

nodes.label: Static methods refer to traditional, predominantly manual approaches to BA artef
nodes.text: Static methods refer to traditional, predominantly manual approaches to BA artefact generation, in which the resulting outputs are fixed representations that rapidly become outdated and lack adaptability to dynamic and evolving business environments [7].

nodes.label: Agentic AI, often regarded as the fourth wave of artificial intelligence (AI), e
nodes.text: Agentic AI, often regarded as the fourth wave of artificial intelligence (AI), evolves from traditional AI agents [8] by integrating deep learning techniques [9], including LLMs [10].

nodes.label: This fusion enables autonomous decision-making, contextual awareness, and the ab
nodes.text: This fusion enables autonomous decision-making, contextual awareness, and the ability to execute complex, multi-step tasks with minimal human input.

nodes.label: Agentic AI has the potential not only to replicate but also to exceed human capa
nodes.text: Agentic AI has the potential not only to replicate but also to exceed human capabilities in managing complex systems [11].

nodes.label: Such systems are capable of autonomously performing reasoning, planning, and exe
nodes.text: Such systems are capable of autonomously performing reasoning, planning, and executing actions in real time.

nodes.label: There is growing research interest in generating knowledge relevant to BA throug
nodes.text: There is growing research interest in generating knowledge relevant to BA through LLMs by using various prompt-based techniques [12].

nodes.label: However, exploring agentic AI systems in the context of BA remains underexplored
nodes.text: However, exploring agentic AI systems in the context of BA remains underexplored in the current literature and practice [13].

nodes.label: This research is motivated by limitations of current BA methods and focuses on b
nodes.text: This research is motivated by limitations of current BA methods and focuses on business capability modelling (BCM), which is one of the critical parts of BA.

nodes.label: BCM is important to analyse and improve the strategic business and IT alignment
nodes.text: BCM is important to analyse and improve the strategic business and IT alignment [14].

nodes.label: BCM involves identifying and visualising the core and enabling capabilities of a
nodes.text: BCM involves identifying and visualising the core and enabling capabilities of an organisation or business area, such as customer relationship management capability, claims management, human resource management capability, etc [15, 16].

nodes.label: In particular, this paper aims to address the limitations of existing manual or
nodes.text: In particular, this paper aims to address the limitations of existing manual or static BCM by using an agentic AI-enabled approach [17].

nodes.label: This is an ongoing research project aimed at developing a BA Copilot, designed
nodes.text: This is an ongoing research project aimed at developing a BA Copilot, designed in partnership with Capsifi* Pvt.

nodes.label: Ltd, a leading platform in BA modelling and operating model transformation.
nodes.text: Ltd, a leading platform in BA modelling and operating model transformation.

nodes.label: Preliminary work of this project involved conducting a systematic literature sur
nodes.text: Preliminary work of this project involved conducting a systematic literature survey on BA methods [7] and developing and evaluating reasoning-based prompting techniques for generating BA artefacts [18].

nodes.label: Through that, we found that by incorporating reasoning-based prompting technique
nodes.text: Through that, we found that by incorporating reasoning-based prompting techniques, particularly Tree-of-Thought (ToT) [19] results in improved BA artefact generation.

nodes.label: We are currently extending this work by introducing an agent-based capability mo
nodes.text: We are currently extending this work by introducing an agent-based capability modelling process.

nodes.label: It is noteworthy that this project is a work-in-progress where our proposed solu
nodes.text: It is noteworthy that this project is a work-in-progress where our proposed solution is validated and evaluated iteratively.

nodes.label: We present our preliminary findings here, which have been validated by the autho
nodes.text: We present our preliminary findings here, which have been validated by the authors, experts in BA modelling.

nodes.label: Our results and insights are intended to inform both research and practice in en
nodes.text: Our results and insights are intended to inform both research and practice in enterprise modelling.

nodes.label: The key contributions of this research project are as follows: 1.
nodes.text: The key contributions of this research project are as follows: 1.

nodes.label: The design of the Agentic AI-enabled Business Modelling Architecture (ABMA), whi
nodes.text: The design of the Agentic AI-enabled Business Modelling Architecture (ABMA), which integrates a multi-agent system with enterprise knowledge graphs for structured BA artefacts. 2.

nodes.label: The ABMA process that automates the extraction, reasoning, and organisation of B
nodes.text: The ABMA process that automates the extraction, reasoning, and organisation of BA artefacts. 3.

nodes.label: Proof-of-concept is a tool demonstration of Agentic AI-enabled Business Capabili
nodes.text: Proof-of-concept is a tool demonstration of Agentic AI-enabled Business Capability Modelling Architecture (ABCMA), and the modelling process proposed.
