nodes.text: No activity Exploring/piloting Deployment and productionising GenAI use cases with the most benefit Expanding and scaling production GenAI use cases with the most investment Current status Mostly internal use cases Mostly external use cases Internal use cases External use cases Predicted status in three years Growth and revenue will come for companies that can extract the most value from their data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Unstructured data, for instance, has historically been challenging to process, m
nodes.text: Unstructured data, for instance, has historically been challenging to process, manage and use, but it offers immense value.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In a medical setting, for instance, consider the ability to combine diagnostic i
nodes.text: In a medical setting, for instance, consider the ability to combine diagnostic images, text assessments, and numerical values from assessments and clinical readings.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Insurance companies can use data from satellites, GPS or wearables to inform ris
nodes.text: Insurance companies can use data from satellites, GPS or wearables to inform risk modelling and therefore the pricing of anything from heat waves to heart attacks.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Today’s AI models are appealing precisely because of how much information they c
nodes.text: Today’s AI models are appealing precisely because of how much information they can harvest and put to work.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Companies report a flywheel effect: continual insights harnessed from data, usin
nodes.text: Companies report a flywheel effect: continual insights harnessed from data, using AI, in turn allows them to constantly improve products and services.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: For instance, Rivian is integrating its technology stack into EVs made by Volksw
nodes.text: For instance, Rivian is integrating its technology stack into EVs made by Volkswagen (VW), giving Rivian access to a much greater volume of data and deeper insights on usage patterns, says Mr Bensaid, the company’s chief software officer. “We'll be able to bring our technology to a much broader set of vehicles and scale across different VW brands and architectures, given the inbuilt modularity and flexibility of our software architecture,” he adds.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This volume of data will help speed up electrification across millions of vehicl
nodes.text: This volume of data will help speed up electrification across millions of vehicles.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This kind of data-driven approach is driving a trend of personalised products ac
nodes.text: This kind of data-driven approach is driving a trend of personalised products across major industries, tailored to the needs and behaviours of customers. “The future is about really understanding your customer.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The apps, products and commerce offers that they'd like to see are all becoming
nodes.text: The apps, products and commerce offers that they'd like to see are all becoming increasingly personalised,” says Sanjay Bhakta, chief product and technology officer at media company Condé Nast.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The public sector could be a surprise winner, given that it presides over vast r
nodes.text: The public sector could be a surprise winner, given that it presides over vast reserves of hitherto-neglected data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: McKinsey, a consultancy, reckons that more than US$3trn in economic value global
nodes.text: McKinsey, a consultancy, reckons that more than US$3trn in economic value globally could be generated through the enhanced use of open data.15 When Transport for London shared data about service provision, it spurred the creation of over 600 apps in areas like journey and logistics planning, delivering economic savings of up to £95m.16 Meanwhile, the US DoD has been able to tap into its vast proprietary data to process over 10 million payment records, allowing it to identify and take action on US$12.7bn in improper payments since 2020.17
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “The future is about really understanding your customer. The apps, products and
nodes.text: “The future is about really understanding your customer. The apps, products and commerce offers that they'd like to see are all becoming increasingly personalised.”
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Sanjay Bhakta, chief product and technology officer, Condé Nast 15 McKinsey, “Ho
nodes.text: Sanjay Bhakta, chief product and technology officer, Condé Nast 15 McKinsey, “How government can promote open data and help unleash over $3 trillion in economic value”, https://www.mckinsey.com/~/media/mckinsey/industries/public%20and%20social%20sector/our%20insights/how%20 government%20can%20promote%20open%20data/how_govt_can_promote_open_data_and_help_unleash_over_$3_ trillion_in_economic_value.pdf 16 Deloitte, “Assessing the value of TfL’s open data and digital partnerships”, 2017, https://content.tfl.gov.uk/deloitte-report-tfl-open-data.pdf 17 CDAO, “Advana Industry Day”, https://storage.tradewindai.com/pdfs/Advana-Industry-Day.pdf Organisations need to develop frameworks for establishing investment returns and justifying spend.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Our survey found the largest companies rely heavily on tracking key performance
nodes.text: Our survey found the largest companies rely heavily on tracking key performance indicators (KPIs) and conducting post-impact or post-deployment evaluations. Smaller companies also rely on KPIs such as revenue per worker, customer retention and operational costs.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Mr Francis at GM says the company takes a highly disciplined approach, applying
nodes.text: Mr Francis at GM says the company takes a highly disciplined approach, applying AI and ML where it will drive incremental revenue or mitigate costs. “It cannot be technology for the sake of technology; it has to be tied to value and impact for the organisation.” The Detroit-based carmaker collects data on small-scale pilots and proof of concept projects. “Part of the process is finding the opportunities to test these products, to get a quick read before you make a big investment.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: That experimentation cycle needs to be a big part of the journey,” adds Mr Franc
nodes.text: That experimentation cycle needs to be a big part of the journey,” adds Mr Francis.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: But the company remains laser-focused on business outcomes. “Against every dolla
nodes.text: But the company remains laser-focused on business outcomes. “Against every dollar, every penny of compute, we should be able to articulate what the return is.” Mahindra Group is focusing on challenges that go beyond basic productivity improvements.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: As Mr Kapoor at Mahindra states, the organisation is "focused on hard things to
nodes.text: As Mr Kapoor at Mahindra states, the organisation is "focused on hard things to solve" particularly on the factory shop floors, to reduce mean time to repair and mean time between failures or to improve customer experience.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The latter includes innovations like GenAI-powered assistance for faster car ser
nodes.text: The latter includes innovations like GenAI-powered assistance for faster car servicing.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Condé Nast determines ROI across a checklist of outcomes including lowering cost
nodes.text: Condé Nast determines ROI across a checklist of outcomes including lowering costs, increasing speed and efficiency, improving audience experience, or increasing time on site.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Unilever allocates its spending across AI for productivity, creativity and growt
nodes.text: Unilever allocates its spending across AI for productivity, creativity and growth.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Seven Digital has a similar approach. “We are taking a practical approach in usi
nodes.text: Seven Digital has a similar approach. “We are taking a practical approach in using AI to improve the business model—efficiency, outcomes and upskilling, which is organisational transformation,” says the group managing director, Mr Roberts.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Siam Commercial Bank’s former chief digital banking officer, Chalee Asavathirath
nodes.text: Siam Commercial Bank’s former chief digital banking officer, Chalee Asavathiratham, emphasised the need for AI teams to work with finance to measure the outcomes and ROI to avoid getting lured by novelty.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: He warned that AI teams may “neglect to ask what kind of numbers, what kind of R
nodes.text: He warned that AI teams may “neglect to ask what kind of numbers, what kind of ROI” an AI project would generate. “[AI teams] would sometimes get lured [by] the coolness of the projects," he explains.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “Part of the process is finding the opportunities to test these products, to get
nodes.text: “Part of the process is finding the opportunities to test these products, to get a quick read before you make a big investment. That experimentation cycle needs to be a big part of the journey.”
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Jon Francis, chief data and analytics officer, General Motors Frontier’s Ms Pint
nodes.text: Jon Francis, chief data and analytics officer, General Motors Frontier’s Ms Pint makes a similar argument. “There is no such thing as a technology strategy.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: There's only a business strategy that technology supports.
nodes.text: There's only a business strategy that technology supports.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: So whatever you start doing, understand the business strategy very, very well, a
nodes.text: So whatever you start doing, understand the business strategy very, very well, and then tie your technology strategy to it.” At Opendoor, executives determined that productivity should not be the central ROI measure, instead focusing on top-line growth and improved consumer experience.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: While the company’s use of AI yields productivity gains, these are not at the fo
nodes.text: While the company’s use of AI yields productivity gains, these are not at the forefront of their approach. “Our priority is better use of human intuition and improved consistency, which improves customer experience,” says Mr Chakrabarti at Opendoor. “It’s better for employees, customers and the business.” Building a strong data foundation can help companies pursue the higher value goals of revenue and business model innovation. “We got started with AI by building a strong data foundation, then we pivoted to a commercial use case around variable pricing of select add- on products,” recalls Ms Clements at JetBlue. “We first used the model in a very narrow set of markets, and we were immediately struck by the incredible value that it delivered right out of the gate.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This use case was the catalyst for us to understand the power of AI and the ways
nodes.text: This use case was the catalyst for us to understand the power of AI and the ways it could transform our business; it got the creative juices flowing across the organisation to think about leveraging the technology to drive even more value.” Cost control is a valid goal too.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: For companies with significant operational expenditure, even optimising spending
nodes.text: For companies with significant operational expenditure, even optimising spending by a few percent could lead to savings of millions, some of our executive interviewees have observed, and these operational savings can be re-invested in innovation.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “There is no such thing as a technology strategy.
nodes.text: “There is no such thing as a technology strategy.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: There's only a business strategy that technology supports.
nodes.text: There's only a business strategy that technology supports.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: So whatever you start doing, understand the business strategy very, very well, a
nodes.text: So whatever you start doing, understand the business strategy very, very well, and then tie your technology strategy to it.” Melissa Pint, chief digital information officer, Frontier Defining ROI requires identifying objectives and establishing realistic timelines for achieving them, calibrated based on an accurate understanding of AI capabilities, implementation challenges, and organisational readiness.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: When it comes to digital transformation and AI, many executives expect short-ter
nodes.text: When it comes to digital transformation and AI, many executives expect short-term performances and results, explains Helen Choi, chief digital and information officer at CJ CheilJedang, a South Korean consumer goods firm. “But it’s very hard to institutionalise AI technology and data analysis with employees.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: You cannot just measure over the short-term; you need to think over a three- yea
nodes.text: You cannot just measure over the short-term; you need to think over a three- year timeline.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This is because it is not just a matter of data or AI technology implementation,
nodes.text: This is because it is not just a matter of data or AI technology implementation,” says Ms Choi. “Achieving real business value, led by digital technology, involves a long journey of innovation across ways of working and organisational change management.” “A significant challenge lies in the fact that the monetisation and business models for value creation through GenAI are still not well established,” says Takaaki Sato, the senior executive vice president and chief technology officer at NTT Docomo, a Japanese telecoms company. “There is uncertainty about whether to invest in general-purpose LLMs or specialised smaller LLMs, [and] unclear ROI at this stage.”
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “It’s very hard to institutionalise AI technology and data analysis with employe
nodes.text: “It’s very hard to institutionalise AI technology and data analysis with employees. You cannot just measure over the short-term; you need to think over a three-year timeline.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Helen Choi, chief digital and information officer, CJ CheilJedang
nodes.text: Helen Choi, chief digital and information officer, CJ CheilJedang
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency gains are early, measurable wins, but true leaders w
nodes.text: Productivity and efficiency gains are early, measurable wins, but true leaders will
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: seek more ambitious returns. Revenue, talent attraction, and novel products and
nodes.text: seek more ambitious returns. Revenue, talent attraction, and novel products and services are among the loftier targets. Some firms can also unlock value by using AI to extract and apply their proprietary data in new ways, including developing more personalised products.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Organisations must strike a fine balance between business returns and the
nodes.text: Organisations must strike a fine balance between business returns and the
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: time to achieve true value.
nodes.text: time to achieve true value.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Companies will soon need to demonstrate financial returns from their AI investme
nodes.text: Companies will soon need to demonstrate financial returns from their AI investments, but their timelines must strike the right balance.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Experimentation takes time, and some projects will inevitably fail to deliver.
nodes.text: Experimentation takes time, and some projects will inevitably fail to deliver.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Firms need an initial runway to upgrade their data infrastructure.
nodes.text: Firms need an initial runway to upgrade their data infrastructure.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Regulatory constraints also limit the speed of deployment in sectors like health
nodes.text: Regulatory constraints also limit the speed of deployment in sectors like healthcare.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: There is also merit in watchful waiting as the AI vendor and product landscape e
nodes.text: There is also merit in watchful waiting as the AI vendor and product landscape evolves.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: While a laser-focus on returns can mitigate excessive spending, companies might
nodes.text: While a laser-focus on returns can mitigate excessive spending, companies might take one step back to take two steps forward.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: For example, some of the algorithms currently used by Dream Sports, an Indian fa
nodes.text: For example, some of the algorithms currently used by Dream Sports, an Indian fantasy sports company, produced negative business metrics initially. “Obviously, the product teams and the finance teams got a little nervous about that. [It’s about] being able to persevere and persist and educate them,” recalls Amit Sharma, the company’s chief technology officer.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In regulated sectors like pharmaceuticals and biotech, meanwhile, achieving quic
nodes.text: In regulated sectors like pharmaceuticals and biotech, meanwhile, achieving quick wins can be challenging, as it takes years to bring a pharmaceutical innovation from the lab to the patient. “AI accelerates the process of identifying synthesisable molecules and advancing them to phase two or three trials,” notes Mr Bucher at Novartis. “However, it may take five to ten years to determine AI’s effectiveness, once the product’s safety is confirmed.” The rapid rate of AI progress and innovation might also merit strategic patience, with some experts likening it to waiting for Google to emerge from the many search engines in the 1990s (it appeared six years after Archie, the debut product).18 Jeff Martin, senior vice president and chief data officer at TD, cautions against “being too confident [about] where the future is going”.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: He adds that companies should assume they will need to pivot on every decision t
nodes.text: He adds that companies should assume they will need to pivot on every decision they’re making, “so be sure to be ready, flexible and agile”.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “AI accelerates the process of identifying synthesisable molecules and advancing
nodes.text: “AI accelerates the process of identifying synthesisable molecules and advancing them to phase two or three trials.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: However, it may take five to ten years to determine AI’s effectiveness, once the
nodes.text: However, it may take five to ten years to determine AI’s effectiveness, once the product’s safety is confirmed.” Bernd Bucher, chief information officer, Novartis 18 SEO Mechanic, “The Complete History of Search Engines”, 2023, https://www.seomechanic.com/complete-history-search-engines/ As organisations harness AI's potential, they face a critical challenge: their data infrastructure is woefully unprepared, akin to having Victorian-era plumbing in a modern building.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Silos, latency and security are all inhibitors for AI deployment.
nodes.text: Silos, latency and security are all inhibitors for AI deployment.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: From accessing valuable but unstructured data to navigating the complexities of
nodes.text: From accessing valuable but unstructured data to navigating the complexities of cloud solutions, organisations need to reimagine their data architecture.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: One of the biggest shifts in the AI era is not the power of the models themselve
nodes.text: One of the biggest shifts in the AI era is not the power of the models themselves, but the underlying plumbing needed to deliver them at scale.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Many of the world’s largest companies are sitting on the technological equivalen
nodes.text: Many of the world’s largest companies are sitting on the technological equivalent of Victorian-era pipes that cannot handle the demands of AI, with legacy systems that are creaking at the seams.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Only 22% of organisations in our survey say their current architecture is fully
nodes.text: Only 22% of organisations in our survey say their current architecture is fully capable of supporting the unique demands of AI workloads, and just 23% say their current architecture fully integrates AI applications to relevant business data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Even among the largest companies we surveyed, these rates only rise to 28% and 2
nodes.text: Even among the largest companies we surveyed, these rates only rise to 28% and 27%, respectively.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: These deficits hinder progress and likely contribute to quality concerns, with o
nodes.text: These deficits hinder progress and likely contribute to quality concerns, with only 37% of executives and even fewer practitioners (29%) believing that GenAI applications at their organisations are production ready.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The demands of AI and advanced analytics workloads were already creating infrast
nodes.text: The demands of AI and advanced analytics workloads were already creating infrastructure headaches before GenAI, largely because of the different types of data generated by companies and the variety of storage mechanisms developed to handle them.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Many companies used cloud-based warehouses to store highly structured data that
nodes.text: Many companies used cloud-based warehouses to store highly structured data that have clear classifications and labels, managed by expert IT teams and easily queried.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: However, as much as 90% of data collected by organisations is unstructured and,
nodes.text: However, as much as 90% of data collected by organisations is unstructured and, therefore, unfit for warehousing.19 As ML and AI advanced, unstructured data came to be seen as ever more valuable because of ML systems’ ability to spot patterns in vast troves of this previously neglected data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Only 22% of organisations in our survey say their current architecture is fully
nodes.text: Only 22% of organisations in our survey say their current architecture is fully capable of supporting the unique demands of AI workloads.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: 19 MIT Sloan, “Tapping the power of unstructured data”, 2021, https://mitsloan.m
nodes.text: 19 MIT Sloan, “Tapping the power of unstructured data”, 2021, https://mitsloan.mit.edu/ideas-made-to-matter/ tapping-power-unstructured-data Data lakes, capable of storing vast quantities of both structured and unstructured data, emerged as an alternative, allowing organisations to leverage unstructured data, but these led to fragmentation between lakes and warehouses.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In effect, this led to silos, patchy support for use cases, and incompatible sec
nodes.text: In effect, this led to silos, patchy support for use cases, and incompatible security models, all of which made it harder for companies to utilise their data effectively.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Then, over time, a new model called the data lakehouse emerged, aimed at address
nodes.text: Then, over time, a new model called the data lakehouse emerged, aimed at addressing these problems.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This model combines aspects of both lakes and warehouses and is built on open-so
nodes.text: This model combines aspects of both lakes and warehouses and is built on open-source software, using open standards.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Looking ahead, speed is the primary architecture constraint that organisations p
nodes.text: Looking ahead, speed is the primary architecture constraint that organisations plan to address, with real-time data processing voted the top capacity gap by 47% of respondents, followed by robust data pipelines and security (see figure 7).
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Architects’ views were also corroborated by data engineers, who were the most li
nodes.text: Architects’ views were also corroborated by data engineers, who were the most likely to rank streaming data issues as their top challenge in maintaining data quality.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Real-time streaming of live production data is crucial for AI applications that
nodes.text: Real-time streaming of live production data is crucial for AI applications that require immediate querying.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Mahindra Group, for instance, incorporates real-time data on solar panel perform
nodes.text: Mahindra Group, for instance, incorporates real-time data on solar panel performance into an algorithm that directs maintenance and cleaning crews around their renewable energy installations for effective cleaning and maintenance schedules.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Similarly Dream Sports’ use of AI to decide when to generate lucrative contests
nodes.text: Similarly Dream Sports’ use of AI to decide when to generate lucrative contests around sports events depends on knowing exactly how many users are building a fantasy team for a specific event at any moment.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Finding the right infrastructure can become more difficult as companies get bigg
nodes.text: Finding the right infrastructure can become more difficult as companies get bigger.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Diversified companies with individual business units, and conglomerates comprisi
nodes.text: Diversified companies with individual business units, and conglomerates comprising entirely independent companies, must determine where the data go.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: For instance, having data centralised in company-wide stores allows insights to
nodes.text: For instance, having data centralised in company-wide stores allows insights to be drawn across the group, while opting for individual units to manage the data gives more flexibility to analytics and data science teams to work with it in ways that suit their own needs.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Figure 7: Architecture limitations
nodes.text: Figure 7: Architecture limitations
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The most significant gaps in respondents' current architecture that need to be a
nodes.text: The most significant gaps in respondents' current architecture that need to be addressed in the next 3-5 years Real-time data processing Robust data pipelines Integration with downstream systems Ethical Al/safety considerations/ bias detection Efficient model hosting Efficient model training Scalable compute At Mahindra Group, core functions like cloud and cybersecurity are managed in common, but individual architecture decisions are left to business units.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The firm takes a ‘problem first, solution later’ approach, emphasising collabora
nodes.text: The firm takes a ‘problem first, solution later’ approach, emphasising collaboration between business and technology teams.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Its car company uses a data mesh to store driver relevant information such as jo
nodes.text: Its car company uses a data mesh to store driver relevant information such as journey history separately from manufacturing data, while allowing the two datasets to be combined if needed.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This reduces latency for driver queries, because only relevant information is se
nodes.text: This reduces latency for driver queries, because only relevant information is searched, but allows for supply chain problems to be identified—for example, if several drivers report a common problem the company can trace the manufacturing history of the relevant component in each car.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Mahindra’s finance team, meanwhile, uses a more flexible architecture so that it
nodes.text: Mahindra’s finance team, meanwhile, uses a more flexible architecture so that it can consider personal loans, fixed deposits, insurance, mortgages and loans for small and medium-sized enterprises simultaneously in order to assess risk.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: It would be wrong to assume that start-ups or digital native companies are free
nodes.text: It would be wrong to assume that start-ups or digital native companies are free from infrastructure challenges.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Dream Sports, for example, was still querying production databases for its repor
nodes.text: Dream Sports, for example, was still querying production databases for its reporting needs several years after its founding and only set up a data store once the company had found product-market fit.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: However, such companies show a remarkable willingness to reengineer their data a
nodes.text: However, such companies show a remarkable willingness to reengineer their data architecture from scratch when needed, perhaps because they lack the substantial technical debt.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Rivian, an EV manufacturer, has integrated information from across its complex s
nodes.text: Rivian, an EV manufacturer, has integrated information from across its complex systems, including supply chain, commercial operations and financial departments, into a unified environment, enabling data-driven decision- making at all levels of the organisation.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This benefits not just AI and GenAI applications, but also the entire gamut of d
nodes.text: This benefits not just AI and GenAI applications, but also the entire gamut of data-linked business processes from dashboards and analytics to customer profiles and history.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: GenAI can pose an infrastructure pressure for some because of the demands of new
nodes.text: GenAI can pose an infrastructure pressure for some because of the demands of new use cases, with their corresponding unique tooling requirements.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: One of these is Retrieval Augmented Generation, or RAG, the process through whic
nodes.text: One of these is Retrieval Augmented Generation, or RAG, the process through which an LLM checks the answer it has generated against an external source, like an encyclopaedia, and then refines it.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: When building their own LLMs, organisations supply internal data, such as custom
nodes.text: When building their own LLMs, organisations supply internal data, such as customer support interactions, as the authoritative external source of information.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: But before this can happen the internal data must be cleaned and prepared for ML
nodes.text: But before this can happen the internal data must be cleaned and prepared for ML, which requires specific tools and technologies.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In their rush to embrace GenAI, many companies are building and maintaining dupl
nodes.text: In their rush to embrace GenAI, many companies are building and maintaining duplicates of data or systems to clean and prepare data, for example, by adding an additional data pipeline to the company’s infrastructure. This adds further complexity to already complicated data infrastructure.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “You're kind of building in a vacuum. And every application is building its own
nodes.text: “You're kind of building in a vacuum. And every application is building its own set of infrastructure and tooling around large language model operations or machine learning operations,” says Ms Vaezy at Providence.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Indeed, in a world of GenAI, data infrastructure could become congested, overlap
nodes.text: Indeed, in a world of GenAI, data infrastructure could become congested, overlapping and harder to keep track of.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: When asked what would yield the biggest benefits for their productivity, data en
nodes.text: When asked what would yield the biggest benefits for their productivity, data engineers identified ‘simplifying data source connections for ingesting data’, ‘using a single unified solution instead of multiple tools’ and ‘better visibility into data pipelines to find and fix issues’ among the top interventions.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Implementing traditional AI use-cases prematurely, without a solid data foundati
nodes.text: Implementing traditional AI use-cases prematurely, without a solid data foundation, could expose companies to operational, security and reputational risks. “Back in March of 2023, when all the excitement [around GenAI] was taking place, I knew that we were going to run into a problem that everybody was going to be excitedly jumping into things and potentially exposing the company in ways we shouldn't be exposed,” says Mr Hallworth at HP.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Many organisations are increasing their reliance on cloud services to support AI
nodes.text: Many organisations are increasing their reliance on cloud services to support AI workloads, as reported by 61% of enterprise architects in our survey, with a trend towards multi-cloud solutions among 37%, based on factors including control, security, data ownership, compliance and cost.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Companies in our survey often report moving some workloads from public to privat
nodes.text: Companies in our survey often report moving some workloads from public to private cloud environments since first investing in AI, where resources like server hardware are dedicated to a specific company rather than serving many clients at once.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The control, security, data ownership and compliance aspects of private cloud in
nodes.text: The control, security, data ownership and compliance aspects of private cloud infrastructure can make it an attractive option for integrating GenAI applications.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Compliance needs and the sensitive data required to train and run models often d
nodes.text: Compliance needs and the sensitive data required to train and run models often demand tailored infrastructure.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: On the other hand, the scalability and flexibility offered by public cloud syste
nodes.text: On the other hand, the scalability and flexibility offered by public cloud systems at low upfront costs are valuable.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Shorter development cycles, integrated technical frameworks, experimentation at
nodes.text: Shorter development cycles, integrated technical frameworks, experimentation at scale and the ability to rapidly scale can make public cloud systems a tool of choice.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: But there is a balance between cost-efficiency and control, security and flexibi
nodes.text: But there is a balance between cost-efficiency and control, security and flexibility that remains an open question.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Beyond the question moving data into storage comes the knotty issue of its accur
nodes.text: Beyond the question moving data into storage comes the knotty issue of its accuracy and security, along with the appropriate access controls.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Incorrect or conflicting data, after all, could lead a GenAI model to offer inco
nodes.text: Incorrect or conflicting data, after all, could lead a GenAI model to offer incorrect information, and weak controls could lead to compliance breaches.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: As Ms Clements at JetBlue said: “You can have all the AI in the world, but if it
nodes.text: As Ms Clements at JetBlue said: “You can have all the AI in the world, but if it's on a shaky data foundation, then it's not going to bring you any value.” “Ultimately, all of this runs on data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: If your data isn’t properly categorised, it's going to be very difficult to bene
nodes.text: If your data isn’t properly categorised, it's going to be very difficult to benefit from any of this technology, so you have to focus on the fundamentals of good data engineering and data management,” argues Mr Vohs at Molson Coors.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: When it comes to these fundamentals, enterprise architects identified security,
nodes.text: When it comes to these fundamentals, enterprise architects identified security, data privacy and data silos as the three biggest challenges faced when managing data for AI applications (see figure 8).
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “You can have all the AI in the world, but
nodes.text: “You can have all the AI in the world, but
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: if it's on a shaky data foundation, then it's not going to bring you any value.”
nodes.text: if it's on a shaky data foundation, then it's not going to bring you any value.”
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Carol Clements, chief digital and technology officer, JetBlue
nodes.text: Carol Clements, chief digital and technology officer, JetBlue
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Figure 8: The challenges of data gatekeeping
nodes.text: Figure 8: The challenges of data gatekeeping
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Enterprise architects' biggest challenges in managing and controlling data for A
nodes.text: Enterprise architects' biggest challenges in managing and controlling data for Al applications Controlling who can access data Protecting privacy and sensitive information Dealing with data silos and a lack of integration Keeping track of where data comes from and how data are used Lack of dedicated resources or personnel for data management Meeting regulatory compliance requirements Ensuring data are high quality, diverse and representative Ensuring consistent data management across different systems or cloud platforms Handling large volumes of data efficiently One challenge introduced by GenAI, in particular, is how much information to put into the cloud for analysis.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Some are reluctant to push more data into the cloud without a clear use case in
nodes.text: Some are reluctant to push more data into the cloud without a clear use case in mind.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This reluctance is pronounced in sectors like energy and banking, which have alr
nodes.text: This reluctance is pronounced in sectors like energy and banking, which have already accrued substantial amounts of technical debt and data silos through legacy systems and mergers and acquisitions, for example.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Both Spanish energy company Repsol and Canadian bank TD also emphasise the poten
nodes.text: Both Spanish energy company Repsol and Canadian bank TD also emphasise the potential expense of excessive data upload, both directly in cloud- related costs, and indirectly in terms of requiring staff and teams to prepare data and build and maintain pipelines to push data into storage.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: While companies in the past tended to ignore unstructured data—despite it making
nodes.text: While companies in the past tended to ignore unstructured data—despite it making up the vast majority of what companies hold—it has taken on increasing importance as AI tools make it easier to access.20 UPS uses a data mesh to classify data and analytics tools that allow both data scientists and business teams to engage in machine learning operations (MLOps). “It is powerful because it can combine different ML models in a quick time frame.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: If tomorrow we wanted to infuse a GenAI model into it, we would be able to do th
nodes.text: If tomorrow we wanted to infuse a GenAI model into it, we would be able to do that because we've got all the platforms and orchestration in place to do it,” says Mr Finnerty at UPS.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Figure 9: GenAI quality: leaders and laggards
nodes.text: Figure 9: GenAI quality: leaders and laggards
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Strategies employed for evaluating the quality of GenAI responses Automated eval
nodes.text: Strategies employed for evaluating the quality of GenAI responses Automated evaluation collection (using Al-assisted judges)
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The application collects user feedback through a user interface or in-app feedba
nodes.text: The application collects user feedback through a user interface or in-app feedback form Feedback through customer support One-on-one user interviews Surveys and questionnaires Feature request platforms Analytics and usage data User testing sessions Focus groups A/B testing to compare different versions of Al systems Laggards = those whose GenAI tools are not Leaders = those whose GenAI tools are production ready 20 MIT Sloan, “Tapping the power of unstructured data”, 2021, https://mitsloan.mit.edu/ideas-made-to-matter/tapping-power-unstructured-data GenAI stands out among other uses of data in the technology arsenal for how much upkeep and maintenance it requires.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: LLMs, for example, suffer from so-called model drift over time, as the informati
nodes.text: LLMs, for example, suffer from so-called model drift over time, as the information they were trained on becomes dated and their performance deteriorates.21 Indeed, Mr Hallworth from HP likens AI models to cars that start to depreciate the moment they are driven off a parking lot.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Upkeep comes in at least three forms.
nodes.text: Upkeep comes in at least three forms.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: First, datasets must be constantly validated and checked for completeness, recen
nodes.text: First, datasets must be constantly validated and checked for completeness, recency and validity.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: These checks can be performed by open-source tools and application programming i
nodes.text: These checks can be performed by open-source tools and application programming interfaces (APIs) integrated into the existing data sets and clouds.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Quality assurance can also be applied across data pipelines to prevent faulty da
nodes.text: Quality assurance can also be applied across data pipelines to prevent faulty data from entering the organisation’s systems in the first place.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Very large firms (revenue over US$10bn) are more likely than others to use ML to
nodes.text: Very large firms (revenue over US$10bn) are more likely than others to use ML to detect data anomalies, our survey found.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: And when it comes to data upkeep for GenAI models specifically, ‘leaders’—compan
nodes.text: And when it comes to data upkeep for GenAI models specifically, ‘leaders’—companies with production ready GenAI tools—are also more likely than others to use AI to assess the data (see figure 9).
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Second, for GenAI systems, the encyclopaedia- like external data sources used by
nodes.text: Second, for GenAI systems, the encyclopaedia- like external data sources used by LLMs to refine their answers must be regularly updated to maintain the quality of information they contain.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Finally, the models themselves must be constantly tested for efficacy. One examp
nodes.text: Finally, the models themselves must be constantly tested for efficacy. One example highlighted in figure 9 is automated evaluation collection, whereby ML algorithms are used to quantify the accuracy of outputs generated by an LLM in comparison to expected answers.22
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Only a fifth of organisations have an architecture fully capable of supporting A
nodes.text: Only a fifth of organisations have an architecture fully capable of supporting AI
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.text: workloads.

nodes.label: This is a major constraint to operationalising AI.
nodes.text: This is a major constraint to operationalising AI.

nodes.label: Real-time data processing is one high-value capability sought by companies, whic
nodes.text: Real-time data processing is one high-value capability sought by companies, which can only be delivered with an infrastructure refit.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Unified infrastructure helps AI deliver on its potential not just as a productiv
nodes.text: Unified infrastructure helps AI deliver on its potential not just as a productivity tool, but also as an input for better decision-making at the speed businesses need.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Size or digital savvy is no predictor of success in infrastructure
nodes.text: Size or digital savvy is no predictor of success in infrastructure
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: transformation. Large companies have more complex decisions to make around centr
nodes.text: transformation. Large companies have more complex decisions to make around centralisation and devolution, and digital native firms may have more complex tooling environments due to early adoption of best-of-breed software.

nodes.label: Datasets must be constantly validated and checked for completeness, recency and
nodes.text: Datasets must be constantly validated and checked for completeness, recency and validity, performed by open-source tools and APIs integrated into the existing data sets and clouds.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Quality assurance should be applied across data pipelines to prevent faulty data
nodes.text: Quality assurance should be applied across data pipelines to prevent faulty data from entering the organisation’s systems in the first place.

nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Enterprise architects: walking the tightrope
nodes.text: Enterprise architects: walking the tightrope
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The role of a data and AI architect is complex, challenging and crucial in today
nodes.text: The role of a data and AI architect is complex, challenging and crucial in today's technology-driven organisations.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Gregor Hohpe, who has held leadership positions at tech giants like Google and A
nodes.text: Gregor Hohpe, who has held leadership positions at tech giants like Google and Amazon Web Services, defined a software architect as "a technical leader who thrives on complexity but insists on simplicity".
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Architects are charged with the design, implementation, and oversight of a scala
nodes.text: Architects are charged with the design, implementation, and oversight of a scalable and secure data and AI architecture, requiring a deep understanding of current and emerging technologies.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Our survey found that just 22% of organisations have architectures that fully su
nodes.text: Our survey found that just 22% of organisations have architectures that fully support AI workloads without modifications, and only 23% can connect AI applications to relevant business data without changes.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Architects are the ones now tasked with bridging this gap and helping organisati
nodes.text: Architects are the ones now tasked with bridging this gap and helping organisations achieve comprehensive AI integration.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Key capability gaps include real-time data processing, identified by nearly half
nodes.text: Key capability gaps include real-time data processing, identified by nearly half (47%)
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: of architects as a significant need.
nodes.text: of architects as a significant need.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Other concerns include data privacy and security breaches.
nodes.text: Other concerns include data privacy and security breaches.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: To strengthen governance, architects are focusing on implementing custom APIs (5
nodes.text: To strengthen governance, architects are focusing on implementing custom APIs (58%), human-in-the-loop processes (55%) and integrating ML models into familiar data tools (52%).
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This approach reflects the need for both technical solutions and human oversight
nodes.text: This approach reflects the need for both technical solutions and human oversight in AI implementation, ensuring that AI capabilities are seamlessly woven into the fabric of organisational operations.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Technologies that enhance accessibility and efficiency across different platform
nodes.text: Technologies that enhance accessibility and efficiency across different platforms are becoming increasingly important.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Natural language processing (NLP) is at the forefront of this trend, with 58% of
nodes.text: Natural language processing (NLP) is at the forefront of this trend, with 58% of architects believing it will become the primary (or only) tool in data pipeline creation.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Sixty-seven percent say the same about its impact on workflow development, as do
nodes.text: Sixty-seven percent say the same about its impact on workflow development, as do 52% about its role in report generation.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This indicates a shift towards more intuitive, user- friendly tools in data and
nodes.text: This indicates a shift towards more intuitive, user- friendly tools in data and AI operations.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In the current economic climate, with mounting pressure to reduce the total cost
nodes.text: In the current economic climate, with mounting pressure to reduce the total cost of ownership, these cross-platform capabilities are becoming more critical.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The emphasis on accessibility and efficiency not only reduces costs but also pro
nodes.text: The emphasis on accessibility and efficiency not only reduces costs but also promotes the democratisation of data and AI, enabling a broader range of users to access and leverage these powerful tools and insights across the organisation.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The challenges faced by data and AI architects are numerous, but so are the oppo
nodes.text: The challenges faced by data and AI architects are numerous, but so are the opportunities. As they seek to develop strategies for implementing ethical AI, managing data ecosystems and democratising data access, architects will face a delicate balancing act of innovation, security and accessibility.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Data engineers: bringing order to the data storm
nodes.text: Data engineers: bringing order to the data storm
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Data engineering is under immense pressure to meet the evolving demands of moder
nodes.text: Data engineering is under immense pressure to meet the evolving demands of modern enterprises, with two dominant challenges— fostering high-data quality for end users and ensuring security and governance.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: These objectives come on top of data engineers’ responsibility for managing most
nodes.text: These objectives come on top of data engineers’ responsibility for managing most aspects of the data engineering lifecycle.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The need for unification, simplification and democratisation across the data eng
nodes.text: The need for unification, simplification and democratisation across the data engineering discipline is at a fever pitch.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Today, nearly two- thirds of organisations are fully dependent on data engineers
nodes.text: Today, nearly two- thirds of organisations are fully dependent on data engineers for every aspect of data pipeline creation and management, and almost half of data engineers spend most of their time configuring and fixing data source connections.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Data engineers face significant burnout and fatigue given the number of data sou
nodes.text: Data engineers face significant burnout and fatigue given the number of data sources and tools required to configure usable data pipelines.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The emergence of NLP as a tool for democratising data pipeline and workflow crea
nodes.text: The emergence of NLP as a tool for democratising data pipeline and workflow creation is a promising trend, with more than 97% of enterprise architects predicting that NLP will either supplement or replace traditional methods for pipeline creation and workflow orchestration.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: This shift means, in the future, that data engineers will increasingly enjoy sup
nodes.text: This shift means, in the future, that data engineers will increasingly enjoy support from intelligent automation to handle the complexity of real-time data processing and pipeline management, allowing them to focus on more strategic tasks.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: At the top of the wish list for most data and AI teams are simplified data sourc
nodes.text: At the top of the wish list for most data and AI teams are simplified data source connections, integrated GenAI tools for coding assistance, and unified platforms to reduce the reliance on disparate systems.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Architects are placing significant bets on real-time data processing and robust
nodes.text: Architects are placing significant bets on real-time data processing and robust data pipelines, with nearly 75% identifying one of these as a critical goal for their organisations’ data and AI capabilities.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: As streaming architectures become more prevalent, with 46% of companies migratin
nodes.text: As streaming architectures become more prevalent, with 46% of companies migrating a significant number of batch pipelines to real-time streams in the last year alone, the emphasis on latency and real-time insights is intensifying.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In sum, data engineering is at a critical juncture. The field is moving towards
nodes.text: In sum, data engineering is at a critical juncture. The field is moving towards greater efficiency through automation and real-time data processing, but it remains anchored by the foundational challenges of security, governance and data quality.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: As AI becomes a baseline for business, companies will need to find their unique
nodes.text: As AI becomes a baseline for business, companies will need to find their unique path by integrating foundation models with proprietary data and domain expertise, balancing off-the-shelf convenience with the power of tailored solutions, all while safeguarding their most valuable asset: their data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: As foundation models become commoditised, usage will be a baseline, not a boost.
nodes.text: As foundation models become commoditised, usage will be a baseline, not a boost.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: An oft-repeated warning in the contemporary discourse is that AI will not replac
nodes.text: An oft-repeated warning in the contemporary discourse is that AI will not replace humans, but humans using AI will replace those that don’t.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: The same goes for companies.
nodes.text: The same goes for companies.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Each must find their edge—their unique recipe of data, need and know-how. “You n
nodes.text: Each must find their edge—their unique recipe of data, need and know-how. “You need to build on top of your existing value.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: In our case it is our medical knowledge, user experience, connection to wearable
nodes.text: In our case it is our medical knowledge, user experience, connection to wearables, and using GenAI to produce helpful insights or propose answers to questions,” says Mr Bugaev at Flo Health.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Our survey found that two-thirds of organisations see significant potential in i
nodes.text: Our survey found that two-thirds of organisations see significant potential in integrating GenAI models with their own proprietary data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Most will mix and match based on workflows and needs.
nodes.text: Most will mix and match based on workflows and needs.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: For instance, 58% of data scientists augment their LLMs with proprietary data th
nodes.text: For instance, 58% of data scientists augment their LLMs with proprietary data through a process known as RAG—the use of a knowledgeable external source by an LLM when inferring or producing an output—while 45% say they use LLMs off the shelf without connecting to their data (see figure 10).
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Meanwhile, 21% do both.
nodes.text: Meanwhile, 21% do both.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Mr Hallworth at HP draws a pizza analogy to describe the difference.
nodes.text: Mr Hallworth at HP draws a pizza analogy to describe the difference.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Off-the-shelf AI models may be convenient and time-saving, but a home-cooked one
nodes.text: Off-the-shelf AI models may be convenient and time-saving, but a home-cooked one that takes longer to make tastes better.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: A ‘supermarket pizza’ AI equivalent might write first draft marketing copy or me
nodes.text: A ‘supermarket pizza’ AI equivalent might write first draft marketing copy or meeting summaries, while advanced tasks like customer-facing interactions, product insights or forecasting need to be cooked at home.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: “Most of the value that you see on AI is buried in the bottom,” explains Accentu
nodes.text: “Most of the value that you see on AI is buried in the bottom,” explains Accenture’s Mr Ramani. “The top is immediate benefits like productivity, but that’s the starting point.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: You’ve not gone into your core value chain, that's when you go beneath the icebe
nodes.text: You’ve not gone into your core value chain, that's when you go beneath the iceberg.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: That is where your proprietary data is.” Foundation models are the coffee grinde
nodes.text: That is where your proprietary data is.” Foundation models are the coffee grinder; the beans are the company’s data.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Using that data as part of a ‘compound’23 system helps organisations brew AI res
nodes.text: Using that data as part of a ‘compound’23 system helps organisations brew AI results that consider their unique domain or situation.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Blending multiple models to meet varied use cases is a common trend, our survey
nodes.text: Blending multiple models to meet varied use cases is a common trend, our survey shows (see figure 12), as is integrating proprietary data into GenAI models, which most respondents are either doing or exploring.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Respondents from the financial services industry were by far the most likely to
nodes.text: Respondents from the financial services industry were by far the most likely to recognise significant potential in integrating GenAI models with proprietary data, with 80% agreeing (see figure 11).
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.text: Across sectors, perspectives on this differ substantially, reflecting diverse views on the value of organisational data, the complexity of processing it, the risks of using it in GenAI models and the cultural or operational readiness to implement such technologies.

nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Open-source AI models, for instance, allow anyone to ‘study, modify, use and sha
nodes.text: Open-source AI models, for instance, allow anyone to ‘study, modify, use and share’ them.24 Although there are debates over the definition of open source for AI specifically, such as whether it should include the data used to create models, the presence of open- source tools has widened access to AI.25 Meta’s Llama, for instance, has been downloaded 300 million times, allowing organisations to build AI models suited to their purposes.26,27 A hybrid approach of utilising both open- and closed-source AI is a common strategy, our survey found, with 75% of organisations employing open- and closed-source models based on industry-specific factors, and 89% will do so by 2027.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Closed-source models are typically deployed for internal applications, while ope
nodes.text: Closed-source models are typically deployed for internal applications, while open-source models are increasingly used by those expanding into external use-cases.

nodes.label: Organisations cite several reasons for open- source adoption.
nodes.text: Organisations cite several reasons for open- source adoption.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Greater development transparency tops the list at 52%, followed by access to ope
nodes.text: Greater development transparency tops the list at 52%, followed by access to open-source community resources, the opportunity for proprietary intellectual property (IP) development, mitigation of vendor lock-in, and greater customisation, privacy and security control.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.text: 24 Open Source Initiative, “The Open Source AI Definition – draft v. 0.0.8”, https://opensource.org/deepdive/drafts/the-open-source-ai-definition-draft-v-0-0-8 25 Economist Impact, “Open sourcing the AI revolution”, 2024, https://impact.economist.com/perspectives/technology-innovation/open-sourcing-ai-revolution 26 The Economist, “Meta is accused of “bullying” the open-source community”, 2024, https://www.economist.com/business/2024/08/28/meta-is-accused-of-bullying-the-open-source-community 27 Microsoft, “Tiny but mighty: The Phi-3 small language models with big potential”, 2024, https://news.microsoft.com/source/features/ai/the-phi-3-small-language-models-with-big-potential/ But closed-source models offer some advantages.

nodes.text: Some also find that open-source models require more in-house expertise and skills to adapt and develop compared with off-the-shelf closed-source models.28 Industry leaders are developing frameworks to inform their model decisions.

nodes.label: Accenture helps organisations through what it calls its switchboard, a proprieta
nodes.text: Accenture helps organisations through what it calls its switchboard, a proprietary tool set and framework, enabling organisations to decide which models to use based on the business use case, relevance, cost, accuracy and latency.

nodes.text: Relatively few companies see merit in developing their own LLM from scratch. “We don’t believe a company of our size can promptly build something truly meaningful in terms of home-grown LLMs,” says Mr Bugaev at Flo Health.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Equally, companies will not be able to find a measurable advantage relying solel
nodes.text: Equally, companies will not be able to find a measurable advantage relying solely on off-the-shelf models.

nodes.text: “We believe that partnerships between tech companies and pharma/healthcare are essential,” says Mr Bucher at Novartis. “Tech companies bring strong platforms, data capabilities and AI talent, which are invaluable in creating usable models.
nodes.author: Economist Impact

nodes.label: Pharma companies possess deep expertise in safety, clinical trials and productio
nodes.text: Pharma companies possess deep expertise in safety, clinical trials and production, which are critical for developing potential drugs.

nodes.text: AI serves as an accelerator, enhancing our combined efforts.

nodes.label: By leveraging our distinct strengths, we can achieve greater advancements togeth
nodes.text: By leveraging our distinct strengths, we can achieve greater advancements together.”
