nodes.text: The Enterprise Orchestration Layer is the functional abstraction for coordinating, governing, and optimizing complex, multi-step workflows and business processes that span AI agents, humans, automation tools, and deterministic systems.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This layer leverages a blended orchestration model that allows individual agents
nodes.text: This layer leverages a blended orchestration model that allows individual agents and systems to autonomously handle locally choreographed tasks using open protocols such as MCP and A2A while providing centralized end-to-end oversight and coordination of the entire process.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: To implement the blended orchestration model, this layer represents critical bus
nodes.text: To implement the blended orchestration model, this layer represents critical business processes in machine-legible semantically rich formats that define both the deterministic steps (modeled during design time) and the dynamic steps (decided by agents during run-time) of a business process, creating the process model foundation for governance and optimization.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Traditionally, significant portions of human-driven business processes remain un
nodes.text: Traditionally, significant portions of human-driven business processes remain undocumented or inaccessible in machine-readable forms.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: However, the detailed observability of AI agents’ activities including rich data
nodes.text: However, the detailed observability of AI agents’ activities including rich data and metadata about their tasks and actions enables capturing, documenting, and integrating dynamic, previously unstructured work with deterministic linear workflows to create comprehensive process models.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The detailed process documentation captures previously invisible task interdepen
nodes.text: The detailed process documentation captures previously invisible task interdependencies and execution paths, enabling the enterprise to continuously optimize operational efficiency, effectively address bottlenecks, and systematically codify agent-identified best practices into reusable enterprise-wide playbooks.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This results in a holistic digital twin of individual processes, and when scaled
nodes.text: This results in a holistic digital twin of individual processes, and when scaled, the entire enterprise.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: For example, complex processes such as executing comprehensive sales strategies,
nodes.text: For example, complex processes such as executing comprehensive sales strategies, or onboarding new employees involve numerous interdependent steps where the orchestration layer can ensure appropriate levels of human involvement (e.g., exception handling), bounded autonomy for AI agents, and enforce compliance.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Throughout these processes, the top-down orchestration layer adds predictability
nodes.text: Throughout these processes, the top-down orchestration layer adds predictability and governance, continually tracks and evaluates key performance indicators (KPIs), ensures the transactional integrity of workflows and rollback logic, and maintains visibility into every stage of the workflow to ensure alignment with overarching business objectives.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: To implement this functionality, this layer consumes policies, rules, and guardr
nodes.text: To implement this functionality, this layer consumes policies, rules, and guardrails from the security and governance layer (via policy-as-code) and business goals and KPIs stored in the Semantic Layer.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Given the autonomous and rapid nature of AI-driven interactions, solely relying
nodes.text: Given the autonomous and rapid nature of AI-driven interactions, solely relying on a decentralized choreography risks strategic misalignment or compliance violations, especially in long-running, multi-step workflows.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The blended orchestration approach mitigates these risks by embedding enterprise
nodes.text: The blended orchestration approach mitigates these risks by embedding enterprise-wide business rules, compliance checks, and policy enforcement directly into complex workflows, and integrates human oversight at critical junctures.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Each of these 11 layers contributes specific functionality to deploying AI agent
nodes.text: Each of these 11 layers contributes specific functionality to deploying AI agents at scale in a secure, trusted, and effective way that unlocks the full potential of agentic AI to transform how work gets done in an enterprise.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The below section outlines the layer’s function, novel changes due to the rise o
nodes.text: The below section outlines the layer’s function, novel changes due to the rise of AI agents, and its key technology capabilities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Experience Layer serves as the primary interface for human users,
nodes.text: Function: The Experience Layer serves as the primary interface for human users, enabling multimodal interaction by capturing inputs (text, voice, visual) and delivering contextually relevant responses across multiple devices.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It seamlessly hands off user intentions to the Agentic Layer for action while al
nodes.text: It seamlessly hands off user intentions to the Agentic Layer for action while also providing the dynamic UI and visualizations that facilitate human escalations and approvals within agentic workflows.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: AI will augment traditional GUI-based interfaces with
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: AI will augment traditional GUI-based interfaces with natural language processing, contextual awareness, and proactive decision support.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI agents will be able to proactively initiate interactions, delivering personal
nodes.text: AI agents will be able to proactively initiate interactions, delivering personalized, real-time recommendations across all channels and modalities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Key technology capabilities:
nodes.text: Key technology capabilities:
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Conversational AI & Digital Assistants: Enable the UX by default to feature AI a
nodes.text: Conversational AI & Digital Assistants: Enable the UX by default to feature AI assistance to support human users.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Attribution & Transparency UI: Make responses explainable in the user interface
nodes.text: Attribution & Transparency UI: Make responses explainable in the user interface such as showing citations, sources of files/systems, and the approach/rationale on the decisions.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Proactive & Ambient Notification Service: Enables agents to proactively push ins
nodes.text: Proactive & Ambient Notification Service: Enables agents to proactively push insights or alerts to users on the most appropriate channel and form factor, based on the user's current context.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Omnichannel Experiences: Provides a seamless, consistent, and unified experience
nodes.text: Omnichannel Experiences: Provides a seamless, consistent, and unified experience across all channels with journey continuity, where conversations and tasks follow the person instead of the app.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Multi-Modal Capabilities: Allows humans to interact with agents and applications
nodes.text: Multi-Modal Capabilities: Allows humans to interact with agents and applications via text, voice, image, touch, video, and AR/VR so agents can understand and present information in the most efficient modality.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Context-Aware Personalization & Dynamic UI: Enables contextually aware real-time
nodes.text: Context-Aware Personalization & Dynamic UI: Enables contextually aware real-time (time, location, user actions) user experiences to enable personalization, including UI generation on the fly.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Agentic Layer acts as the default runtime environment for doing wo
nodes.text: Function: The Agentic Layer acts as the default runtime environment for doing work in the enterprise where AI agents decompose tasks from the experience layer and execute tasks by dynamically assembling workflows using tools from the applications and app services layer and the data layer.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The configuration state of AI agents are stored and managed in this layer.
nodes.text: The configuration state of AI agents are stored and managed in this layer.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agents are instantiated for specific tasks, and the specific agent instances are
nodes.text: Agents are instantiated for specific tasks, and the specific agent instances are decommissioned afterwards.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This implementation enables agents to be always invoked from the latest configur
nodes.text: This implementation enables agents to be always invoked from the latest configuration state based on offline optimizations (using functionality from AI/ML, observability, and orchestration layers).
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This layer is responsible for the AI agents’ comprehensive lifecycle management,
nodes.text: This layer is responsible for the AI agents’ comprehensive lifecycle management, coordination, and governance.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: This layer will emerge today’s current disparate set
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: This layer will emerge today’s current disparate set of pilots and limited agentic deployments. While rule-based bots exist, there are few adaptive, non-deterministic, and goal-oriented software programs deployed at scale.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Runtime Environment: Manages the lifecycle, execution, and resource alloca
nodes.text: Agent Runtime Environment: Manages the lifecycle, execution, and resource allocation for AI agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Lifecycle Management Suite: Includes development frameworks, dev and test
nodes.text: Agent Lifecycle Management Suite: Includes development frameworks, dev and test tooling, and management systems for agent activities and version control.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Reasoning Engine: A cognitive framework for agents to decompose goals, pla
nodes.text: Agent Reasoning Engine: A cognitive framework for agents to decompose goals, plan, and decide what tools to use to solve complex problems.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Memory and Context Store: Allows agent instances to recall and maintain co
nodes.text: Agent Memory and Context Store: Allows agent instances to recall and maintain context about previous interactions, ensuring consistency and personalization.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Interoperability Protocols: Standardized interfaces for agent-to-agent com
nodes.text: Agent Interoperability Protocols: Standardized interfaces for agent-to-agent communication (A2A) and for agents to interface with external systems (such as via the Model Context Protocol).
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Tool Registry: A curated set of internal and externally supported tools for agen
nodes.text: Tool Registry: A curated set of internal and externally supported tools for agents to invoke to accomplish a particular task.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Registry: A curated ecosystem of pre-built AI solutions and agents that su
nodes.text: Agent Registry: A curated ecosystem of pre-built AI solutions and agents that supports discovery and capability matching.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Distributed Agent Policy Enforcement: Enables enterprise-wide governance by allo
nodes.text: Distributed Agent Policy Enforcement: Enables enterprise-wide governance by allowing agents to self-check compliance before taking actions.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Self-Reflection & Adaptation Framework: Provides a mechanism for an agent
nodes.text: Agent Self-Reflection & Adaptation Framework: Provides a mechanism for an agent to analyze its own performance and, with human approval, trigger improvements or suggest modifications to itself.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: This layer functions as a centralized intelligence hub, offering AI mo
nodes.text: Function: This layer functions as a centralized intelligence hub, offering AI models as a set of shared services to be consumed by the Agentic Layer (and applications) to power its reasoning and decision-making capabilities with safety frameworks and monitoring built-in.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: Traditionally, AI models were embedded within specifi
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: Traditionally, AI models were embedded within specific applications.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: In the IT architecture of the Agentic Enterprise, the AI/ML layer will be a firs
nodes.text: In the IT architecture of the Agentic Enterprise, the AI/ML layer will be a first-class, centralized set of services that power many applications and agents, supporting the entire model lifecycle from development to real-time serving at scale.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Pre-built Foundation Models: Large ML models trained on a broad spectrum of data
nodes.text: Pre-built Foundation Models: Large ML models trained on a broad spectrum of data, capable of performing a wide variety of general tasks.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): An AI-centric pipeline that grounds founda
nodes.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): An AI-centric pipeline that grounds foundation models in enterprise-specific data to improve accuracy and reduce hallucinations.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI Trust, Safety, & Governance Hub: A suite of tools integrated into the model l
nodes.text: AI Trust, Safety, & Governance Hub: A suite of tools integrated into the model lifecycle to enforce responsible AI principles like bias detection, explainability, and safety monitoring.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Model Gateway: A routing engine that acts as a single entry point for all model
nodes.text: Model Gateway: A routing engine that acts as a single entry point for all model inference requests, managing calls to various internal and external models to optimize for cost, performance, and compliance.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Model Development Workbench: An integrated development environment for data scie
nodes.text: Model Development Workbench: An integrated development environment for data scientists with tools for data preprocessing, model training, and experimentation.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: MLOps & Lifecycle Automation Pipeline: The CI/CD engine for machine learning, au
nodes.text: MLOps & Lifecycle Automation Pipeline: The CI/CD engine for machine learning, automating the end-to-end lifecycle of models from training to deployment and retirement.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Model Serving & Inference Runtime: A scalable and low-latency environment for de
nodes.text: Model Serving & Inference Runtime: A scalable and low-latency environment for deploying trained models as secure API endpoints for real-time consumption.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Model & Asset Registry: A centralized, version-controlled repository for all AI/
nodes.text: Model & Asset Registry: A centralized, version-controlled repository for all AI/ML assets, including models, datasets, and source code, ensuring reproducibility and auditability.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Synthetic Data Generation and Management: Tooling to generate and manage synthet
nodes.text: Synthetic Data Generation and Management: Tooling to generate and manage synthetic data that maintains the statistical properties of real data without exposing sensitive information.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Enterprise Orchestration Layer is the control plane for end-to-end
nodes.text: Function: The Enterprise Orchestration Layer is the control plane for end-to-end work in an agentic enterprise.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It ensures that agentic workflows and interactions adhere to enterprise objectiv
nodes.text: It ensures that agentic workflows and interactions adhere to enterprise objectives and governance policies.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It consumes observability telemetry from other layers to build comprehensive bus
nodes.text: It consumes observability telemetry from other layers to build comprehensive business process models, enabling optimization against KPIs drawn from the Semantic Layer.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This layer provides the shared context and long-running memory to each new insta
nodes.text: This layer provides the shared context and long-running memory to each new instance of an AI agent for critical workflows.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: This layer provides unified visibility into business
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: This layer provides unified visibility into business processes by creating machine-legible models that capture both structured, deterministic steps, and the unstructured, dynamic work performed by humans and agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It moves beyond today’s human-centric collaboration and governance models by pro
nodes.text: It moves beyond today’s human-centric collaboration and governance models by programmatically encoding business objectives and compliance rules as constraints into agentic workflows to govern the agentic workforce.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Hybrid Workflow Execution Engine: The core runtime that executes the "blended or
nodes.text: Hybrid Workflow Execution Engine: The core runtime that executes the "blended orchestration model," providing centralized oversight while allowing for local agent choreography.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Process Governance & Constraint Engine: A real-time governance service that cons
nodes.text: Process Governance & Constraint Engine: A real-time governance service that consumes and applies declarative business rules, policies, and constraints to all in-flight processes.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Shared Memory and Context Management: A shared memory layer accessible to all ac
nodes.text: Shared Memory and Context Management: A shared memory layer accessible to all actors in a workflow to maintain continuity and coherence across multiple steps.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Process Modeling Studio: A design-time environment for creating and managing mac
nodes.text: Process Modeling Studio: A design-time environment for creating and managing machine-legible, semantically rich process models that define both deterministic and dynamic, goal-oriented steps.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Process Optimization and Simulation: A capability that constructs digital simula
nodes.text: Process Optimization and Simulation: A capability that constructs digital simulations of business processes for advanced analysis, what-if simulations, and predictive optimization.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Process Discovery & Health Monitoring: Ingests process models and real-time data
nodes.text: Process Discovery & Health Monitoring: Ingests process models and real-time data to report on business metrics of process health.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Digital Twin Process Modeling: A real-time mirror of live workflows for testing,
nodes.text: Digital Twin Process Modeling: A real-time mirror of live workflows for testing, simulating changes, and optimizing without impacting production.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: This layer exposes existing business application functionality as comp
nodes.text: Function: This layer exposes existing business application functionality as composable and modular tools and services for agents to use.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It also serves as the presentation runtime for embedding agentic capabilities in
nodes.text: It also serves as the presentation runtime for embedding agentic capabilities into the user experience.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Applications continue to be the system of record but are re-engineered to be "he
nodes.text: Applications continue to be the system of record but are re-engineered to be "headless" capabilities for agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: Applications will evolve from monolithic UIs to "back
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: Applications will evolve from monolithic UIs to "back-end services" that agents can dynamically call via APIs and events.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This layer will be natively integrated with AI agents and models, and the prolif
nodes.text: This layer will be natively integrated with AI agents and models, and the proliferation of code-generation LLMs will lead to an increase in the number of custom, agent-built micro-applications.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Modular Application Services: Decomposed business logic from traditional applica
nodes.text: Modular Application Services: Decomposed business logic from traditional applications, published as machine-readable actions for agents to call.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Embedding SDKs: Toolkits and libraries that enable developers to securely
nodes.text: Agent Embedding SDKs: Toolkits and libraries that enable developers to securely embed agents directly into application UIs.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Dynamic UI Generation Services: Services that allow an AI agent to generate or m
nodes.text: Dynamic UI Generation Services: Services that allow an AI agent to generate or modify UI components in real-time based on user context.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI-Native UI Frameworks: Front-end frameworks designed with built-in support for
nodes.text: AI-Native UI Frameworks: Front-end frameworks designed with built-in support for handling AI-driven UIs, such as managing probabilistic data and streaming text responses.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent-Infused Systems of Engagement: Enterprise productivity and collaboration a
nodes.text: Agent-Infused Systems of Engagement: Enterprise productivity and collaboration applications that incorporate AI agent capabilities through visual components.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI-Enhanced Low-Code / No-Code Application Development: Tools that enable users
nodes.text: AI-Enhanced Low-Code / No-Code Application Development: Tools that enable users and agents to create custom apps and services using natural language and prompts.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: App Guardrails for Agent Usage: Application-side controls for agent usage, such
nodes.text: App Guardrails for Agent Usage: Application-side controls for agent usage, such as rate limiting, scoped permissions, and telemetry.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Semantic Layer provides a unified understanding of data and knowle
nodes.text: Function: The Semantic Layer provides a unified understanding of data and knowledge across the enterprise, enabling both humans and AI agents to interpret and act on information consistently.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It uses knowledge representation tools like ontologies and knowledge graphs to t
nodes.text: It uses knowledge representation tools like ontologies and knowledge graphs to translate natural language queries into precise, context-aware data queries.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: While today’s enterprises have disparate metadata sto
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: While today’s enterprises have disparate metadata stores, the IT architecture of the Agentic Enterprise requires a centralized Enterprise Knowledge Graph (EKG) that links data across domains with explicitly defined semantic relationships.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This provides the rich context that AI agents can traverse to perform complex re
nodes.text: This provides the rich context that AI agents can traverse to perform complex reasoning, creating requirements for a set of technical capabilities to power knowledge graphs that span multiple functional domains.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Metadata Service: Provides descriptive metadata, including data lineage, ownersh
nodes.text: Metadata Service: Provides descriptive metadata, including data lineage, ownership, and classifications.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Business Glossary & Taxonomy Management: A tool for business users to define and
nodes.text: Business Glossary & Taxonomy Management: A tool for business users to define and agree upon standard business terms.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Semantic Model Management: A workbench for knowledge engineers to create, manage
nodes.text: Semantic Model Management: A workbench for knowledge engineers to create, manage, and govern semantic models and ontologies.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Enterprise Knowledge Graph (EKG): A run-time instantiation of the enterprise ont
nodes.text: Enterprise Knowledge Graph (EKG): A run-time instantiation of the enterprise ontology that stores and maps the relationships between business entities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Metadata Ingestion & Harmonization Engine: An automated pipeline that populates
nodes.text: Metadata Ingestion & Harmonization Engine: An automated pipeline that populates and maintains the Enterprise Knowledge Graph from various source systems.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Semantic Query Engine : Interprets natural language queries and constructs struc
nodes.text: Semantic Query Engine : Interprets natural language queries and constructs structured queries based on the EKG to retrieve data from diverse sources.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Semantic Reasoning Engine: Analyzes and derives implicit knowledge and hidden re
nodes.text: Semantic Reasoning Engine: Analyzes and derives implicit knowledge and hidden relationships from the EKG.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Data Layer is the foundational source of truth, managing and provi
nodes.text: Function: The Data Layer is the foundational source of truth, managing and providing secure, governed access to all enterprise data for the Semantic Layer to interpret, the AI/ML Layer to use for training, applications to use for transactions, and agents for reasoning.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: The Data Layer evolves to be more unified, real-time,
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: The Data Layer evolves to be more unified, real-time, and governance-focused, often centered on a cloud-scale data lakehouse.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It must handle a greater volume and variety of data and shift from batch-oriente
nodes.text: It must handle a greater volume and variety of data and shift from batch-oriented processing to real-time streaming to support reactive agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Data governance and quality take on even greater importance to prevent poor data
nodes.text: Data governance and quality take on even greater importance to prevent poor data from creating flawed AI outputs.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: VectorDB: A specialized database optimized for storing and querying high-dimensi
nodes.text: VectorDB: A specialized database optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional vector embeddings, critical for RAG.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Intelligent Analytical Data Pipelines: An automated, metadata-driven service for
nodes.text: Intelligent Analytical Data Pipelines: An automated, metadata-driven service for data ingestion, transformation, and loading (ETL/ELT) into the Data Layer.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Enterprise Data Lakehouse: A central repository for structured, semi-structured,
nodes.text: Enterprise Data Lakehouse: A central repository for structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data, optimized for both analytics and AI workloads.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Zero-Copy Data Federation & Search: Techniques for accessing, querying, and sear
nodes.text: Zero-Copy Data Federation & Search: Techniques for accessing, querying, and searching data across multiple stores without physical data movement.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Natural Language to SQL: A technique for converting natural language queries int
nodes.text: Natural Language to SQL: A technique for converting natural language queries into SQL.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Enterprise Data Catalog & Discovery Service: A centralized, searchable inventory
nodes.text: Enterprise Data Catalog & Discovery Service: A centralized, searchable inventory of all data assets across the enterprise.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Master & Reference Data Management (MDM): Manages the "golden record" for critic
nodes.text: Master & Reference Data Management (MDM): Manages the "golden record" for critical business entities like Customer and Product.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Adaptive Data Quality Service: A continuous monitoring service that uses AI to a
nodes.text: Adaptive Data Quality Service: A continuous monitoring service that uses AI to automatically detect and remediate data quality issues in real time.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Data Contracts: Machine-readable agreements between data producers and consumers
nodes.text: Data Contracts: Machine-readable agreements between data producers and consumers that specify the schema, semantics, and SLAs of data exchange.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI-Specialized Data Stores: Databases designed for specific AI use cases, such a
nodes.text: AI-Specialized Data Stores: Databases designed for specific AI use cases, such as time-series or graph databases.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI-Ready Data Fabric: A logical data abstraction layer that provides a unified,
nodes.text: AI-Ready Data Fabric: A logical data abstraction layer that provides a unified, virtualized view of data across disparate physical systems.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Real-Time Data Processing: Capabilities for processing and analyzing multi-modal
nodes.text: Real-Time Data Processing: Capabilities for processing and analyzing multi-modal data streams continuously at machine speeds.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Infrastructure Layer underpins all other layers, providing the com
nodes.text: Function: The Infrastructure Layer underpins all other layers, providing the compute, storage, network, and cloud capabilities required to run AI and agentic workloads at scale in a resilient and cost-efficient manner.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: AI workloads require greater scalability and elastici
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: AI workloads require greater scalability and elasticity to handle the probabilistic nature of agentic systems. Infrastructure must support rapid provisioning, specialized hardware like GPUs, and low-latency, high-throughput network traffic for inter-agent communication.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Infrastructure as Code: Automated provisioning and management of infrastructure
nodes.text: Infrastructure as Code: Automated provisioning and management of infrastructure with CI/CD deployment pipelines.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Hybrid & Multi-Cloud AI Infrastructure: Leverages public cloud elasticity and sp
nodes.text: Hybrid & Multi-Cloud AI Infrastructure: Leverages public cloud elasticity and specialized infrastructure for generative AI workloads.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI-Optimized Compute, Storage, and Network: Dynamically allocates and scales inf
nodes.text: AI-Optimized Compute, Storage, and Network: Dynamically allocates and scales infrastructure resources based on variable demand from AI workloads.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Edge AI Infrastructure: Enables AI models and agents to be deployed at the edge
nodes.text: Edge AI Infrastructure: Enables AI models and agents to be deployed at the edge of the network for use cases with unique latency or privacy requirements.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Self-Healing Infrastructure: Uses AI to manage system recovery without manual in
nodes.text: Self-Healing Infrastructure: Uses AI to manage system recovery without manual input, ensuring high availability.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Sustainable AI Computing: Energy-efficient approaches to mitigate the environmen
nodes.text: Sustainable AI Computing: Energy-efficient approaches to mitigate the environmental impact of AI workloads.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Cost- & Carbon-Aware Autoscaling: Uses FinOps and sustainability signals to driv
nodes.text: Cost- & Carbon-Aware Autoscaling: Uses FinOps and sustainability signals to drive scaling and placement of capacity.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: The Integration Layer serves as the universal communication fabric for
nodes.text: Function: The Integration Layer serves as the universal communication fabric for all systems (legacy and new) through APIs, events, protocols, and middleware to ensure agents can discover and interact with services, data, and tools seamlessly.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: Integration must evolve to support the dynamic, many-
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: Integration must evolve to support the dynamic, many-to-many communication patterns of AI agents, rather than just handling predetermined, static interactions between a few known systems.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It requires real-time data processing, and must accommodate ad-hoc discovery and
nodes.text: It requires real-time data processing, and must accommodate ad-hoc discovery and collaboration between agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Operational Data Connectivity Pipeline: AI-assisted tools for automatic schema m
nodes.text: Operational Data Connectivity Pipeline: AI-assisted tools for automatic schema mapping, data transformation, and workflow generation, including API-led, event-driven, and Reverse ETL capabilities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Adaptive API Management and Service Mesh: API gateways and service mesh technolo
nodes.text: Adaptive API Management and Service Mesh: API gateways and service mesh technology that can dynamically register, discover, and govern services with adaptive policy enforcement for agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Semantic Knowledge Adapters: An integration component that provides a shared voc
nodes.text: Semantic Knowledge Adapters: An integration component that provides a shared vocabulary and data model across agents and applications for consistent data interpretation.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Event-Driven Integration Fabric: A high-throughput, low-latency messaging and st
nodes.text: Event-Driven Integration Fabric: A high-throughput, low-latency messaging and streaming backbone that enables decoupled, asynchronous communication.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Protocol Gateway: A gateway for MCP services that allows agents to securel
nodes.text: Agent Protocol Gateway: A gateway for MCP services that allows agents to securely discover tools and trigger actions, bridging MCP to internal APIs and events.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Composable Capability Catalog & Marketplace: A centralized, governed catalog for
nodes.text: Composable Capability Catalog & Marketplace: A centralized, governed catalog for all enterprise capabilities—APIs, services, agent skills, models, and datasets—annotated with semantic metadata for on-demand composition.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Function: This layer monitors and manages the health and operational performance
nodes.text: Function: This layer monitors and manages the health and operational performance of agents and the entire system (observability embedded principle), providing transparency and control by generating insights to enable auditing, debugging, explainability, cost, and resource optimization of the enterprise’s agentic workforce.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today : This layer becomes even more crucial given the risk
nodes.text: What’s different vs today : This layer becomes even more crucial given the risk of AI agents creating errors at machine speed.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: It must expand beyond infrastructure monitoring to include the unpredictable beh
nodes.text: It must expand beyond infrastructure monitoring to include the unpredictable behavior of autonomous agents, requiring new kinds of telemetry and the ability to understand semantic correctness, not just technical performance.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Real-Time Monitoring & Observability Platform: Continuously collects logs, metri
nodes.text: Real-Time Monitoring & Observability Platform: Continuously collects logs, metrics, and traces across the entire IT environment, with extensions for ML metrics and agent behavior.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: FinOps & Cloud Cost Management: Responsible for monitoring, analyzing, and optim
nodes.text: FinOps & Cloud Cost Management: Responsible for monitoring, analyzing, and optimizing the infrastructure costs associated with AI and agentic workloads.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent and ML-specific Monitoring: Logs each step of an agent's run in an immutab
nodes.text: Agent and ML-specific Monitoring: Logs each step of an agent's run in an immutable audit trail and continuously profiles agent behaviors to detect deviations from established norms.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AIOps, Incident and Change Management: Uses AI/ML to predict potential IT issues
nodes.text: AIOps, Incident and Change Management: Uses AI/ML to predict potential IT issues, identify root causes, and create remediation workflows.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Closed Learning Feedback Loop: Integrates observability telemetry from agents ba
nodes.text: Closed Learning Feedback Loop: Integrates observability telemetry from agents back into MLOps pipelines, enabling automated model retraining or prompt adjustment.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Semantic Observability Engine: Integrates observability with the semantic layer
nodes.text: Semantic Observability Engine: Integrates observability with the semantic layer for contextualization to enable the detection of semantic anomalies in agent behavior.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Purpose: This layer embeds trust and safety throughout the architecture by prote
nodes.text: Purpose: This layer embeds trust and safety throughout the architecture by protecting the enterprise’s assets from threats, managing risk, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. It encompasses identity management, threat detection, GRC, and AI-specific security measures.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: What’s different vs today: The security layer must evolve to address new attack
nodes.text: What’s different vs today: The security layer must evolve to address new attack surfaces presented by AI models and agents, such as prompt injection and model poisoning.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Identity and access management must shift from static, role-based controls to dy
nodes.text: Identity and access management must shift from static, role-based controls to dynamic, intent-based permissions that are granted just-in-time and revoked immediately after use.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: LLM Input/Output Security & Guardrails: Enterprise guardrails applied at prompt
nodes.text: LLM Input/Output Security & Guardrails: Enterprise guardrails applied at prompt and response time to block unsafe content, PII leaks, and jailbreaks.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Zero Trust Architecture with AI Verification: Continuous authentication enhanced
nodes.text: Zero Trust Architecture with AI Verification: Continuous authentication enhanced by AI-based behavioral analysis, with granular, just-in-time access for agents based on their specific task.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent Security Framework: Fine-grained permission models, monitoring for harmful
nodes.text: Agent Security Framework: Fine-grained permission models, monitoring for harmful behaviors, and containment mechanisms to safely interrupt agent activities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI Model Security: A defense-in-depth strategy with controls at each stage of th
nodes.text: AI Model Security: A defense-in-depth strategy with controls at each stage of the model lifecycle to protect against poisoning, extraction, and adversarial attacks.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Privacy-Preserving AI: Techniques such as federated learning and differential pr
nodes.text: Privacy-Preserving AI: Techniques such as federated learning and differential privacy to protect sensitive data.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: AI-Enhanced GRC: Use of AI agents to continuously monitor IT architecture compli
nodes.text: AI-Enhanced GRC: Use of AI agents to continuously monitor IT architecture compliance with controls.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Policy-as-Code Engine: A single source of truth for defining business rules and
nodes.text: Policy-as-Code Engine: A single source of truth for defining business rules and compliance constraints in a declarative, machine-readable format to set guardrails for agent behavior.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Continuous Red-Teaming: Automated, ongoing adversarial testing of AI models and
nodes.text: Continuous Red-Teaming: Automated, ongoing adversarial testing of AI models and agents to identify vulnerabilities before attackers exploit them.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Transforming into Agentic Enterprise requires following a multi-stage journey by
nodes.text: Transforming into Agentic Enterprise requires following a multi-stage journey by setting the technology foundations while creating tangible business value (see Figure 3 below).
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: While the precise roadmap depends on the enterprise’s strategy, culture, AI gove
nodes.text: While the precise roadmap depends on the enterprise’s strategy, culture, AI governance model and IT architectural starting point, most organizations should adopt a phased approach as continued IT investments power agents with growing scope, complexity, and value creation.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Salesforce’s Agentic Maturity Model offers a useful framework of maturity levels
nodes.text: Salesforce’s Agentic Maturity Model offers a useful framework of maturity levels for enterprises to strategize their transformation, outlining how agent capabilities can grow from basic informational retrieval (level 1) to orchestrating more complex multi-domain (level 2 and 3) and multi-agent workflows (level 4).
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: To successfully advance through these stages, the IT architecture must evolve in
nodes.text: To successfully advance through these stages, the IT architecture must evolve in a concerted manner, with targeted investments in different layers of the architecture at each phase to meet the needs of the more complex and higher-value deployments of AI agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: For each maturity level, the specific technology capabilities required across th
nodes.text: For each maturity level, the specific technology capabilities required across the 11 architectural layers are identified with a rationale for investment.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Maturity Level 1: Information Retrieval Agents Business Objective & Value: Enhan
nodes.text: Maturity Level 1: Information Retrieval Agents Business Objective & Value: Enhance human worker productivity by providing a trusted, conversational interface for querying enterprise knowledge.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The primary value is in augmenting human capabilities, not replacing them.
nodes.text: The primary value is in augmenting human capabilities, not replacing them.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: These agents assist humans by retrieving information and recommending actions.
nodes.text: These agents assist humans by retrieving information and recommending actions.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Architectural Focus: The focus is on establishing a secure, reliable data founda
nodes.text: Architectural Focus: The focus is on establishing a secure, reliable data foundation and the basic AI components needed for information retrieval. Governance and observability are critical from day one to build user trust and to control costs.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Key Technology Investments (Figure 4): At this stage, IT should focus on creatin
nodes.text: Key Technology Investments (Figure 4): At this stage, IT should focus on creating a trustworthy data-to-agent pipeline and other foundation capabilities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Technologies in the Data Layer , such as a VectorDB , are essential for enabling
nodes.text: Technologies in the Data Layer , such as a VectorDB , are essential for enabling the retrieval augmented generation (RAG) techniques that powers information retrieval agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This is coupled with a centralized AI/ML Layer that includes a Model Gateway for
nodes.text: This is coupled with a centralized AI/ML Layer that includes a Model Gateway for secure, cost-controlled access to foundation models and an AI Trust, Safety & Governance Hub to monitor for unsafe outputs and ensure compliance.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Master data management and business glossaries in the Semantic Layer are foundat
nodes.text: Master data management and business glossaries in the Semantic Layer are foundational for agents to retrieve accurate information.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Agent runtime and lifecycle capabilities are required to ensure agents built in
nodes.text: Agent runtime and lifecycle capabilities are required to ensure agents built in this stage can be modified and extended for future use cases.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: To deliver value and build user confidence, the Experience Layer must incorporat
nodes.text: To deliver value and build user confidence, the Experience Layer must incorporate an Attribution & Transparency UI , which makes agent responses explainable by showing citations and the sources of its information.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Foundational observability and security investments (for example zero trust ) mu
nodes.text: Foundational observability and security investments (for example zero trust ) must begin implementation to set the stage for future agentic deployments.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Maturity Level 2: Simple Orchestration, Single Domain Agents Business Objective
nodes.text: Maturity Level 2: Simple Orchestration, Single Domain Agents Business Objective & Value: Automate routine tasks and orchestrate low-complexity workflows within a single business domain.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This improves operational efficiency and reduces manual work, allowing human wor
nodes.text: This improves operational efficiency and reduces manual work, allowing human workers to focus on higher-value activities.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Architectural Focus: The key architectural shift is from read-only data retrieva
nodes.text: Architectural Focus: The key architectural shift is from read-only data retrieval to executing actions.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This requires beginning a longer journey to modularize application functionality
nodes.text: This requires beginning a longer journey to modularize application functionality (often exposed as APIs) for agents to access, implementing robust security for agent actions, and building semantic and AI development functionality to further the intelligence of AI agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Key Technology Investments (Figure 5): Investments thematically center on enabli
nodes.text: Key Technology Investments (Figure 5): Investments thematically center on enabling AI agents to take action with proper governance in place.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The Applications and App Services Layer undergoes a critical change, as monolith
nodes.text: The Applications and App Services Layer undergoes a critical change, as monolithic business logic is decomposed into modular application services (APIs) for agents to call.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: These are protected by App Guardrails to prevent agents from overwhelming system
nodes.text: These are protected by App Guardrails to prevent agents from overwhelming systems, with integrations into observability tooling.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: To power these agents, investments need to be made in agent reasoning, tool prot
nodes.text: To power these agents, investments need to be made in agent reasoning, tool protocols (such as MCP), and registries .
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This introduces new risks, making a dedicated Agent Security Framework and AI mo
nodes.text: This introduces new risks, making a dedicated Agent Security Framework and AI model and agent monitoring capabilities essential for governance and security.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Lastly, enterprises can begin scaling their AI/ML capabilities for custom models
nodes.text: Lastly, enterprises can begin scaling their AI/ML capabilities for custom models to power these agents tackling domain-specific tasks.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Maturity Level 3: Multi-Domain Orchestration Agents Business Objective & Value:
nodes.text: Maturity Level 3: Multi-Domain Orchestration Agents Business Objective & Value: Automate complex, end-to-end business processes that span organizational and functional boundaries (such as "quote-to-cash", “lead to order”).
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The value is in breaking down silos, accelerating process cycle times, and optim
nodes.text: The value is in breaking down silos, accelerating process cycle times, and optimizing entire value chains within the business.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Higher step changes in human worker productivity are possible as organizational
nodes.text: Higher step changes in human worker productivity are possible as organizational barriers begin to break down and humans begin to focus more on overseeing AI agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Architectural Focus: The architecture must now support cross-cutting technical i
nodes.text: Architectural Focus: The architecture must now support cross-cutting technical issues. A shared semantic understanding across the enterprise, a centralized orchestration engine for governance, and a decoupled, event-driven integration fabric become the critical enablers.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Key Technology Investments (Figure 6): Technology investments are thematically f
nodes.text: Key Technology Investments (Figure 6): Technology investments are thematically focused on orchestrating processes at an enterprise scale across humans, agents, and deterministic systems.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The Enterprise Orchestration Layer becomes a focus of investment, requiring a Hy
nodes.text: The Enterprise Orchestration Layer becomes a focus of investment, requiring a Hybrid Workflow Execution Engine to coordinate activities, and a Process Governance & Constraint Engine to enforce business rules and compliance policies on in-flight processes since agents are working across domains, often with different policies and semantic definitions.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This cross-domain coordination is only possible with a mature Semantic Layer tha
nodes.text: This cross-domain coordination is only possible with a mature Semantic Layer that features an Enterprise Knowledge Graph (EKG) , which creates a shared understanding of how business entities relate across domains.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The Integration Layer must evolve to include an Event-Driven Integration Fabric
nodes.text: The Integration Layer must evolve to include an Event-Driven Integration Fabric , which uses a streaming backbone to decouple systems and enable the resilient, asynchronous communication typical of long-running enterprise processes.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Given the higher value of these workflows and the associated risks, additional i
nodes.text: Given the higher value of these workflows and the associated risks, additional investments in security and monitoring become paramount (for example AIOps, policy-as-code ).
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Lastly, further investment must be made in the Application and Services Layer (s
nodes.text: Lastly, further investment must be made in the Application and Services Layer (such as AI-enabled LCNC, more dynamic and multi-modal user experiences) as human users begin to monitor and collaborate with more capable AI agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Maturity Level 4: Multi-Agent, Multi-Domain Orchestration Business Objective & V
nodes.text: Maturity Level 4: Multi-Agent, Multi-Domain Orchestration Business Objective & Value: Redesign and optimize business operations across domains to drive step changes in productivity and efficiency.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This stage moves toward creating a holistic digital simulation (digital twin) of
nodes.text: This stage moves toward creating a holistic digital simulation (digital twin) of the enterprise for continuous improvement and strategic planning across major business processes and workflows.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Architectural Focus: The final stage focuses on enabling dynamic, emergent colla
nodes.text: Architectural Focus: The final stage focuses on enabling dynamic, emergent collaboration between agents.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This requires advanced agent-to-agent (A2A) communication protocols, agent self-
nodes.text: This requires advanced agent-to-agent (A2A) communication protocols, agent self-learning capabilities, further investing in maturing the Orchestration, Data, and Semantic layers, and fully dynamic and self-healing infrastructure to support the growing needs of dynamic AI workloads as agents have been scaled out across the enterprise.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Key Technology Investments (Figure 7): Thematic investments focus on creating a
nodes.text: Key Technology Investments (Figure 7): Thematic investments focus on creating a self-improving, autonomous system.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: In the Agentic Layer , an Agent Self-Reflection & Adaptation Framework provides
nodes.text: In the Agentic Layer , an Agent Self-Reflection & Adaptation Framework provides the mechanism for an agent to analyze its own performance logs and trigger improvements.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: This learning is supported by the IT Operations and Observability Layer , which
nodes.text: This learning is supported by the IT Operations and Observability Layer , which implements a Closed Learning Feedback Loop to automatically feed observability data back into MLOps pipelines for model retraining which can also leverage synthetic data generation to further optimize model performance.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: With networks of agents being deployed across departments along with ongoing app
nodes.text: With networks of agents being deployed across departments along with ongoing application modularization efforts, further investments in security and crucially a composable capability catalog become necessary for agents to dynamically compose capabilities to solve more abstract and higher-value tasks.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: These processes are all orchestrated and optimized via a new Digital Twin Proces
nodes.text: These processes are all orchestrated and optimized via a new Digital Twin Process Modeling capability, which uses real-time data to create simulations for "what-if" analysis and predictive optimization, allowing the enterprise to safely test and deploy new agentic deployments.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Conclusion The roadmap to an Agentic Enterprise runs through an IT architectural
nodes.text: Conclusion The roadmap to an Agentic Enterprise runs through an IT architectural evolution.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: Enterprise architects will be the crucial drivers of this transformation, drivin
nodes.text: Enterprise architects will be the crucial drivers of this transformation, driving the critical investment decisions, along with other partners in the business and IT, necessary for the organization to realize the value from the new business capabilities of the Agentic Enterprise.
nodes.author: Salesforce Architects

nodes.label: The Future of Enterprise Architecture in the Age of AI: The Shift Towards Near R
nodes.text: The Future of Enterprise Architecture in the Age of AI: The Shift Towards Near Real-Time Digital Governance
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA in the age of AI: from the ground detail to the big-picture perspective.
nodes.text: EA in the age of AI: from the ground detail to the big-picture perspective.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Enterprise Architecture (EA) is no longer a discipline defined by static artefac
nodes.text: Enterprise Architecture (EA) is no longer a discipline defined by static artefacts and quarterly governance reviews.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: In today’s digital-first environment, it has evolved into a dynamic, near real-t
nodes.text: In today’s digital-first environment, it has evolved into a dynamic, near real-time strategic decision-support that also supports the intelligent adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Generative AI (GenAI) .
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Together, these technologies enable structured, data-driven change and guide str
nodes.text: Together, these technologies enable structured, data-driven change and guide strategic direction with greater clarity and agility, highlighting things in a big-picture perspective; one of EA’s primary goals is to ensure strategy to execution with focus on model-driven outcomes.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The purpose of EA remains the same: leading structured change with clarity, coll
nodes.text: The purpose of EA remains the same: leading structured change with clarity, collaboration, and guidance. While modelling continues to be its underlying engine, EA today is far more than documentation.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: A decade ago, EA’s role was largely to define ownership and produce visual model
nodes.text: A decade ago, EA’s role was largely to define ownership and produce visual models that took years to develop; we often refer to this as the way to prepare everything yourself, see  “ Kitchen Principle” ; however, modern EA is stakeholder-focused and enables digital governance by making responsibilities visible and actionable with speed and focus on outcomes.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Enterprise Architects enable change, they do not prohibit change; maybe a decade
nodes.text: Enterprise Architects enable change, they do not prohibit change; maybe a decade back the function was known for its red-tape rather than the red-carpet of guiding the change.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Platforms such as Next-Insight exemplify this shift to modern practice, empoweri
nodes.text: Platforms such as Next-Insight exemplify this shift to modern practice, empowering individuals to co-create updates in real time transforming decision-support into a living digital twin of the enterprise.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Modern EA must provide clarity through digital roles, an online knowledge portal
nodes.text: Modern EA must provide clarity through digital roles, an online knowledge portal, and feedback loops that make architecture the strategic interface between business planning and technology.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: It must enable near real-time decision-making rather than retrospective analysis
nodes.text: It must enable near real-time decision-making rather than retrospective analysis.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Nobody wants to sit in a quarterly meeting realising what should have been actio
nodes.text: Nobody wants to sit in a quarterly meeting realising what should have been actioned the same day of the occurrence.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The current challenge lies in integrating AI and GenAI so that EA evolves beyond
nodes.text: The current challenge lies in integrating AI and GenAI so that EA evolves beyond modern practice into the next wave of strategic intelligence; capable of responding instantly to emerging situations that demand immediate, data-driven action.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: In the recent paper, “ Modifying TOGAF To Optimise Artificial Intelligence”  ( E
nodes.text: In the recent paper, “ Modifying TOGAF To Optimise Artificial Intelligence”  ( Ern, Yang, Wu, Ambakkat, Dilnutt & Meratian, University of Melbourne, 2025) ,  the authors highlight how the TOGAF framework apparently lacks the agility, ethical governance, and lifecycle artefacts required for AI-driven transformation to really fulfil its role as purposeful within EA.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Their work proposes extending the framework to meet the needs of automation, AI,
nodes.text: Their work proposes extending the framework to meet the needs of automation, AI, and digital governance in contemporary enterprises.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The paper presents a critique of TOGAF’s limitations in supporting AI integratio
nodes.text: The paper presents a critique of TOGAF’s limitations in supporting AI integration.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: It argues that although TOGAF remains a powerful EA framework as a skeleton, it
nodes.text: It argues that although TOGAF remains a powerful EA framework as a skeleton, it must evolve to accommodate agile methods, ethical governance, and advanced systems integration with near-realtime updates.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Key recommendations include:
nodes.text: Key recommendations include:
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Embedding iterative feedback loops within the ADM cycle
nodes.text: Embedding iterative feedback loops within the ADM cycle
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Expanding EA artefacts to include information of AI lifecycle components (e.g. G
nodes.text: Expanding EA artefacts to include information of AI lifecycle components (e.g. GenAI  catalogues, drift detection, explainability modules)
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Integrating ethical governance and regulatory compliance into architectural prac
nodes.text: Integrating ethical governance and regulatory compliance into architectural practice
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Enhancing system integration capabilities across hybrid and cloud environments w
nodes.text: Enhancing system integration capabilities across hybrid and cloud environments with near-realtime updates
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Developing interdisciplinary roles and strengthening AI literacy within EA teams
nodes.text: Developing interdisciplinary roles and strengthening AI literacy within EA teams
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The authors recognise EA’s strategic value and call for its evolution into a mor
nodes.text: The authors recognise EA’s strategic value and call for its evolution into a more adaptive, AI-aware discipline. Read the full paper here.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA as Digital Governance versus Traditional Documentation
nodes.text: EA as Digital Governance versus Traditional Documentation
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Modern EA must transcend traditional documentation.
nodes.text: Modern EA must transcend traditional documentation.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: It must enable digital governance as a digital hub where obligations are visible
nodes.text: It must enable digital governance as a digital hub where obligations are visible, traceable, and continuously updated.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The article supports this shift, calling for dynamic, AI-integrated governance m
nodes.text: The article supports this shift, calling for dynamic, AI-integrated governance models that reflect real-time enterprise realities.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: This is not about a single GenAI agent; it is about the collective intelligence
nodes.text: This is not about a single GenAI agent; it is about the collective intelligence of many.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: When hundreds of AI agents interact, they can exhibit emergent behaviour, diverg
nodes.text: When hundreds of AI agents interact, they can exhibit emergent behaviour, diverging from expected norms.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: This ‘flock mentality’ can lead to unforeseen outcomes, requiring human oversigh
nodes.text: This ‘flock mentality’ can lead to unforeseen outcomes, requiring human oversight and structured intervention.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA must be the layer that identifies these patterns and triggers informed respon
nodes.text: EA must be the layer that identifies these patterns and triggers informed responses.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: We can easily pin-point a handfull of key observations from the research paper.
nodes.text: We can easily pin-point a handfull of key observations from the research paper.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: #1 – EA as a Digital Twin to respond with AI Signals
nodes.text: #1 – EA as a Digital Twin to respond with AI Signals
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: For sure, EA will continue to rely on the concept of the digital twin , but now
nodes.text: For sure, EA will continue to rely on the concept of the digital twin , but now it must respond to AI signals much closer and near real time.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The paper critiques TOGAF’s ADM cycle as being “linear” (which is per se not its
nodes.text: The paper critiques TOGAF’s ADM cycle as being “linear” (which is per se not its full intention, it is iterative) and recommends embedding agile feedback loops to create a living, responsive system.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: AI platforms increasingly generate governance signals (alerts, anomalies, or tra
nodes.text: AI platforms increasingly generate governance signals (alerts, anomalies, or traffic-light indicators) that EA solutions must interpret and act upon much faster and almost immediately.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: This is akin to occurrence management in risk domains: there is no time for retr
nodes.text: This is akin to occurrence management in risk domains: there is no time for retrospective analysis.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA must empower leaders to make evidence-based decisions on the spot and use out
nodes.text: EA must empower leaders to make evidence-based decisions on the spot and use outcomes for continual improvement.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: #2 – Governance of GenAI Agents and Clusters
nodes.text: #2 – Governance of GenAI Agents and Clusters
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: As GenAI agents proliferate, their interactions become complex and potentially r
nodes.text: As GenAI agents proliferate, their interactions become complex and potentially risky.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: While each agent may operate under predefined business rules, EA must govern the
nodes.text: While each agent may operate under predefined business rules, EA must govern these clusters collectively, not merely as individual systems.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The research highlights the need for lifecycle artefacts, ethical audits, and ca
nodes.text: The research highlights the need for lifecycle artefacts, ethical audits, and capability matrices to manage this complexity.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The risk increases exponentially when AI agents begin influencing one another.
nodes.text: The risk increases exponentially when AI agents begin influencing one another.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA must therefore provide governance structures that detect divergence within cl
nodes.text: EA must therefore provide governance structures that detect divergence within clusters and initiate escalation protocols.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: This governance is not purely technical, it is strategic, and should be embedded
nodes.text: This governance is not purely technical, it is strategic, and should be embedded within the EA solution itself.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: #3 – EA and Operations: Shared Strategic Oversight
nodes.text: #3 – EA and Operations: Shared Strategic Oversight
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Real-time oversight has traditionally been considered operational, yet the resea
nodes.text: Real-time oversight has traditionally been considered operational, yet the research suggests that EA must expand to include operational governance of AI.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA remains the strategic layer defining how governance is structured, automated,
nodes.text: EA remains the strategic layer defining how governance is structured, automated, and monitored.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: When markets shift or systems fail, EA must provide predefined routines that ena
nodes.text: When markets shift or systems fail, EA must provide predefined routines that enable swift, informed action.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: These are not quarterly review topics; they are near real-time decisions.
nodes.text: These are not quarterly review topics; they are near real-time decisions.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The closer the integration between EA systems and AI platforms, the stronger the
nodes.text: The closer the integration between EA systems and AI platforms, the stronger the organisation’s ability to steer intelligently.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: #4 – Systems Integration and Infrastructure Readiness EA must orchestrate real-t
nodes.text: #4 – Systems Integration and Infrastructure Readiness EA must orchestrate real-time, cross-platform integration.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The paper identifies TOGAF’s limitations in this area and calls for usage of cap
nodes.text: The paper identifies TOGAF’s limitations in this area and calls for usage of capabilities (reference models) where AI components aligned to strategic goals will serve best. EA must ensure that AI platforms are not isolated from the business layer and strategic intent.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Governance signals must flow seamlessly across systems, with architecture provid
nodes.text: Governance signals must flow seamlessly across systems, with architecture providing a holistic interpretation.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Infrastructure readiness now extends beyond hardware; it encompasses visibility,
nodes.text: Infrastructure readiness now extends beyond hardware; it encompasses visibility, agility, and the capacity for continuous improvement.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: #5 – Skills, Collaboration, and Hybrid Roles The evolution of EA depends on inte
nodes.text: #5 – Skills, Collaboration, and Hybrid Roles The evolution of EA depends on interdisciplinary collaboration .
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: The research recommends creating hybrid roles such as AI-EA strategists and embe
nodes.text: The research recommends creating hybrid roles such as AI-EA strategists and embedding AI literacy into EA certifications.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: EA must become the meeting point between architecture, data science, and leaders
nodes.text: EA must become the meeting point between architecture, data science, and leadership.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: These hybrid roles are essential to interpret AI signals, assess strategic impac
nodes.text: These hybrid roles are essential to interpret AI signals, assess strategic impact, and guide structured change.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: This is not a distant future vision—it is an immediate organisational necessity.
nodes.text: This is not a distant future vision—it is an immediate organisational necessity.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Conclusion: EA as the Strategic Interface to AI Platforms The future of EA is no
nodes.text: Conclusion: EA as the Strategic Interface to AI Platforms The future of EA is not about modelling; it is about interacting.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: As GenAI agents become embedded within enterprise operations, EA must provide th
nodes.text: As GenAI agents become embedded within enterprise operations, EA must provide the strategic governance layer that ensures alignment, accountability, and agility.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: Platforms like Next-Insight already embody this vision offering digital modellin
nodes.text: Platforms like Next-Insight already embody this vision offering digital modelling and integration options to allow real-time signal integration, and strategic clarity across business and technology layers.
nodes.author: staunstender.com

nodes.label: If you are seeking an EA approach that moves beyond static frameworks and embrac
nodes.text: If you are seeking an EA approach that moves beyond static frameworks and embraces the future of digital governance, this is the time to adopt an architecture that is living, responsive, and AI-integrated.
