nodes.label: Source: MIT CISR 2022 Future Ready Survey (N=721) and interviews in 2024 with si
nodes.text: Source: MIT CISR 2022 Future Ready Survey (N=721) and interviews in 2024 with sixteen executives at nine enterprises.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Respondents were grouped into four stages using a measure of Total AI Effectiven
nodes.text: Respondents were grouped into four stages using a measure of Total AI Effectiveness, the equally weighted combo of three measures: effectiveness of AI to (i) improve operations, (ii) improve customer experience, and (iii) support and develop the ecosystem.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: On a 0–100% scale of Total AI Effectiveness, Stage 1 AI Capability=0–49%, Stage
nodes.text: On a 0–100% scale of Total AI Effectiveness, Stage 1 AI Capability=0–49%, Stage 2=50–74%, Stage 3=75–99%, Stage 4=100%.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Measures of growth and profit are from 2022, calculated as percentage points (pp
nodes.text: Measures of growth and profit are from 2022, calculated as percentage points (pp) above/below industry average.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: The first stage of developing enterprise AI maturity is about preparing and expe
nodes.text: The first stage of developing enterprise AI maturity is about preparing and experimenting to use AI effectively.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: In our survey analysis, 28 percent of enterprises were in this stage.
nodes.text: In our survey analysis, 28 percent of enterprises were in this stage.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Stage 1 enterprises focus on educating their workforce, formulating AI policies,
nodes.text: Stage 1 enterprises focus on educating their workforce, formulating AI policies, becoming more evidence-based, and experimenting with AI technologies to get more comfortable with automated decision-making.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: They discuss where humans need to be in the loop for oversight and what is accep
nodes.text: They discuss where humans need to be in the loop for oversight and what is acceptable and ethical use of AI technology.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Funding targets AI literacy for the board and top management team, and skill bui
nodes.text: Funding targets AI literacy for the board and top management team, and skill building on AI technologies integrated into enterprise software for the rest of the enterprise.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: During stage 1, the enterprise begins to identify both value creation opportunit
nodes.text: During stage 1, the enterprise begins to identify both value creation opportunities from AI and the enterprise capabilities and competencies required to realize them.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: For example, Kaiser Permanente, a US West Coast not-for-profit healthcare enterp
nodes.text: For example, Kaiser Permanente, a US West Coast not-for-profit healthcare enterprise with 12.5 million members, 2 defined seven principles for assessing its responsible use of AI: privacy, reliability, high-quality and affordable outcomes, transparency, equity, customer prioritization, and trust.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: These principles help Kaiser Permanente ensure that AI tools meet its standards.
nodes.text: These principles help Kaiser Permanente ensure that AI tools meet its standards. 3 The second stage, representing 34 percent of enterprises in our research, is about setting up AI pilots to demonstrate AI’s ability to create value for both the enterprise and its people.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: In this stage, enterprises define important metrics, begin to simplify and autom
nodes.text: In this stage, enterprises define important metrics, begin to simplify and automate business processes, and develop the enterprise capabilities they learned they need during the last stage.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Stage 2 enterprises focus on moving from experiments to systematic innovation by
nodes.text: Stage 2 enterprises focus on moving from experiments to systematic innovation by piloting use cases, tracking value created in the pilots, and storytelling both internally and externally about learnings from the pilots.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Fundamental to stage 2, the enterprise determines how to consolidate organizatio
nodes.text: Fundamental to stage 2, the enterprise determines how to consolidate organizational data silos and safely and securely serve the data for use with AI; this typically requires a significant investment in, or refinement of, APIs that link the data and the technologies.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: In an AI pilot, the disability underwriting team at Guardian Life, a US mutual i
nodes.text: In an AI pilot, the disability underwriting team at Guardian Life, a US mutual insurance enterprise, is working with a partner to develop a generative AI tool that summarizes documentation and augments decision-making.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Participating underwriters are saving on average five hours per day, building on
nodes.text: Participating underwriters are saving on average five hours per day, building on Guardian’s rich internal data and automated processes.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: This pilot is helping Guardian to achieve its goal of reimagining end-to-end pro
nodes.text: This pilot is helping Guardian to achieve its goal of reimagining end-to-end process transformation. 4 Moving to stage 3 is a big step in the AI journey, both in terms of potential impact on enterprise growth and profitability and the platform capabilities needed to achieve the scale required.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Stage 3, which represented 31 percent of enterprises in our research, is focused
nodes.text: Stage 3, which represented 31 percent of enterprises in our research, is focused on industrializing AI throughout the enterprise.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: This includes building a scalable enterprise architecture (a platform for AI tha
nodes.text: This includes building a scalable enterprise architecture (a platform for AI that allows for scaling and reusing models), making data and outcomes transparent via business dashboards, developing a pervasive test-and-learn culture, and expanding business process automation efforts.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Ally, the largest all-digital bank in the US, is scaling AI via its Ally.ai plat
nodes.text: Ally, the largest all-digital bank in the US, is scaling AI via its Ally.ai platform, which the enterprise launched in July 2023.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: The platform houses Ally AI tools that leverage technologies including natural l
nodes.text: The platform houses Ally AI tools that leverage technologies including natural language processing, machine learning, predictive analytics, and computer vision, and integrates several commercially available large language models with the enterprise’s data in a safe environment.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Early value from generative AI use cases includes saving an average three minute
nodes.text: Early value from generative AI use cases includes saving an average three minutes per customer call on more than four million customer calls in 2023 using call summarization, and developing creative campaigns 34 percent faster using a marketing generative AI chat feature. 5 In stage 3 we see enterprises making significant use of foundation models, and small language models (SLMs) that are trained on an industry, a function, or to perform specific tasks (such as onboarding a customer), to advance their journeys. 6 They take these foundation models and SLMs and, on secure enterprise platforms, apply them to their own data to create and capture new value.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: In the fourth stage enterprises are what we call AI future ready, which represen
nodes.text: In the fourth stage enterprises are what we call AI future ready, which represented 7 percent of enterprises in our research.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: AI is embedded in all decision-making in these enterprises.
nodes.text: AI is embedded in all decision-making in these enterprises.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: They leverage proprietary AI 7 internally, and many sell new business services b
nodes.text: They leverage proprietary AI 7 internally, and many sell new business services based on that capability, the AI capability as a service, or both to other enterprises.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Ping An Insurance, one of the largest financial services enterprises in China, d
nodes.text: Ping An Insurance, one of the largest financial services enterprises in China, debuted an AI banking platform in 2019 8 and is now leveraging it.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: By the end of 2022, 49 percent of the enterprise’s product sales were made by AI
nodes.text: By the end of 2022, 49 percent of the enterprise’s product sales were made by AI service representatives, and 82 percent of service interactions were handled by AI representatives (i.e., AI software agents 9 ). 10 Also by the end of that year, AI representatives had saved RMB 600 million in labor costs 11 for the enterprise, which additionally provided AI banking as a service to thirty other Chinese banks. 12 In 2023, Ping An Group reported increased sales, reduced costs, improved efficiency, and reduced risks from using generative AI applications. 13 In our 2024 interviews as part of this research, an emerging theme was that interviewees expected the most value from AI will be created from combining people and platforms with a combination of four types of AI: analytical, generative, agentic and robotic.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: Your AI Maturity Stage Today
nodes.text: Your AI Maturity Stage Today
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: We recommend that you bring a team together to talk about which of the four stag
nodes.text: We recommend that you bring a team together to talk about which of the four stages your enterprise is in today, and your aspirations and time frames regarding your enterprise’s use of AI. Then, discuss which enterprise capabilities and skill sets need more work.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: No matter where you are in the MIT CISR Enterprise AI Maturity Model, be bold.
nodes.text: No matter where you are in the MIT CISR Enterprise AI Maturity Model, be bold.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: For example, Piyush Gupta, the CEO of DBS Bank, set a KPI of conducting one thou
nodes.text: For example, Piyush Gupta, the CEO of DBS Bank, set a KPI of conducting one thousand experiments per year. 14 DBS is building on that experimental mindset, coupled with a years-long investment in a data foundation, to become an AI-fueled bank, with AI pervasive across the enterprise.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: From 2022 to 2024, the bank doubled the economic impact achieved from AI from S$
nodes.text: From 2022 to 2024, the bank doubled the economic impact achieved from AI from S$150 million to S$370 million.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: DBS has delivered over three hundred fifty AI use cases to date, and expects the
nodes.text: DBS has delivered over three hundred fifty AI use cases to date, and expects the economic impact of these to exceed S$1B in 2025. 15 What are your tangible goals for AI, and how will you get there?
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: 1 The data underlying the MIT CISR Enterprise AI Maturity Model comes from the M
nodes.text: 1 The data underlying the MIT CISR Enterprise AI Maturity Model comes from the MIT CISR 2022 Future Ready Survey (N=721). We divided respondents into four stages of AI Capability using a measure of Total AI Effectiveness, the equally weighted combination of three measures: effectiveness of AI to (i) improve operations, (ii) improve customer experience, and (iii) support and develop the ecosystem. On a 0–100% scale of Total AI Effectiveness, Stage 1 AI Capability=0–49%, Stage 2=50–74%, Stage 3=75–99%, and Stage 4=100%. We supplemented the model with sixteen interviews we conducted in August to November 2024 with senior executives at nine enterprises.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: 6 Foundation models are a type of AI technology trained on vast amounts data tha
nodes.text: 6 Foundation models are a type of AI technology trained on vast amounts data that can be adapted to perform a wide variety of tasks. The models can be fine-tuned for specific domains or tasks by incorporating smaller, specialized, curated datasets into the training set or by using software plug-ins or add-ons. Large language models (LLMs) are general-purpose foundation models. See Ben Lutkevich, “Foundation models explained: Everything you need to know,” TechTarget , April 17, 2024, https://www.techtarget.com/whatis/feature/Foundation-models-explained-Everything-you-need-to-know . Small language models (SLMs) are typically trained on smaller data sets tailored to specific industries, tasks, or domains and contain far fewer parameters. SLMs struggle when it comes to general knowledge and understanding but usually have less bias. See “LLMs vs SLMs,” The Red Hat Blog , October 1, 2024, https://www.redhat.com/en/topics/ai/llm-vs-slm .
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: 7 We define proprietary AI as the AI infrastructure developed in-house. For inst
nodes.text: 7 We define proprietary AI as the AI infrastructure developed in-house. For instance, an AI platform developed in-house that integrates purchased large language models and enterprise assets such as APIs, data, and business processes is proprietary AI.
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: 8 “AI Banker: Reshaping Retail Banking for the Digital Age,” Ping An Group, Augu
nodes.text: 8 “AI Banker: Reshaping Retail Banking for the Digital Age,” Ping An Group, August 26, 2019, https://group.pingan.com/media/perspectives/AI-Banker-Reshaping-Retail-Banking-for-the-Digital-Age.html .
nodes.author: MIT CISR

nodes.label: 9 An AI or intelligent agent is a type of AI software that can understand and re
nodes.text: 9 An AI or intelligent agent is a type of AI software that can understand and respond to its environment without human intervention. A smart thermostat is an example of an AI agent, as is a chatbot that can answer questions, or a piece of software that can make decisions. AI agents can adapt based on experience. See Cameron Hashemi-Pour, “What is an intelligent agent?,” TechTarget , https://www.techtarget.com/searchenterpriseai/definition/agent-intelligent-agent , accessed December 2, 2024.

nodes.label: Title: The PBSAI Governance Ecosystem: A Multi-Agent AI Reference Architecture f
nodes.text: Title: The PBSAI Governance Ecosystem: A Multi-Agent AI Reference Architecture for Securing Enterprise AI Estates
nodes.author: arXiv.org

nodes.label: Abstract: Enterprises are rapidly deploying large language models, retrieval aug
nodes.text: Abstract: Enterprises are rapidly deploying large language models, retrieval augmented generation pipelines, and tool using agents into production, often on shared high performance computing clusters and cloud accelerator platforms that also support defensive analytics. These systems increasingly function not as isolated models but as AI estates: socio technical systems spanning models, agents, data pipelines, security tooling, human workflows, and hyperscale infrastructure. Existing governance and security frameworks, including the NIST AI Risk Management Framework and systems security engineering guidance, articulate principles and risk functions but do not provide implementable architectures for multi agent, AI enabled cyber defense. This paper introduces the Practitioners Blueprint for Secure AI (PBSAI) Governance Ecosystem, a multi agent reference architecture for securing enterprise and hyperscale AI estates. PBSAI organizes responsibilities into a twelve domain taxonomy and defines bounded agent families that mediate between tools and policy through shared context envelopes and structured output contracts. The architecture assumes baseline enterprise security capabilities and encodes key systems security techniques, including analytic monitoring, coordinated defense, and adaptive response. A lightweight formal model of agents, context envelopes, and ecosystem level invariants clarifies the traceability, provenance, and human in the loop guarantees enforced across domains. We demonstrate alignment with NIST AI RMF functions and illustrate application in enterprise SOC and hyperscale defensive environments. PBSAI is proposed as a structured, evidence centric foundation for open ecosystem development and future empirical validation.

nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The enterprise IT overhaul: Architecting your stack for the agentic AI era
nodes.text: The enterprise IT overhaul: Architecting your stack for the agentic AI era
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Forget tweaking your tech stack — if you’re not rebuilding for agentic AI, you’r
nodes.text: Forget tweaking your tech stack — if you’re not rebuilding for agentic AI, you’re already falling behind the next wave of enterprise disruption.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: For the CIO, the conversation has officially moved past the large language model
nodes.text: For the CIO, the conversation has officially moved past the large language model (LLM).
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The next critical chapter is agentic AI — autonomous systems capable of reasonin
nodes.text: The next critical chapter is agentic AI — autonomous systems capable of reasoning, planning and executing multi-step tasks across your enterprise.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: These are not chatbots; they are digital teammates, and integrating them require
nodes.text: These are not chatbots; they are digital teammates, and integrating them requires more than a simple API key.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: It demands a fundamental overhaul of your core IT architecture.
nodes.text: It demands a fundamental overhaul of your core IT architecture.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The shift from monolithic applications to service-oriented architectures took de
nodes.text: The shift from monolithic applications to service-oriented architectures took decades.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The transition to the agentic era must happen in years.
nodes.text: The transition to the agentic era must happen in years.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Your role as CIO is to engineer the foundation — a resilient, adaptive and scala
nodes.text: Your role as CIO is to engineer the foundation — a resilient, adaptive and scalable stack — that turns this generational technology into a competitive advantage.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Here are the three foundational considerations for architecting your Enterprise
nodes.text: Here are the three foundational considerations for architecting your Enterprise IT for the age of autonomous agents.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: 1. The integration challenge: From simple API calls to a central nervous system
nodes.text: 1. The integration challenge: From simple API calls to a central nervous system
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The traditional approach to integrating new tools — a simple REST API call to a
nodes.text: The traditional approach to integrating new tools — a simple REST API call to a monolithic system like your ERP or CRM — is a bottleneck for autonomous agents.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Agents operate in dynamic, continuous loops of observation, reasoning and action
nodes.text: Agents operate in dynamic, continuous loops of observation, reasoning and action.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: They need to react to events in real-time and trigger actions across disparate s
nodes.text: They need to react to events in real-time and trigger actions across disparate systems simultaneously.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: This is the key architectural shift: moving from synchronous, tightly coupled co
nodes.text: This is the key architectural shift: moving from synchronous, tightly coupled connections to an event-driven architecture (EDA).
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The problem with APIs: Direct API calls create a fragile, point-to-point “spaghe
nodes.text: The problem with APIs: Direct API calls create a fragile, point-to-point “spaghetti” of connections. An agent attempting a complex, cross-system workflow (e.g., identify a high-value customer churn risk in CRM, check their payment status in ERP and automatically trigger a personalized offer in the Marketing cloud ) requires the agent to manage all states and dependencies.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The EDA solution: An event-driven architecture, typically built on a message bro
nodes.text: The EDA solution: An event-driven architecture, typically built on a message broker like Kafka, decouples agents from one another and from your core systems. An agent simply publishes an event ( customer_risk_flagged ) to a topic. All subscribing agents — finance, marketing and service — can react instantly, in parallel and without knowing anything about the publishing agent. This ensures loose coupling, immense scalability and resilience. As Sean Falconer , an AI strategist, puts it, an event-driven design is essential for building a scalable foundation.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Key takeaway for the CIO: Your data streaming and messaging backbone is now your
nodes.text: Key takeaway for the CIO: Your data streaming and messaging backbone is now your primary agent orchestration layer. Invest in upgrading this layer to handle the semantic complexity and volume of agent-to-agent communication.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: 2. The new talent frontier: Orchestrating the digital workforce
nodes.text: 2. The new talent frontier: Orchestrating the digital workforce
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The new architecture demands new expertise. The IT team of the agentic era requi
nodes.text: The new architecture demands new expertise. The IT team of the agentic era requires skillsets that bridge machine learning, systems architecture and traditional process engineering.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: We are seeing the emergence of highly specialized roles:
nodes.text: We are seeing the emergence of highly specialized roles:
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Agent orchestrators: These individuals sit at the intersection of IT and the bus
nodes.text: Agent orchestrators: These individuals sit at the intersection of IT and the business. They design the goals, guardrails and composition of multi-agent systems. They are responsible for routing tasks, verifying results and designing safe escalation paths to human colleagues. The human-centric role is shifting from execution to oversight.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: MLOps engineers specializing in multi-agent systems: Standard MLOps is about dep
nodes.text: MLOps engineers specializing in multi-agent systems: Standard MLOps is about deploying and monitoring a single model. Agentic MLOps is about managing the collective behavior, performance and security of an interconnected network of autonomous agents. This includes version control for agent capabilities and continuous testing for emergent behavior — when a group of agents acts in an unintended or unpredictable way.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Advanced prompt & specification engineers: This is prompt engineering evolved. I
nodes.text: Advanced prompt & specification engineers: This is prompt engineering evolved. It’s about creating precise specification literacy — writing unambiguous task definitions and data contracts that prevent agents from hallucinating or taking unauthorized actions.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: This transition highlights a critical truth: People remain the orchestrators, se
nodes.text: This transition highlights a critical truth: People remain the orchestrators, setting the direction and providing governance.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Organizations must prioritize building “specification literacy” and “verificatio
nodes.text: Organizations must prioritize building “specification literacy” and “verification discipline” to ensure agents remain accountable.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Industry analysis suggests, the focus must be on faster, better outcomes, withou
nodes.text: Industry analysis suggests, the focus must be on faster, better outcomes, without sacrificing accountability.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Key takeaway for the CIO: Stop hiring for “genAI experts.” Start developing “age
nodes.text: Key takeaway for the CIO: Stop hiring for “genAI experts.” Start developing “agent orchestrators” and investing in upskilling your MLOps team on the specific complexities of multi-agent coordination.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: 3. The build vs. buy strategy: Where to differentiate
nodes.text: 3. The build vs. buy strategy: Where to differentiate
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: The choice between leveraging an off-the-shelf cloud vendor copilot tool and bui
nodes.text: The choice between leveraging an off-the-shelf cloud vendor copilot tool and building a custom, fine-tuned agent is arguably the most critical strategic decision you will face. It’s a decision that must align IT spending directly with your unique business value.

nodes.label: When to buy (the utility): Leverage off-the-shelf agents — like those offered by
nodes.text: When to buy (the utility): Leverage off-the-shelf agents — like those offered by your cloud provider or ERP/CRM vendor — for system-specific, non-differentiating tasks. These agents offer speed, native integration and low upfront costs for use cases like generating summaries within a single application or managing simple service tickets.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: When to build (the weapon): Reserve your expensive internal talent for building
nodes.text: When to build (the weapon): Reserve your expensive internal talent for building agents that create unique competitive advantages. If an agent needs to pull data from your legacy manufacturing system, combine it with a real-time market feed and autonomously execute a proprietary trading strategy, you must build it. Custom builds offer the control and deep, cross-system integration required to deliver true business differentiation.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Ultimately, the smartest path is often a hybrid approach, supported by a unified
nodes.text: Ultimately, the smartest path is often a hybrid approach, supported by a unified agent platform.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: You buy the foundational infrastructure and non-differentiating agents, but you
nodes.text: You buy the foundational infrastructure and non-differentiating agents, but you build the proprietary logic and specialized agents that connect your unique business processes.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: According to early adopters and industry leaders, this blended approach is key.
nodes.text: According to early adopters and industry leaders, this blended approach is key.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Start with simple ‘buy’ options and plan for a ‘build’ component when aiming for
nodes.text: Start with simple ‘buy’ options and plan for a ‘build’ component when aiming for meaningful business transformation.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Key takeaway for the CIO: Treat the agentic stack like your application portfoli
nodes.text: Key takeaway for the CIO: Treat the agentic stack like your application portfolio. Buy the commodities; build your core competitive weapons. Ensure your architecture is modular enough to accommodate both vendor-locked and custom agents on a common EDA backbone.

nodes.label: Title: Enterprise Architecture as a Dynamic Capability for Scalable and Sustaina
nodes.text: Title: Enterprise Architecture as a Dynamic Capability for Scalable and Sustainable Generative AI adoption: Bridging Innovation and Governance in Large Organisations
nodes.author: arXiv.org

nodes.label: Abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence is a powerful new technology with t
nodes.text: Abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence is a powerful new technology with the potential to boost innovation and reshape governance in many industries. Nevertheless, organisations face major challenges in scaling GenAI, including technology complexity, governance gaps and resource misalignments. This study explores how Enterprise Architecture Management can meet the complex requirements of GenAI adoption within large enterprises. Based on a systematic literature review and the qualitative analysis of 16 semi-structured interviews with experts, it examines the relationships between EAM, dynamic capabilities and GenAI adoption. The review identified key limitations in existing EA frameworks, particularly their inability to fully address the unique requirements of GenAI. The interviews, analysed using the Gioia methodology, revealed critical enablers and barriers to GenAI adoption across industries. The findings indicate that EAM, when theorised as sensing, seizing and transforming dynamic capabilities, can enhance GenAI adoption by improving strategic alignment, governance frameworks and organisational agility. However, the study also highlights the need to tailor EA frameworks to GenAI-specific challenges, including low data governance maturity and the balance between innovation and compliance. Several conceptual frameworks are proposed to guide EA leaders in aligning GenAI maturity with organisational readiness. The work contributes to academic understanding and industry practice by clarifying the role of EA in bridging innovation and governance in disruptive technology environments.

nodes.label: Enterprise Architecture (EA) is the function that turns high‑level principles in
nodes.text: Enterprise Architecture (EA) is the function that turns high‑level principles into deployable designs , enforceable controls , and auditable evidence —so your organization can innovate with AI without losing control of its institutional intelligence.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: ( Research and conference summaries have highlighted EA’s evolving role as the e
nodes.text: ( Research and conference summaries have highlighted EA’s evolving role as the enterprise’s “decision architecture,” integrating strategy, data, platforms, risks, and controls.)
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: This article shows how to operationalize AI sovereignty through governance , usi
nodes.text: This article shows how to operationalize AI sovereignty through governance , using open frameworks (NIST AI RMF and ISO/IEC 42001) and Bizzdesign Alfabet (now part of Bizzdesign’s Transformation Suite) to model ownership, boundaries, lifecycle controls, and traceability—while staying adaptable to Malaysia’s guidance today and its emerging regulatory framework.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: What AI Sovereignty Means—Practically
nodes.text: What AI Sovereignty Means—Practically
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: AI sovereignty is your organization’s ability to decide, design, and demonstrate
nodes.text: AI sovereignty is your organization’s ability to decide, design, and demonstrate where and how sensitive data and model intelligence are stored, processed, accessed, and reused—by you, not by external platforms.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: In practice, it answers: Where is our data?
nodes.text: In practice, it answers: Where is our data?
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Who can use it, for what purpose, with which keys, and under whose jurisdiction?
nodes.text: Who can use it, for what purpose, with which keys, and under whose jurisdiction?
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: (Those answers must be provable in audits and resilient to regulatory change.)
nodes.text: (Those answers must be provable in audits and resilient to regulatory change.)

nodes.text: Anchor Governance in Open Frameworks (Not Proprietary Labels)

nodes.label: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) offers free, risk‑based guidance
nodes.text: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) offers free, risk‑based guidance across four functions— GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE —with a companion Playbook and Generative AI profile to tailor controls to context.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: ISO/IEC 42001 (AI Management Systems) provides a certifiable management‑system f
nodes.text: ISO/IEC 42001 (AI Management Systems) provides a certifiable management‑system framework for policies, roles, risk assessment, data management, lifecycle oversight, and continual improvement across AI systems.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: In Malaysia, SIRIM has launched ISO/IEC 42001 certification pathways, giving ent
nodes.text: In Malaysia, SIRIM has launched ISO/IEC 42001 certification pathways, giving enterprises a locally recognized route to demonstrate responsible AI management.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Why use both: NIST helps you do risk management; ISO/IEC 42001 helps you prove y
nodes.text: Why use both: NIST helps you do risk management; ISO/IEC 42001 helps you prove you do it systematically—a combination that aligns well with regulators and scales across use cases.

nodes.label: Malaysia: Govern Now, Adapt for What’s Coming
nodes.text: Malaysia: Govern Now, Adapt for What’s Coming
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Malaysia’s National Guidelines on AI Governance & Ethics (AIGE) —launched by MOS
nodes.text: Malaysia’s National Guidelines on AI Governance & Ethics (AIGE) —launched by MOSTI in September 2024 —provide voluntary national guidance with principles like fairness, safety, privacy, transparency, accountability, inclusivity, and human benefit, and role‑specific recommendations for users, policymakers, and developers.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: At the same time, Malaysia is developing a National AI Regulatory Framework and
nodes.text: At the same time, Malaysia is developing a National AI Regulatory Framework and has signaled the possibility of legislation by 2H 2026 , so organizations should build adaptable governance that can meet today’s voluntary guidance yet scale to future legal obligations.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: The AI Technology Action Plan 2026–2030 aims to reinforce ethical AI governance
nodes.text: The AI Technology Action Plan 2026–2030 aims to reinforce ethical AI governance and strengthen cross‑sector collaboration—another reason to favor open, evidence‑based frameworks (NIST + ISO/IEC 42001) that can absorb new requirements without re‑architecting your control model.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Pragmatic tip for Malaysia: If you want assurance while the law is finalized, pu
nodes.text: Pragmatic tip for Malaysia: If you want assurance while the law is finalized, pursue ISO/IEC 42001 certification via SIRIM to build audit muscle now, and map NIST AI RMF controls to your EA repository so evidence is searchable on demand.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Classification & Tagging Everywhere – Adopt a single scheme and propagate via yo
nodes.text: Classification & Tagging Everywhere – Adopt a single scheme and propagate via your catalog and EA repository; enforce access and reuse by classification. (Modern governance leaders emphasize metadata‑driven control.)
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Column‑Level Lineage & Purpose Binding – Bind data to intended purpose (analytic
nodes.text: Column‑Level Lineage & Purpose Binding – Bind data to intended purpose (analytics vs training vs inference) and deny reuse outside purpose.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Residency Patterns & Landing Zones – Standardize placements (e.g., in‑region pub
nodes.text: Residency Patterns & Landing Zones – Standardize placements (e.g., in‑region public cloud with boundary controls , hybrid/on‑prem for high‑control scenarios, SaaS with no training/retention and in‑region processing). Cloud providers now publish sovereignty controls, which you should codify as policy‑as‑code and architecture standards .
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Access & Key Management – Enforce least privilege via centralized identity; use
nodes.text: Access & Key Management – Enforce least privilege via centralized identity; use customer‑managed, HSM‑backed keys ; log all key operations and access as evidence.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Model I/O Guardrails – Treat prompts, embeddings, and outputs as governed data;
nodes.text: Model I/O Guardrails – Treat prompts, embeddings, and outputs as governed data; apply DLP/PII policies and log flows; align to NIST risk actions and AIGE privacy/accountability principles.
nodes.author: MR Consultancy Services

nodes.label: Evidence by Design – Capture machine‑readable evidence (who, what, where, which
nodes.text: Evidence by Design – Capture machine‑readable evidence (who, what, where, which keys/tests) to satisfy ISO/IEC 42001 audits and NIST outcomes.

nodes.label: AI sovereignty isn’t a single product or policy—it’s an EA‑led operating model g

nodes.text: While emerging technologies continue to reshape all industries, enterprises are at the forefront of advanced solutions to improve operational efficiency, customer experience and competitiveness in the global market.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In this dynamic context, the convergence of traditional practices with advanced
nodes.text: In this dynamic context, the convergence of traditional practices with advanced technologies is more than just an option: it is a necessity to stay resilient.

nodes.text: This article has been written by Emmanuel Larroze, Senior Manager Data & AI, and Ahmed Iyed Hadj Khalifa, Senior Consultant Data & AI.

nodes.label: The arrival of Generative AI (GenAI) will redefine the operational paradigms of
nodes.text: The arrival of Generative AI (GenAI) will redefine the operational paradigms of the industry, particularly when associated within a structured framework like The Open Group Architecture Framework (TOGAF).
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This alignment of GenAI added value with the systemic approach addressed by TOGA
nodes.text: This alignment of GenAI added value with the systemic approach addressed by TOGAF, presents key opportunities for innovation and transformation, facing enterprises to unprecedented ideas for their IT architectures.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: As the cutting-edge technology of artificial intelligence that goes beyond tradi
nodes.text: As the cutting-edge technology of artificial intelligence that goes beyond traditional machine learning models, how can GenAI revolutionize business strategy?

nodes.text: Are there any potential risks related to GenAI’s adoption to consider?

nodes.label: This article aims to provide train of thoughts to these questions by providing a
nodes.text: This article aims to provide train of thoughts to these questions by providing a detailed exploration of how this harmony between GenAI and TOGAF methodology can reshape the business and IT landscapes.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: The important role of GenAI in shaping business strategy
nodes.text: The important role of GenAI in shaping business strategy
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI, with its capacity to create new content and solutions, can play a crucial
nodes.text: GenAI, with its capacity to create new content and solutions, can play a crucial role in shaping business strategy, notably by enhancing customer experience and innovating in product design.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It is true that Conventional AI is already improving the customer experience by
nodes.text: It is true that Conventional AI is already improving the customer experience by promoting personalization and innovation.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: However, GenAI's ability to analyze large data sets allows it to deliver highly
nodes.text: However, GenAI's ability to analyze large data sets allows it to deliver highly customized interactions that match individual preferences, leading to enhance customer journey.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This personalized content can include custom product recommendations, tailored n
nodes.text: This personalized content can include custom product recommendations, tailored news feeds, virtual shopping assistants, etc. Besides, advanced natural language processing allows to add a human empathy shade to user interactions.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Those specificities make GenAI a lever for improving or even redesigning the cus
nodes.text: Those specificities make GenAI a lever for improving or even redesigning the customer experience.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Moreover, GenAI is considered as a product design innovation enabler thanks to i
nodes.text: Moreover, GenAI is considered as a product design innovation enabler thanks to its capacity to generate inventive new concepts, pushing the boundaries of creativity.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Through database analysis and iterative processes, it optimizes designs and impr
nodes.text: Through database analysis and iterative processes, it optimizes designs and improves efficiency and functionality.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In addition, thanks to GenAI, prototyping is increasingly accelerated, which smo
nodes.text: In addition, thanks to GenAI, prototyping is increasingly accelerated, which smooths the transition from concept to prototype to evaluate the designed model usability rapidly and better.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI also promotes user-driven innovation which allows tailors designs to indiv
nodes.text: GenAI also promotes user-driven innovation which allows tailors designs to individual preferences. Collaborative and iterative, GenAI effectively impacts the product development cycle from its ideation phase by introducing continuous innovation, adaptability, and diversity.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Leveraging GenAI in data architecture
nodes.text: Leveraging GenAI in data architecture
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Data modeling, master data management (DMD) and data visualization are some of t
nodes.text: Data modeling, master data management (DMD) and data visualization are some of the aspects of the data architecture layer that GenAI can affect in a substantial way.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: For a predefined business context, GenAI provides data models based on set of pa
nodes.text: For a predefined business context, GenAI provides data models based on set of patterns analysis within high volume of pre-trained datasets.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It allows to generate data objects, their attributes as well as relationships be
nodes.text: It allows to generate data objects, their attributes as well as relationships between them.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This capacity accelerates the data modeling workflows, reduces the manual effort
nodes.text: This capacity accelerates the data modeling workflows, reduces the manual effort traditionally required, and enhances the alignment with the business specifications.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI allows also to streamline the complex process of master data management by
nodes.text: GenAI allows also to streamline the complex process of master data management by automating the creation of master data models.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It also has the capacity to suggest strategies to maintain high data quality lev
nodes.text: It also has the capacity to suggest strategies to maintain high data quality level on several dimensions, e.g., accuracy, completeness, and consistency.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: By automating these tasks, GenAI improves the efficiency of identifying and mana
nodes.text: By automating these tasks, GenAI improves the efficiency of identifying and managing master data, contributing to a unified and accurate representation of key business objects.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Moreover, GenAI fortifies data security and privacy through various mechanisms.
nodes.text: Moreover, GenAI fortifies data security and privacy through various mechanisms. It provides robust encryption algorithms allowing to protect confidential data from unauthorized access.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Additionally, one of the most common uses of data is the preparation of visualiz
nodes.text: Additionally, one of the most common uses of data is the preparation of visualizations and insights which enhances data-driven decision-making.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In this context, GenAI can revolutionize ways to visualize data by offering the
nodes.text: In this context, GenAI can revolutionize ways to visualize data by offering the capability to generate on-demand insightful representations even from complex datasets.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: User simply needs to write the description of the desired visualization and GenA
nodes.text: User simply needs to write the description of the desired visualization and GenAI tool will generate it for him immediately.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It also adapts to various data structures and dynamically adjusts visualization
nodes.text: It also adapts to various data structures and dynamically adjusts visualization styles (heatmaps, bar charts, diagrams, etc.) based on content, ensuring optimal representation.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI integration in application architecture
nodes.text: GenAI integration in application architecture
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI transforms application architecture through various ways, notably by provi
nodes.text: GenAI transforms application architecture through various ways, notably by providing ready-to-use key accelerators and architectural artifacts, which enables the creation of robust applications and allow architects to concentrate efforts mainly on strategic aspects.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This dynamic approach not only enhances efficiency but also ensures the generate
nodes.text: This dynamic approach not only enhances efficiency but also ensures the generated patterns alignment with functional specifications.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Furthermore, GenAI optimizes API integration by suggesting optimal strategies al
nodes.text: Furthermore, GenAI optimizes API integration by suggesting optimal strategies aligned with business requirements.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It provides secure communication approaches which allows to protect data during
nodes.text: It provides secure communication approaches which allows to protect data during integration.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: By generating various test cases, GenAI also ensures the integrated system’s rob
nodes.text: By generating various test cases, GenAI also ensures the integrated system’s robustness.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It also facilitates agile API design to cover future potential requirements.
nodes.text: It also facilitates agile API design to cover future potential requirements.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Furthermore, it can provide an API comprehensive documentation including latest
nodes.text: Furthermore, it can provide an API comprehensive documentation including latest updates.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI’s strengths for technology architecture
nodes.text: GenAI’s strengths for technology architecture
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI can modernize technology architecture by facilitating optimal best-of-bree
nodes.text: GenAI can modernize technology architecture by facilitating optimal best-of-breed solutions selection based on diverse criteria deep analyses.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It offers tailored guidance aligned with business requirements as well as key ca
nodes.text: It offers tailored guidance aligned with business requirements as well as key capabilities such as scalability, resilience, and reversibility.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This dynamic capacity adapts to evolving IT landscapes and business requirements
nodes.text: This dynamic capacity adapts to evolving IT landscapes and business requirements, continuously refining recommendations based on the changing need and technological state-of-art.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Moreover, GenAI accelerates homemade solutions development by generating code sn
nodes.text: Moreover, GenAI accelerates homemade solutions development by generating code snippets.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It produces error-free functions and classes code segments written in any progra
nodes.text: It produces error-free functions and classes code segments written in any programming language, which improves efficiency and reduces manual coding efforts.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This capacity improves developers' productivity and allow teams to focus more on
nodes.text: This capacity improves developers' productivity and allow teams to focus more on high-level design.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It also ensures that generated code is aligned with coding standards related to
nodes.text: It also ensures that generated code is aligned with coding standards related to maintainability, readability, collaboration, and consistency.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Risks and Considerations
nodes.text: Risks and Considerations
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI has amazing advantages, but it also has some major challenges.
nodes.text: GenAI has amazing advantages, but it also has some major challenges.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: One of them is sustainability issues, which are increasingly important in techno
nodes.text: One of them is sustainability issues, which are increasingly important in technology adoption.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In fact, many enterprises take this criterion into account in their technology a
nodes.text: In fact, many enterprises take this criterion into account in their technology architecture principles and assess it when they select a new solution to enhance their IT landscape.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI, despite being potent and innovative, poses significant environnemental qu
nodes.text: GenAI, despite being potent and innovative, poses significant environnemental questions. Training big models requires high compute power, which consumes a lot of energy and emits huge amounts of carbon, increasing the threat to our environment.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Therefore, it is essential to tackle those issues by developing energy-efficient
nodes.text: Therefore, it is essential to tackle those issues by developing energy-efficient calculations/algorithms that consume less energy or encouraging data centers to use green energy sources, to reduce impact of GenAI on on the environment.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Moreover, GenAI also poses significant challenges for change management.
nodes.text: Moreover, GenAI also poses significant challenges for change management.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Indeed, employees who are accustomed to conventional work methods might need ass
nodes.text: Indeed, employees who are accustomed to conventional work methods might need assistance, which means reskilling and training programs to help them to adapt themselves to GenAI usages.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Also, using GenAI with existing systems requires a possible organizational resha
nodes.text: Also, using GenAI with existing systems requires a possible organizational reshaping as well as a revision of roles and responsibilities.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Therefore, GenAI adoption requires a strategic approach emphasizing the GenAI’s
nodes.text: Therefore, GenAI adoption requires a strategic approach emphasizing the GenAI’s strengths and its capacity to support decision-making as well as the ritual tasks execution.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In conclusion, the combination of GenAI capabilities and TOGAF architecture laye
nodes.text: In conclusion, the combination of GenAI capabilities and TOGAF architecture layers represents a transformative journey for any industry.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Understanding GenAI's capacities and implementing them are a real enabler allowi
nodes.text: Understanding GenAI's capacities and implementing them are a real enabler allowing enterprises to navigate new possibilities, enhance customer experiences and optimize processes and operations.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI: pioneering creative intelligence
nodes.text: GenAI: pioneering creative intelligence
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI, often considered as the cutting-edge technology of artificial intelligenc
nodes.text: GenAI, often considered as the cutting-edge technology of artificial intelligence, goes beyond traditional machine learning models.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: As its name indicates, it has the specificity of generating new content, whether
nodes.text: As its name indicates, it has the specificity of generating new content, whether it is text, images, music, etc. This creativity makes it a great lever for all industries where problem solving and innovation are crucial.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI is also seen as an enabler that fosters a collaborative environment where
nodes.text: GenAI is also seen as an enabler that fosters a collaborative environment where human creativity combines with machine intelligence, which allows to explore alternatives previously considered unexplored.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Thus, understanding the pioneering creative intelligence of GenAI is essential t
nodes.text: Thus, understanding the pioneering creative intelligence of GenAI is essential to unlock its full capabilities.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Understanding TOGAF architecture layers: Harmonizing technology with business vi
nodes.text: Understanding TOGAF architecture layers: Harmonizing technology with business vision
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: On the other hand, the TOGAF framework is an enterprise architecture methodology
nodes.text: On the other hand, the TOGAF framework is an enterprise architecture methodology widely adopted in different sectors.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It plays a central role in aligning technology solutions with business objective
nodes.text: It plays a central role in aligning technology solutions with business objectives, which is important for organizations that aim to leverage technology to increase their profits, improve their quality of service and reduce costs.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It provides a structured and holistic framework for designing and evaluating ent
nodes.text: It provides a structured and holistic framework for designing and evaluating enterprise architectures, based on a multi-layered model including business, data, application, and technology architectures.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: First, the business architecture focuses on describing the structure and interac
nodes.text: First, the business architecture focuses on describing the structure and interaction between strategy, processes, and business needs. It highlights customer experience objectives to guarantee that customer-centric vision is embedded in the overall business strategy. It plays a governor role in the enterprise architecture and ensures that each technology initiative is a business enabler.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Second, the data architecture layer addresses the organization and structuring o
nodes.text: Second, the data architecture layer addresses the organization and structuring of data within the enterprise. It ensures that data is managed not only efficiently, but also strategically and aligned with business needs, which allows to organizations to gain meaningful insights, support decision-making processes, and help achieve business goals.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: As we move through the layers, the application architecture highlights the softw
nodes.text: As we move through the layers, the application architecture highlights the software design.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: At this level, the goal is to ensure that applications align seamlessly with bus
nodes.text: At this level, the goal is to ensure that applications align seamlessly with business processes.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This architecture layer aims to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of busi
nodes.text: This architecture layer aims to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of business processes through well-designed applications, thereby contributing to the achievement of predefined strategic objectives.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Finally, the technology architecture layer addresses the infrastructure supporti
nodes.text: Finally, the technology architecture layer addresses the infrastructure supporting applications.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Its role is to ensure that the homemade or best-of-breed chosen technological so
nodes.text: Its role is to ensure that the homemade or best-of-breed chosen technological solutions not only meet the current company’s needs, but are also scalable, reversible, sustainable, and adaptable to future requirements.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Top 3 Strategic Priorities for Heads of Enterprise Architect
nodes.author: Gartner

nodes.label: Agentic AI — the new frontier in GenAI: An executive playboo
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In an era where speed, efficiency, and customer centricity dictate market leader
nodes.text: In an era where speed, efficiency, and customer centricity dictate market leadership, organisations need to harness every tool at their disposal.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Over the past couple of years, artificial intelligence (AI) has exploded onto th
nodes.text: Over the past couple of years, artificial intelligence (AI) has exploded onto the world stage, with companies and individuals across the globe rapidly adopting the technology.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: The GCC is playing a lead role in the space, with business leaders in the region
nodes.text: The GCC is playing a lead role in the space, with business leaders in the region exploring ways of integrating this rapidly developing technology into their operations.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Generative AI (GenAI) is being recognised as a game-changer for innovation in th
nodes.text: Generative AI (GenAI) is being recognised as a game-changer for innovation in the region, empowering enterprises by automating routine tasks, enhancing customer experiences and assisting in critical decision- making processes.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Insights from our 27th Annual CEO Survey: Middle East findings have shown that 7
nodes.text: Insights from our 27th Annual CEO Survey: Middle East findings have shown that 73% of CEOs in the Middle East believe GenAI will significantly change the way their company creates, delivers and captures value over the next three years1.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: GenAI is poised to make a significant economic impact, with estimates indicating
nodes.text: GenAI is poised to make a significant economic impact, with estimates indicating that it could contribute between $2.6 trillion and $4.4 trillion annually to global GDP across various industries by 2030.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In specific sectors, such as energy, investments in GenAI are expected to triple
nodes.text: In specific sectors, such as energy, investments in GenAI are expected to triple, from $40 billion in 2023 to over $140 billion by the end of the decade.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This surge in investment reflects the transformative potential of GenAI, particu
nodes.text: This surge in investment reflects the transformative potential of GenAI, particularly in enhancing productivity, streamlining business processes, and reshaping value chains across industries2.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Against this backdrop, multimodal GenAI agentic frameworks has emerged as transf
nodes.text: Against this backdrop, multimodal GenAI agentic frameworks has emerged as transformative catalysts, enabling businesses to accelerate process automation at an unprecedented scale.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This technology involves multiple AI agents working together, each specialising
nodes.text: This technology involves multiple AI agents working together, each specialising in different tasks or data types, to solve complex problems and automate processes.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: By collaborating and constantly learning, these agents enhance decision- making,
nodes.text: By collaborating and constantly learning, these agents enhance decision- making, optimise processes, and drive innovation.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It combines range of advanced AI techniques to process diverse data types and au
nodes.text: It combines range of advanced AI techniques to process diverse data types and automate complex tasks.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: The central question isn’t whether to adopt this technology, but how swiftly org
nodes.text: The central question isn’t whether to adopt this technology, but how swiftly organisations can integrate it to stay ahead of the competition.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This executive playbook explores how organisations can leverage this technology
nodes.text: This executive playbook explores how organisations can leverage this technology to boost operational efficiency, enhance customer experience, and drive revenue growth.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: It provides real-world success stories spanning industry sectors and organisatio
nodes.text: It provides real-world success stories spanning industry sectors and organisational functions, strategic insights, tactical blueprints, and best practices to guide your journey into this revolutionary landscape.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Key insights
nodes.text: Key insights
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Agentic AI, differentiated by its advanced human -like reasoning and interaction
nodes.text: Agentic AI, differentiated by its advanced human -like reasoning and interaction capabilities, is transforming the manufacturing, healthcare, finance, retail, transportation, and energy sectors, among others.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Organisations’ AI strategies should leverage multimodal GenAI capabilities while
nodes.text: Organisations’ AI strategies should leverage multimodal GenAI capabilities while ensuring ethical AI safeguards to drive autonomous process re-engineering and enhanced decision-making across all lines of business.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Integrated effectively, agentic AI can enhance efficiency, lower costs, improve
nodes.text: Integrated effectively, agentic AI can enhance efficiency, lower costs, improve customer experience, and drive revenue growth.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Agentic AI generally refers to AI systems that possess the capacity to make auto
