nodes.label: Commercial solutions Open-source solutions LangGraph28 ●Target audience: Startup
nodes.text: Commercial solutions Open-source solutions LangGraph28 ●Target audience: Startups and established enterprises ●Support: Offers robust customer support and professional services ●Integration: Seamlessly integrates with existing enterprise systems ●Customisation: High level of customisation and control over workflows ●Features: Advanced features like statefulness (having a perfect memory or knowledge of previous calls or requests), streaming support, and moderation loops ●Target audience: Fortune 500 companies and large enterprises ●Ease of use:Provides no-code tools and templates for quick deployment ●Deployment options: Supports both self -hosted and cloud deployments ●Support: Comprehensive support and maintenance services ●Efficiency: Designed for handling complex, multi - agent tasks efficiently ●Target audience: Developers and researchers ●Open-source framework: Facilitates cooperation among multiple AI agents ●Simplification: Orchestrates, automates, and optimises complex LLM workflows ●Human-in-the-loop: Supports human-in-the-loop workflows for enhanced performance ●Community-driven: Encourages innovation and collaboration within the community ●Target audience: AI enthusiasts and developers ●Autonomous AI agent: Executes tasks independently using GPT-4 architecture ●Task management:Breaks down complex goals into manageable sub-tasks ●Capabilities: Utilises internet access and code execution for task completion ●Versatility: Applied in various domains like content creation and customer service ●Popularity: Rapidly growing open -source project with a strong community When deciding between commercial vs open -source agentic AI tools, consider your organisation’s needs, upstream/downstream integration capabilities, and accessibility to resources to build, deploy, and manage these solutions.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Commercial solutions such as LangGraph and CrewAI offer robust support, seamless
nodes.text: Commercial solutions such as LangGraph and CrewAI offer robust support, seamless integration, and advanced features, making them suitable for complex, large -scale deployments.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Conversely, open -source solutions like AutoGen and AutoGPT are excellent choice
nodes.text: Conversely, open -source solutions like AutoGen and AutoGPT are excellent choices for rapid prototyping and proof -of-concept development, providing flexibility, community -driven innovation, and low cost of entry for technology decision makers and developers.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: The agentic AI tools ecosystem is expected to witness a rapid surge over the nex
nodes.text: The agentic AI tools ecosystem is expected to witness a rapid surge over the next few quarters.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Commercial solutions will likely continue to enhance their enterprise capabiliti
nodes.text: Commercial solutions will likely continue to enhance their enterprise capabilities, focusing on a wide range of integration options, security, and developer -friendly features.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Meanwhile, open-source tools will see increased community contributions, leading
nodes.text: Meanwhile, open-source tools will see increased community contributions, leading to rapid innovation in depth and coverage of agentic AI features and increased adoption.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: As commercial and open -source AI solutions evolve, organisations should stay ag
nodes.text: As commercial and open -source AI solutions evolve, organisations should stay agile, leveraging the strengths of both types to remain competitive and innovative.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: the AI capability roadmap that works for your business
nodes.text: the AI capability roadmap that works for your business
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: A vision without execution is hallucination—align your GenAI strategy with actio
nodes.text: A vision without execution is hallucination—align your GenAI strategy with actionable plans and meticulous execution.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Let's explore how to effectively integrate these principles into your AI roadmap
nodes.text: Let's explore how to effectively integrate these principles into your AI roadmap: ●Define clear objectives: What do you aim to achieve – cost reduction, revenue growth, customer satisfaction, or building an economic moat? ●Align AI initiatives with business goals: Ensure that AI projects are underpinned by your company's strategic objectives.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Whether it's cost reduction, increasing revenue, customer satisfaction, or creat
nodes.text: Whether it's cost reduction, increasing revenue, customer satisfaction, or creating a competitive advantage, aligning AI efforts with business goals ensures relevance and maximises impact. ●Secure executive sponsorship: Having support from top management is crucial for securing resources and driving organisational change.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Executive sponsorship can also help align AI initiatives with broader business s
nodes.text: Executive sponsorship can also help align AI initiatives with broader business strategies. ●Stakeholders buy-in: Ensure executive and departmental alignment. ●Start with high-impact use cases: Identify areas where AI can deliver significant value quickly.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Prioritise projects that address pressing challenges or offer substantial benefi
nodes.text: Prioritise projects that address pressing challenges or offer substantial benefits, such as cost reduction or revenue growth, to demonstrate AI ROI early on. ●Seek expert advice: Consult with AI experts or hire consultants to formulate your AI strategy and help you in making informed decisions.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Technology infrastructure: Is your IT environment ready for AI integration? ●Pl
nodes.text: ●Technology infrastructure: Is your IT environment ready for AI integration? ●Platform options: Weigh-in commercial and open -source AI solutions and make build -vs-buy decisions based on your organisation’s requirements, budget, and technical expertise. ●Consider integration: Ensure the chosen platform can integrate seamlessly with your existing systems and workflows, both upstream and downstream. ●Data readiness: Do you have access to quality, multimodal data? ●Talent pool: Do you have the skills in-house, or will you need external expertise?
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Start small: Begin with small pilot projects to test the effectiveness of agent
nodes.text: ●Start small: Begin with small pilot projects to test the effectiveness of agentic AI in your business environment. ●Measure success: Define clear metrics for success and monitor the performance of the pilot projects.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Gather feedback from stakeholders and make necessary adjustments. ●Agile methodo
nodes.text: Gather feedback from stakeholders and make necessary adjustments. ●Agile methodology: Be flexible, nimble and adaptive in your implementations. ●Iterate and improve: Use the insights gained from pilot projects to refine your approach and address any challenges.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Assess capabilities Meticulous execution Vision alignment Assess Meticulous exec
nodes.text: Assess capabilities Meticulous execution Vision alignment Assess Meticulous execution Organisational change Risk management Step 1 Step 2 Step 3 Step 6 Step 5 ●Gradual expansion: Once the pilot projects are successful, gradually scale up the implementation of agentic AI across more areas of your operations. ●Ensure support: Provide adequate training and support to your team to ensure a smooth transition and adoption of the new technology. ●Monitor and optimise: Continuously monitor the performance of agentic AI systems and optimise them for better results.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Ethical considerations: Address potential biases and compliance issues. ●Securi
nodes.text: ●Ethical considerations: Address potential biases and compliance issues. ●Security protocols: Protect sensitive data and align AI governance with national and global standards.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Educate and upskill: Begin by familiarising your workforce with the core concep
nodes.text: ●Educate and upskill: Begin by familiarising your workforce with the core concepts of data and AI. Understand what it is, how it works, and its potential applications in your organisation, business function and/or industry32.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Foster innovation: Encourage a culture of innovation within your organisation b
nodes.text: ●Foster innovation: Encourage a culture of innovation within your organisation by promoting experimentation and collaboration. ●Adapt and evolve: Be prepared to adapt your strategies and processes as the technology evolves and new opportunities arise. ●Stay informed: Keep up with the latest developments and trends in AI by reading industry reports, inviting experts to all-hands sessions, attending conferences, and participating in webinars.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: from AI investments
nodes.text: from AI investments
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Avoid the GenAI hype trap—focus on pragmatic steps that deliver real value.
nodes.text: Avoid the GenAI hype trap—focus on pragmatic steps that deliver real value.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Ensure a customer-centric approach: Always prioritise the end -user experience,
nodes.text: ●Ensure a customer-centric approach: Always prioritise the end -user experience, eventually it pays off in both financial and non -financial results.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Conduct thorough research: Before implementing AI solutions, research the avail
nodes.text: ●Conduct thorough research: Before implementing AI solutions, research the available technologies to find the best fit for your business needs. Understand the capabilities and limitations of agentic AI to set realistic expectations.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Start with small projects: Begin with pilot programmes to test the effectivenes
nodes.text: ●Start with small projects: Begin with pilot programmes to test the effectiveness of AI solutions. Small-scale implementations allow you to measure impact and make adjustments before a full - scale rollout.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Monitor performance and iterate: Regularly track the performance of your AI sys
nodes.text: ●Monitor performance and iterate: Regularly track the performance of your AI systems using key metrics aligned with your business goals. Use this data to refine models, adjust strategies, and make data-driven improvements over time.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Build cross-functional teams: Assemble teams that include members from various
nodes.text: ●Build cross-functional teams: Assemble teams that include members from various departments, such as IT, operations, finance, and marketing. Cross -functional collaboration ensures that AI initiatives are well-rounded and consider different perspectives and expertise.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Invest in employee training:Equip your team with the necessary skills to work a
nodes.text: ●Invest in employee training:Equip your team with the necessary skills to work alongside AI systems. Training ensures smooth integration and helps employees leverage AI tools effectively.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Invest in quality data: High-quality data is the backbone of effective AI solut
nodes.text: ●Invest in quality data: High-quality data is the backbone of effective AI solutions. Invest in data cleaning, integration, and management processes to ensure your AI systems have accurate and reliable data to work with.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Prioritise data security and privacy: Implement robust security measures to pro
nodes.text: ●Prioritise data security and privacy: Implement robust security measures to protect sensitive data. Ensure compliance with relevant regulations to maintain customer trust and avoid legal issues.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Invest in scalable AI platforms:Choose AI platforms and tools that are scalable
nodes.text: ●Invest in scalable AI platforms:Choose AI platforms and tools that are scalable and can grow with your business needs. Scalable solutions allow you to expand AI capabilities without significant additional investments.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Invest in continuous learning: Stay curious and updated with AI advancements an
nodes.text: ●Invest in continuous learning: Stay curious and updated with AI advancements and industry trends.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Ignore customer feedback: Pay attention to how your customers interact with AI
nodes.text: ●Ignore customer feedback: Pay attention to how your customers interact with AI solutions. Use their feedback to refine and enhance the user experience.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Underestimate complexity: AI projects are not plug-and-play.
nodes.text: ●Underestimate complexity: AI projects are not plug-and-play.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Rush implementation: Avoid hastily integrating AI without a clear strategy. A r
nodes.text: ●Rush implementation: Avoid hastily integrating AI without a clear strategy. A rushed implementation can lead to wasted resources and suboptimal results.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Neglect human oversight: While AI can automate many tasks, human oversight rema
nodes.text: ●Neglect human oversight: While AI can automate many tasks, human oversight remains crucial. Maintain a balance between automation and human input to ensure quality and accountability.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Ignore user adoption: Ensure that the AI solutions are user-friendly and meet t
nodes.text: ●Ignore user adoption: Ensure that the AI solutions are user-friendly and meet the needs of those who will interact with them daily. High user adoption rates lead to better data input, more accurate outputs, and higher ROI.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Overlook ethical considerations: Be mindful of the ethical implications of AI u
nodes.text: ●Overlook ethical considerations: Be mindful of the ethical implications of AI use. Ensure your AI systems are designed to prevent biases, respecting privacy laws, promote fairness and transparency. Adhere to ethical guidelines and legal regulations related to AI use.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Ignore change management: Prepare your workforce for AI adoption through traini
nodes.text: ●Ignore change management: Prepare your workforce for AI adoption through training and change management programmes. Educated employees are more likely to embrace AI tools, leading to better utilisation and ROI.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Underestimate costs: Be realistic about the investment required for AI integrat
nodes.text: ●Underestimate costs: Be realistic about the investment required for AI integration, including infrastructure, maintenance, and training costs. Plan your budget accordingly to avoid financial strain.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Ignore partnerships: Collaborate with trusted technology providers, consultants
nodes.text: ●Ignore partnerships: Collaborate with trusted technology providers, consultants, AI experts, and academic institutions. External expertise can accelerate implementation, provide valuable insights, and help avoid common pitfalls.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: ●Overlook for long-term sustainability: Develop a long-term AI strategy that con
nodes.text: ●Overlook for long-term sustainability: Develop a long-term AI strategy that considers future needs and technological advancements. Sustainable planning ensures that your AI investments continue to deliver value over time.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: By harnessing the unprecedented capabilities of agentic AI systems, both governm
nodes.text: By harnessing the unprecedented capabilities of agentic AI systems, both government entities and organisations can achieve significant efficiency gains, enhanced customer experiences, and superior business outcomes.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: While agentic AI will play a transformative role in both sectors, their specific
nodes.text: While agentic AI will play a transformative role in both sectors, their specific objectives, contexts, and goals will shape the distinct applications and their benefits.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Government entities can prioritise large-scale initiatives such as policymaking,
nodes.text: Government entities can prioritise large-scale initiatives such as policymaking, governance, public welfare, economic stability, and sustainability, leveraging agentic AI to orchestrate complex systems.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In contrast, organisations aiming for profitability growth, cost optimisation, a
nodes.text: In contrast, organisations aiming for profitability growth, cost optimisation, and competitive advantage can focus on developing agentic AI point solutions to address specific challenges within defined domains.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Most entities are expected to begin by experimenting with low hanging use cases.
nodes.text: Most entities are expected to begin by experimenting with low hanging use cases.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: A smaller number will see the vast opportunity window with agentic AI solutions
nodes.text: A smaller number will see the vast opportunity window with agentic AI solutions and adopt a strategic approach, recalibrating AI strategies to fully harness agentic AI solutions across a broader spectrum of business use cases and processes.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Only a handful—like Amazon, Google, Meta etc.—will embrace an AI-first mindset,
nodes.text: Only a handful—like Amazon, Google, Meta etc.—will embrace an AI-first mindset, reimagining products, services, and processes to redefine value creation mechanisms.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: In this transformation, agentic AI systems will take the lead role with humans a
nodes.text: In this transformation, agentic AI systems will take the lead role with humans as co-pilots, optimising speed, accuracy, contextual coherence, and cost-efficiency.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Human oversight will evolve, shifting focus toward more strategic planning and i
nodes.text: Human oversight will evolve, shifting focus toward more strategic planning and innovations rather than operational management.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: The shift from human-driven, labour-intensive processes to AI-managed operations
nodes.text: The shift from human-driven, labour-intensive processes to AI-managed operations will see autonomous agents handling tasks with unprecedented speed, precision, and adaptability.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: This transformation will not only reduce costs but also unlock new revenue strea
nodes.text: This transformation will not only reduce costs but also unlock new revenue streams and growth opportunities, allowing businesses and governments to deliver services faster and at a much larger scale.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: As agentic AI systems become more integrated, they will redefine how we work, pu
nodes.text: As agentic AI systems become more integrated, they will redefine how we work, pushing the boundaries of possibility and enabling a smarter, more agile world.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: The future of agentic AI is nearer than anticipated, propelled by rapid technolo
nodes.text: The future of agentic AI is nearer than anticipated, propelled by rapid technological advancements.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: However, realising its full potential requires greater commitment and adoption a
nodes.text: However, realising its full potential requires greater commitment and adoption across both government and industry.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Leaders at the forefront of AI adoption aren't just leveraging technology—they'r
nodes.text: Leaders at the forefront of AI adoption aren't just leveraging technology—they're redefining what's possible.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: Success in this arena won't be accidental; it demands strategic vision, meticulo
nodes.text: Success in this arena won't be accidental; it demands strategic vision, meticulous planning, and relentless execution.
nodes.author: Deloitte

nodes.label: For C- suite executives and senior leaders, embracing agentic AI is not just an
nodes.text: For C- suite executives and senior leaders, embracing agentic AI is not just an option but a strategic imperative to stay ahead in an increasingly competitive and AI-driven world.

nodes.label: The future belongs to those who prepare for it today –

nodes.label: 📖 Navigating AI Implementation: The Case for an Enterprise A
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Even to the casual observer, Artificial Intelligence (AI) is clearly one of the
nodes.text: Even to the casual observer, Artificial Intelligence (AI) is clearly one of the most discussed digitalization topics of 2023.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: No wonder, since AI has the hallmarks of a real game changer, potentially revolu
nodes.text: No wonder, since AI has the hallmarks of a real game changer, potentially revolutionizing the way the world economy works – and more.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Large and fast-growing investments by companies around the globe reflect that.
nodes.text: Large and fast-growing investments by companies around the globe reflect that.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: AI implementations impact most business areas and require coordinated delivery a
nodes.text: AI implementations impact most business areas and require coordinated delivery across multiple units and all technological layers.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: A plea for establishing a new Enterprise AI Architect role.
nodes.text: A plea for establishing a new Enterprise AI Architect role.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The ongoing global digital transformation is prone to creating technology hypes
nodes.text: The ongoing global digital transformation is prone to creating technology hypes regularly, some short-lived, some with a lasting impact.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: But in all probability, nothing in this century has been more of a game changer
nodes.text: But in all probability, nothing in this century has been more of a game changer than Artificial Intelligence.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: It will potentially completely alter the way we do business with each other and
nodes.text: It will potentially completely alter the way we do business with each other and may even fundamentally change our lifestyle.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Many organizations have realized the importance of AI.
nodes.text: Many organizations have realized the importance of AI.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: BCG surveyed 1,400 C-level executives across 50 markets about artificial intelli
nodes.text: BCG surveyed 1,400 C-level executives across 50 markets about artificial intelligence and GenAI.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Remarkably, 89% of them placed these technologies among their top three prioriti
nodes.text: Remarkably, 89% of them placed these technologies among their top three priorities for 2024.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: In 2022 alone, the worldwide corporate investments in research, development, and
nodes.text: In 2022 alone, the worldwide corporate investments in research, development, and applications amounted to 92 billion US Dollars.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: With Generative AI having added fuel to the fire at the turn of 2022/2023, this
nodes.text: With Generative AI having added fuel to the fire at the turn of 2022/2023, this number was undoubtedly higher in 2023.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Hasty implementations will backfire
nodes.text: Hasty implementations will backfire
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: As with most new technologies, there is no known gold standard for implementatio
nodes.text: As with most new technologies, there is no known gold standard for implementation and utilization of AI.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Different business units within the same organization can tackle similar topics
nodes.text: Different business units within the same organization can tackle similar topics using various solutions from different vendors.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This creates a considerable space for inefficiencies, especially if AI is implem
nodes.text: This creates a considerable space for inefficiencies, especially if AI is implemented without looking at a broader picture of the entire business & IT ecosystem.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Having said that, poor data quality, wrong integration mechanisms, and suboptima
nodes.text: Having said that, poor data quality, wrong integration mechanisms, and suboptimal exposure to the business will all have a detrimental impact on results and performance of an AI application, and may even render it useless.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This will be especially the case within a decentralized IT-landscape, for exampl
nodes.text: This will be especially the case within a decentralized IT-landscape, for example, in enterprises organized in independent communities or domains.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Deploying AI models at scale and making them useful for the normal operational f
nodes.text: Deploying AI models at scale and making them useful for the normal operational field is proving to be difficult at times, but it is a necessary step to ensure value generation.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: First, sort out the basics
nodes.text: First, sort out the basics
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: So, what is needed to get a tangible ROI from money spent on AI solutions? Any c
nodes.text: So, what is needed to get a tangible ROI from money spent on AI solutions? Any company contemplating an investment like that should be able to answer some basic questions. The following are some key examples:
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Value assurance: Where in the organization is AI most useful? For which business
nodes.text: Value assurance: Where in the organization is AI most useful? For which business processes or business capabilities? ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Model governance: Which solutions should be used to address specific business ne
nodes.text: Model governance: Which solutions should be used to address specific business needs? GenAI, Machine Learning, or just conventional algorithms, e.g., decision trees? How can we ensure that AI is used responsibly and that models do not drift over time?
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Platform foundations: Are any solutions already implemented in the organization
nodes.text: Platform foundations: Are any solutions already implemented in the organization that can be utilized to accelerate the whole process and save money? Should the company leverage vendors’ proprietary cloud platforms or develop their own inhouse? ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Data governance: Is the relevant data for model training available? Is it in the
nodes.text: Data governance: Is the relevant data for model training available? Is it in the required format and of good quality? How can we ensure data privacy and avoid data leakage?
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Delivery approach: What is the best way to integrate AI solutions into the busin
nodes.text: Delivery approach: What is the best way to integrate AI solutions into the business processes within the organization? Building new SPA applications or, e.g., embedding AI in Business Process Management or existing domain solutions? How can they be tested?
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: It is apparent that the aforementioned questions cover a vast range of subject a
nodes.text: It is apparent that the aforementioned questions cover a vast range of subject areas, from AI itself and data to cloud solutions and integration.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: To get meaningful answers, a holistic view of the organization, its business and
nodes.text: To get meaningful answers, a holistic view of the organization, its business and IT environment is needed.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: And it must be complemented by comprehensive knowledge of AI, e.g., its capabili
nodes.text: And it must be complemented by comprehensive knowledge of AI, e.g., its capabilities, limitations, or the pros and cons of different approaches and technologies.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The new Enterprise AI Architect Role
nodes.text: The new Enterprise AI Architect Role
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: In our opinion, the sheer number of interdependencies call for a new role to coo
nodes.text: In our opinion, the sheer number of interdependencies call for a new role to coordinate AI-related efforts at an enterprise level, the Enterprise AI Architect.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This person’s job is to keep track of, and continuously manage, requirements fro
nodes.text: This person’s job is to keep track of, and continuously manage, requirements from the business side and match them with technical capabilities and relevant governance policies.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The objective is to implement an effective AI application in a cost-efficient ma
nodes.text: The objective is to implement an effective AI application in a cost-efficient manner by leveraging existing solutions while also adhering to architectural principles.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Alternatively, if the necessity arises, the Enterprise AI Architect could decide
nodes.text: Alternatively, if the necessity arises, the Enterprise AI Architect could decide to discontinue an AI project if it hits insurmountable economic, technical, or legal obstacles.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The role of an Enterprise AI Architect therefore requires someone with a multitu
nodes.text: The role of an Enterprise AI Architect therefore requires someone with a multitude of different hard and soft skills.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The person filling that role needs to have a solid grasp of AI concepts, enterpr
nodes.text: The person filling that role needs to have a solid grasp of AI concepts, enterprise architecture, and data science, as well as a comprehensive understanding of the broad business ecosystem and the legal ramifications, such as: - Knowledge of the AI solutions market ; key vendors and their products, - Proven integration approaches, relevant for specific AI solutions, - Management of AI models over time, - Delivery and licensing models, their pros and cons, - Data management from requirements to acquisition, transformation, and creation of adequate data sets, - Deployment at scale options – on-site vs. cloud, - Continuous observability of AI model effectiveness to avoid any drift in quality over time, - Security and responsible AI by design.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Imperative: Broad business and technical background
nodes.text: Imperative: Broad business and technical background
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Knowing the developments in the evolving AI world is, as already stated, just on
nodes.text: Knowing the developments in the evolving AI world is, as already stated, just one side of the coin.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The Enterprise AI Architect also needs to know the company’s strategic mid- to l
nodes.text: The Enterprise AI Architect also needs to know the company’s strategic mid- to long-term goals, business processes, and IT environment.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: She/He is a key stakeholder in helping define the end-to-end transformation proc
nodes.text: She/He is a key stakeholder in helping define the end-to-end transformation process and ensure the target architecture will be defined and met.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This should, of course, include awareness of the low-hanging fruits, i.e., where
nodes.text: This should, of course, include awareness of the low-hanging fruits, i.e., where an AI-based improvement is needed most.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This makes it possible to determine if a suggested AI solution is useful for the
nodes.text: This makes it possible to determine if a suggested AI solution is useful for the business or is an experiment in disguise.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This is just the beginning of the required skills, as the Enterprise AI Architec
nodes.text: This is just the beginning of the required skills, as the Enterprise AI Architect is expected to be responsible for advising on data preparation, integration, and rollout.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This includes addressing conceivable compatibility and data quality issues due t
nodes.text: This includes addressing conceivable compatibility and data quality issues due to outdated systems or datasets.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: To top all this off, there are internal as well as external governance principle
nodes.text: To top all this off, there are internal as well as external governance principles and guidelines to consider.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Some of these are mandatory, and alignment of the AI solution is a must.
nodes.text: Some of these are mandatory, and alignment of the AI solution is a must.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Finally, the Enterprise AI architect must enhance current architecture principle
nodes.text: Finally, the Enterprise AI architect must enhance current architecture principles, patterns, guidelines, and technical standards to meet the new reality, while preserving freedom for innovation and value creation.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Desirable: Good mediation skills
nodes.text: Desirable: Good mediation skills
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Dependable soft skills are also needed, as the person will have to inspire the b
nodes.text: Dependable soft skills are also needed, as the person will have to inspire the business owners, but also needs to be able to talk to IT about the proverbial ones and zeros.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This is already indicative of the necessity for a very open-minded character, wh
nodes.text: This is already indicative of the necessity for a very open-minded character, who is willing to constantly learn and experiment.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: After all, AI is mostly terra incognita, at least for now.
nodes.text: After all, AI is mostly terra incognita, at least for now.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The Enterprise AI architect must also ensure proper collaboration with AI-relate
nodes.text: The Enterprise AI architect must also ensure proper collaboration with AI-related internal stakeholders, like data engineers, data scientists, and data governance representatives, but also external ones, like third-party AI solution vendors and external consultancy experts, if required.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Enterprise AI Architect – a future must have
nodes.text: Enterprise AI Architect – a future must have
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: All things considered, the Enterprise AI Architect is a very challenging role th
nodes.text: All things considered, the Enterprise AI Architect is a very challenging role that requires management of a quickly changing solution from all perspectives – code, data, and model wise.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Where it is located within the organization – with the enterprise architecture f
nodes.text: Where it is located within the organization – with the enterprise architecture function, in an independent AI realm, or at an entirely different unit – depends on the enterprise’s individual characteristics. ‍ There is only one certainty: The Enterprise AI Architect is a must.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Corporations that are first in establishing such a role the right way will gain
nodes.text: Corporations that are first in establishing such a role the right way will gain a significant head start in the AI race. They will be better positioned to shape their AI strategies and invest their portion of the 92 billion dollars spent on AI in a more optimized and reasonable manner.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: William El Kaim
nodes.text: William El Kaim
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: William El Kaim is a BCG Platinion IT Architecture Director and has more than tw
nodes.text: William El Kaim is a BCG Platinion IT Architecture Director and has more than twenty five years of experience in planning, designing and governing complex Information Systems.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Before joining BCG Platinion, he specialized in agile IT transformation, digital
nodes.text: Before joining BCG Platinion, he specialized in agile IT transformation, digital innovation, new technical architecture (Big data, Cloud, IOT, Microservice) and data governance.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Over the years, he has worked with international companies in Finance, HealthCar
nodes.text: Over the years, he has worked with international companies in Finance, HealthCare, Energy, Travel and Transport.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: William also created a startup, contributed to UML V2 and Model Driven Architect
nodes.text: William also created a startup, contributed to UML V2 and Model Driven Architecture at the OMG and published the Enterprise Architecture Digital Codex web site.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: William holds a doctoral thesis in Computer Science Engineering from the Pierre
nodes.text: William holds a doctoral thesis in Computer Science Engineering from the Pierre and Marie Curie University (Paris, France).
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: In his spare time, he enjoys creating digital games with his kids using Unreal e
nodes.text: In his spare time, he enjoys creating digital games with his kids using Unreal engine and stencil, blogging about technology and watching anime and SF movies.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Przemysław Krajewski
nodes.text: Przemysław Krajewski
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Przemysław is a Principal IT Architect working in the BCG Platinion Warsaw offic
nodes.text: Przemysław is a Principal IT Architect working in the BCG Platinion Warsaw office.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: He has almost 15 years of experience in conducting consulting projects related t
nodes.text: He has almost 15 years of experience in conducting consulting projects related to the IT Architecture management, analysis and optimization of business processes, planning changes in the area of enterprise architecture, managing digital transformation programs.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: More to Explore
nodes.text: More to Explore
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Enterprise as Code: An Operating Model for the AI Era
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Tech + Us: Harness the power of technology and AI Manage Subscriptions For the p
nodes.text: Tech + Us: Harness the power of technology and AI Manage Subscriptions For the past decade, tech leaders have focused on optimizing their technology landscapes.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: They migrated workloads to the cloud and modernized data, dismantling legacy sys
nodes.text: They migrated workloads to the cloud and modernized data, dismantling legacy systems along the way.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Those were the right priorities, but they were never the end goal.
nodes.text: Those were the right priorities, but they were never the end goal.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Today, the question is not how to migrate but how to differentiate through techn
nodes.text: Today, the question is not how to migrate but how to differentiate through technology itself. We call this next chapter “enterprise as code . ”
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Turning Operations into Logic
nodes.text: Turning Operations into Logic
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: At its core, enterprise as code is about explicitly defining how a business oper
nodes.text: At its core, enterprise as code is about explicitly defining how a business operates by capturing the implicit operating model and expressing it as code.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This allows both people and systems to understand, test, and improve how organiz
nodes.text: This allows both people and systems to understand, test, and improve how organizations run, whether the systems involved are automation frameworks, analytical tools, or AI agents .
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Embracing this shift requires organizations to move from intuition to specificat
nodes.text: Embracing this shift requires organizations to move from intuition to specification. Rather than relying on an indefinable sense of how things work, they capture the logic behind operations and decisions in forms that both people and systems can act on and evolve over time.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Most companies can define more of their work than they realize, but vital knowle
nodes.text: Most companies can define more of their work than they realize, but vital knowledge often hides in binders and spreadsheets, in side conversations, or in people’s heads. The lack of documentation and accessible information slows decision making and constrains growth.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: When organizations express their operations in the form of structured logic, eve
nodes.text: When organizations express their operations in the form of structured logic, everything changes.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: They can test and refine how work gets done.
nodes.text: They can test and refine how work gets done.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: They can automate parts of their processes, just as they can amplify the value a
nodes.text: They can automate parts of their processes, just as they can amplify the value and impact of their human workers.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Because it pairs rigor with adaptability, clarity acts as an accelerant: the mor
nodes.text: Because it pairs rigor with adaptability, clarity acts as an accelerant: the more precisely work is defined, the faster it can evolve.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Explicitly defining operations also makes collaboration between people and machi
nodes.text: Explicitly defining operations also makes collaboration between people and machines more natural and predictable.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The Next Productivity Frontier
nodes.text: The Next Productivity Frontier
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Every wave of enterprise technology has expanded what enterprises can do. The in
nodes.text: Every wave of enterprise technology has expanded what enterprises can do. The internet democratized communication. Cloud computing democratized infrastructure. Enterprise as code will democratize capability itself by giving businesses the power to design, execute, and evolve operations.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: When the logic of a business becomes explicit, the distance between intent and e
nodes.text: When the logic of a business becomes explicit, the distance between intent and execution shrinks. Ideas travel faster. Decisions improve in quality and speed. Teams spend less time coordinating and more time creating value.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Readiness Determines Results
nodes.text: Readiness Determines Results
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Organizations differ widely in how prepared they are for this shift. Some alread
nodes.text: Organizations differ widely in how prepared they are for this shift. Some already run on structured processes and formal policies. Others still depend mostly on unwritten knowledge and cultural habits.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Much of the value that a company gains from AI and automation depends on how cle
nodes.text: Much of the value that a company gains from AI and automation depends on how clearly it defines its desired outcomes and how firmly it connects those outcomes to the work that delivers them.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: When processes live only in people’s heads, it is difficult to apply intelligenc
nodes.text: When processes live only in people’s heads, it is difficult to apply intelligence effectively.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: But when workflows and decision rules are written down and maintained, AI can am
nodes.text: But when workflows and decision rules are written down and maintained, AI can amplify what already works instead of patching what does not.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: By operating on this footing, some companies are seeing real returns from AI tod
nodes.text: By operating on this footing, some companies are seeing real returns from AI today.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: For example, some are getting more ROI from AI by rigorously define their data p
nodes.text: For example, some are getting more ROI from AI by rigorously define their data policies.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: They’re also beginning to codify those standards in order to build them into how
nodes.text: They’re also beginning to codify those standards in order to build them into how systems operate, not just how people document them.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Enterprises that are legible to machines today are moving faster, and their mome
nodes.text: Enterprises that are legible to machines today are moving faster, and their momentum will only increase.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Every organization can start this work now. The sooner leaders formalize their o
nodes.text: Every organization can start this work now. The sooner leaders formalize their operating logic, the sooner they build the foundation for scalable intelligence.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Tech + Us: Harness the power of technology and AI
nodes.text: Tech + Us: Harness the power of technology and AI
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The Platform Imperative
nodes.text: The Platform Imperative
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: In enterprise as code, the platform does not just support infrastructure. It is
nodes.text: In enterprise as code, the platform does not just support infrastructure. It is the base layer on which the modern enterprise runs.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: AI performs best on shared, hyperscale platforms. Multitenant systems are more e
nodes.text: AI performs best on shared, hyperscale platforms. Multitenant systems are more efficient than single-tenant environments because they can expand in response to demand and operate across global networks.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: These shared platforms distribute workloads across industries and time zones, ad
nodes.text: These shared platforms distribute workloads across industries and time zones, adjusting to different usage patterns. As AI systems begin operating continuously rather than in shifts, the advantages of this model will continue to grow.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The question is no longer whether to use shared platforms, but how to optimize d
nodes.text: The question is no longer whether to use shared platforms, but how to optimize design with them in mind. The challenge is to create the right balance of resilience, flexibility, and control.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Designing for Resilience and Agency
nodes.text: Designing for Resilience and Agency
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Resilience is non-negotiable.
nodes.text: Resilience is non-negotiable.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Yet many leaders still imagine that relying on large platforms or AI systems int
nodes.text: Yet many leaders still imagine that relying on large platforms or AI systems introduces new forms of risk .
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: In reality, such dependence does not diminish resilience in well-designed system
nodes.text: In reality, such dependence does not diminish resilience in well-designed systems.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Modern distributed architectures, properly built, are far more reliable than any
nodes.text: Modern distributed architectures, properly built, are far more reliable than any single organization’s local setup.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Designing for resilience and agency means developing plans to withstand major fa
nodes.text: Designing for resilience and agency means developing plans to withstand major failures—for example, preparing for region-wide outages.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: It means keeping core systems independent of vendors to maximize flexibility.
nodes.text: It means keeping core systems independent of vendors to maximize flexibility.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Another objective is portability, which entails the ability to move data and res
nodes.text: Another objective is portability, which entails the ability to move data and resources wherever needs arise.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: All of these features help deliver a key benefit of enterprise as code: maintain
nodes.text: All of these features help deliver a key benefit of enterprise as code: maintaining control of the company’s systems and operations, no matter what happens.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Leading in the Era of Enterprise as Code
nodes.text: Leading in the Era of Enterprise as Code
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Competitive advantage depends on how precisely an organization can describe itse
nodes.text: Competitive advantage depends on how precisely an organization can describe itself to the systems that run its processes. The path forward starts with a few practical steps:
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Identify high-volume, high-value processes that drive cost or differentiation.
nodes.text: Identify high-volume, high-value processes that drive cost or differentiation.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Capture their operating logic clearly and as early as possible, as if onboarding
nodes.text: Capture their operating logic clearly and as early as possible, as if onboarding a new team—or an intelligent agent.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Build governance and controls into that logic from the start, not as afterthough
nodes.text: Build governance and controls into that logic from the start, not as afterthoughts.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Design the work in a way that permits continuous measurement and improvement of
nodes.text: Design the work in a way that permits continuous measurement and improvement of performance.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Automation doesn’t create order; it depends on it. The enterprises that move fas
nodes.text: Automation doesn’t create order; it depends on it. The enterprises that move fastest know where definition ends and discretion begins—clear at the edges, creative within.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The shift has already started. The previous era connected systems and data. The
nodes.text: The shift has already started. The previous era connected systems and data. The next will codify operations and decision making. Work will become visible and measurable, and will constantly improve.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This is the new dividing line in business. Organizations that define themselves
nodes.text: This is the new dividing line in business. Organizations that define themselves programmatically will move faster and operate smarter. Those that are not equipped to adapt in real time will struggle to keep up with those that are.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: How Agentic AI Is Transforming Enterprise Platforms
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Stay ahead with BCG insights on digital, technology, and data Manage Subscriptio
nodes.text: Stay ahead with BCG insights on digital, technology, and data Manage Subscriptions Today’s AI agents have the potential to revolutionize business processes across the board.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Our experience shows that recent advances in computing power and the rise of AI-
nodes.text: Our experience shows that recent advances in computing power and the rise of AI-optimized chips can reduce human error and cut employees’ low-value work time by 25% to 40%—and even more in some cases.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: These agents work 24/7 and can handle data traffic spikes without extra headcoun
nodes.text: These agents work 24/7 and can handle data traffic spikes without extra headcount.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: And the AI-powered workflows they create can accelerate business processes by 30
nodes.text: And the AI-powered workflows they create can accelerate business processes by 30% to 50% in areas ranging from finance and procurement to customer operations.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Stay ahead with BCG insights on digital, technology, and data
nodes.text: Stay ahead with BCG insights on digital, technology, and data
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: AI agents are not just improving workflows; they’re redefining how businesses op
nodes.text: AI agents are not just improving workflows; they’re redefining how businesses operate.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Recent breakthroughs in deep learning, generative AI, and autonomous systems, au
nodes.text: Recent breakthroughs in deep learning, generative AI, and autonomous systems, augmented by a proliferation of enterprise data from digital devices and collaboration tools, have dramatically increased their ability to handle complex decision making in real time.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: As a result, these intelligent virtual assistants are transforming core technolo
nodes.text: As a result, these intelligent virtual assistants are transforming core technology platforms like CRM, ERP, and HR from relatively static systems to dynamic ecosystems that can analyze data and make decisions without human intervention, optimizing and adapting instantaneously.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: But implementing these new agents is a complex process.
nodes.text: But implementing these new agents is a complex process.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: It requires system interoperability, high-quality data, redesigned enterprise pl
nodes.text: It requires system interoperability, high-quality data, redesigned enterprise platforms, and agentic designs with the right level of autonomy, constraints, and goals.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: To meet these challenges, BCG has developed a step-by-step playbook for agent-ba
nodes.text: To meet these challenges, BCG has developed a step-by-step playbook for agent-based workflow transformation.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Driving Value with Agentic AI
nodes.text: Driving Value with Agentic AI
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: While both traditional workflow automation and agentic AI focus strongly on effi
nodes.text: While both traditional workflow automation and agentic AI focus strongly on efficiency, AI agents are also capable of intelligence, adaptability, and continuous learning.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: They can take autonomous, goal-directed actions and process and optimize workflo
nodes.text: They can take autonomous, goal-directed actions and process and optimize workflows at an unprecedented rate, without latency issues.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Soon AI-powered, interconnected agents embedded into workflows will even be able
nodes.text: Soon AI-powered, interconnected agents embedded into workflows will even be able to adapt dynamically to changes in the environment, detecting and fixing issues independently and then moving to prevent them from happening again.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: An agent working with SAP to manage the supply chain, for example, might notice
nodes.text: An agent working with SAP to manage the supply chain, for example, might notice that costs are increasing and trigger its finance platform to reassess forecasts.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This dynamic adaptability means companies that embrace agentic AI now will gain
nodes.text: This dynamic adaptability means companies that embrace agentic AI now will gain a competitive edge in productivity, responsiveness, and innovation.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: How Organizations Are Using Agentic AI to Make an Impact
nodes.text: How Organizations Are Using Agentic AI to Make an Impact
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Businesses across industries and functions are integrating agentic AI into a var
nodes.text: Businesses across industries and functions are integrating agentic AI into a variety of workflows, sometimes leveraging platforms such as Salesforce’s Einstein AI and AgentForce platforms, which use predictive analytics and automation to enhance sales, marketing, and customer service workflows.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: In addition, ServiceNow’s AI agents and Now Assist capabilities are automating I
nodes.text: In addition, ServiceNow’s AI agents and Now Assist capabilities are automating IT, HR, and operational processes, reducing manual workloads by up to 60%.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Workflows that are successfully using agentic AI are even found in areas such as
nodes.text: Workflows that are successfully using agentic AI are even found in areas such as finance and fraud detection.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The results are impressive, including the following:
nodes.text: The results are impressive, including the following:
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Workflow Orchestration in ERP/CRM Platforms. AI agents are auto-resolving IT ser
nodes.text: Workflow Orchestration in ERP/CRM Platforms. AI agents are auto-resolving IT service tickets, rerouting supplies to cover inventory shortages, and triggering procurement flows, all without the need for human input. Early adopters are seeing 20% to 30% faster workflow cycles and significant reductions in back-office costs.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Customer Service and Case Management. AI agents are handling insurance claims fr
nodes.text: Customer Service and Case Management. AI agents are handling insurance claims from end to end, including document validation, triage, and escalation or payout. As a result, claim handling time has been cut in some instances by 40%, and net promoter scores—a measure of how likely a customer is to refer an insurer to an acquaintance—have increased by 15 points (on a scale that ranges from –100 to 100).
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Sales and Marketing Automation. AI-driven campaign managers are testing, adaptin
nodes.text: Sales and Marketing Automation. AI-driven campaign managers are testing, adapting, and optimizing consumer touchpoints in real time. One B2B SaaS firm experienced a 25% increase in lead conversion after implementing agentic campaign routing.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Finance and Risk Monitoring. AI agents are autonomously detecting anomalies, for
nodes.text: Finance and Risk Monitoring. AI agents are autonomously detecting anomalies, forecasting cash needs, and recommending reallocation across accounts. As a result, risk events have been reduced by 60% in pilot environments.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Implementing Controls and Governance Around AI Agents
nodes.text: Implementing Controls and Governance Around AI Agents
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This complex new digital technology also creates hazards.
nodes.text: This complex new digital technology also creates hazards.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: It naturally increases the potential for cybersecurity threats, since it creates
nodes.text: It naturally increases the potential for cybersecurity threats, since it creates new or expanded attack surfaces that malicious actors can leverage; for instance, by hijacking or misrouting AI agents.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Potential bias is also a well-known AI concern, along with governance challenges
nodes.text: Potential bias is also a well-known AI concern, along with governance challenges, as without oversight, even well-constructed agents can go off course.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Companies must therefore find the right balance between AI autonomy and human co
nodes.text: Companies must therefore find the right balance between AI autonomy and human control.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Too much leeway leads to risk, while too little renders the agent ineffective.
nodes.text: Too much leeway leads to risk, while too little renders the agent ineffective.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This is especially true in the early days of implementation, which should includ
nodes.text: This is especially true in the early days of implementation, which should include constant monitoring and clear guardrails, escalation paths, and feedback loops, all of which require thoughtful design and tooling.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: In addition, businesses often fail to assign responsibility for autonomous AI ag
nodes.text: In addition, businesses often fail to assign responsibility for autonomous AI agents, meaning that no one has clear accountability if something goes wrong.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Explainability and auditability are therefore crucial, especially within regulat
nodes.text: Explainability and auditability are therefore crucial, especially within regulated industries.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Decisions made in a black box will pose both legal and reputational risk.
nodes.text: Decisions made in a black box will pose both legal and reputational risk.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: To scale AI agents safely and credibly, companies should adopt a coherent set of
nodes.text: To scale AI agents safely and credibly, companies should adopt a coherent set of controls across the value chain, embedding them from day one rather than adding them after incidents occur.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: (See the exhibit.) These controls cannot be afterthoughts.
nodes.text: (See the exhibit.) These controls cannot be afterthoughts.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: They must inform scope, architecture, and operating habits to create clear accou
nodes.text: They must inform scope, architecture, and operating habits to create clear accountability and prevent small errors from compounding.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The following sections outline these controls by phase: design, build, and opera
nodes.text: The following sections outline these controls by phase: design, build, and operate.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Design. Organizations should translate business objectives into a secure-by-desi
nodes.text: Design. Organizations should translate business objectives into a secure-by-design AI-agent concept with explicit ownership, least-privilege access, clear autonomy thresholds, and hard ethical boundaries.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Organizations should translate business objectives into a secure-by-design AI-ag
nodes.text: Organizations should translate business objectives into a secure-by-design AI-agent concept with explicit ownership, least-privilege access, clear autonomy thresholds, and hard ethical boundaries.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Governance and Oversight. Companies should create a virtual control tower that t
nodes.text: Governance and Oversight. Companies should create a virtual control tower that tracks every AI agent deployed and assigns each agent a clear owner. For example, a retail company could assign a manager for an AI that’s responsible for customer refunds, ensuring that the manager reviews any unusual refunds over $500.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Access and Permission Controls. Businesses should treat agents as they would new
nodes.text: Access and Permission Controls. Businesses should treat agents as they would new employees, giving them access to only what they need. For instance, a fintech startup testing an AI chatbot on internal data might realize that the AI can query payroll, but if the startup has correctly established purely role-based access, the agent will only be able to see realistic test data in an isolated “sandbox" environment.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Risk Tiering and Autonomy Levels. Companies should classify each agent and its a
nodes.text: Risk Tiering and Autonomy Levels. Companies should classify each agent and its actions, set monetary and operational thresholds, require approvals or dual control for high-impact moves, and cap agents’ daily spending. For example, an agent should be able to make automatic refunds only up to a defined limit, with manager approval above that amount and a daily budget ceiling.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Ethical and Safety Controls. The company should bake in its values as hard rules
nodes.text: Ethical and Safety Controls. The company should bake in its values as hard rules. For instance, a media company could ensure that its content-generation AI has a permanent block on political endorsements so the agent never creates candidate profiles that could be misinterpreted as bias.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Build. Businesses should turn their requirements into engineering through guardr
nodes.text: Build. Businesses should turn their requirements into engineering through guardrails, sandboxing, logging, and rigorous validation so that each agent is safe, observable, and resilient before real users see it.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Guardrails and Technical Safeguards. Companies should always include a kill swit
nodes.text: Guardrails and Technical Safeguards. Companies should always include a kill switch, like a fire extinguisher one hopes will never be needed. A health care provider, for example, might hit the kill switch if an AI scheduling agent started double-booking critical MRI machines.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Tool Hardening. Businesses should not just connect tools but wrap every action i
nodes.text: Tool Hardening. Businesses should not just connect tools but wrap every action in strict schemas and safe defaults so that mistakes don’t cascade. This should include “allow” lists, input checks, timeouts, and spending caps. For example, when a procurement agent touches the ERP system, the schema should force the use of valid supplier IDs and currency, cap amounts, and block free-text writes—stopping risky transactions before they occur.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Validation and Testing. Businesses should run “red team” drills, assigning a gro
nodes.text: Validation and Testing. Businesses should run “red team” drills, assigning a group to try to trick the AI into doing something harmful, just as banks hire “ethical hackers.” For instance, the group might try convincing the procurement AI to approve absurd purchases, such as a $10,000 espresso machine . In addition, businesses should use sandbox testing as one might train a new driver: letting them drive only in the parking lot until they’re ready to go onto the highway.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Operate. Companies should run the agents as products with empowered human oversi
nodes.text: Operate. Companies should run the agents as products with empowered human oversight, monitoring and logging, explainability, and disciplined change management.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Human Oversight and Intervention. Humans should not just be in the loop, aware o
nodes.text: Human Oversight and Intervention. Humans should not just be in the loop, aware of all the agents’ activities, but alert and empowered to intervene as well. For example, a price-adjusting AI might accidentally drop prices to $0.01 overnight if the night shift has no clear process to stop it. To fix this flaw, a company could have trained staff with override authority on duty 24/7.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Explainability and Auditability. Businesses should log all decisions and rationa
nodes.text: Explainability and Auditability. Businesses should log all decisions and rationales so that, should something go wrong, operators and auditors can reconstruct what happened and why. This is essential in regulated industries and reduces legal and reputational risk from opaque black-box behavior.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Change Management and Release Control. Companies should adopt structured change-
nodes.text: Change Management and Release Control. Companies should adopt structured change-management practices for AI agents to ensure that every update to prompts, tools, data sets, or policies is version-controlled and traceable. And before wide release, companies should run shadow rollouts, deploying the new version in parallel to monitor its performance against the old system without affecting operations. In addition, a tested rollback plan must be in place so that if issues arise, the company can quickly revert its systems to a stable version.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Before wide release, companies should run shadow rollouts, deploying the new ver
nodes.text: Before wide release, companies should run shadow rollouts, deploying the new version in parallel to monitor its performance against the old system without affecting operations.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: For instance, an industrial goods manufacturer deploying an AI agent for predict
nodes.text: For instance, an industrial goods manufacturer deploying an AI agent for predictive maintenance on factory equipment could first shadow-test a new model on a limited set of machines.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: If the updated agent began over-predicting failures, causing unnecessary downtim
nodes.text: If the updated agent began over-predicting failures, causing unnecessary downtime or maintenance costs, the company could immediately roll back to the previous version while investigating the root causes.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: It could thereby capture performance improvements without introducing hidden ris
nodes.text: It could thereby capture performance improvements without introducing hidden risks to production output.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Confronting Implementation Challenges
nodes.text: Confronting Implementation Challenges
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The three biggest challenges that companies encounter as they take on agentic AI
nodes.text: The three biggest challenges that companies encounter as they take on agentic AI transformations center on talent, quick wins, and the use of legacy technologies.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Finding and Developing the Right Talent.
nodes.text: Finding and Developing the Right Talent.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Agentic AI requires a mix of advanced technical talent such as AI-prompt enginee
nodes.text: Agentic AI requires a mix of advanced technical talent such as AI-prompt engineers, AI or machine language specialists, and data engineers as well as business translators who can map AI use cases to workflows.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Most organizations underestimate this need.
nodes.text: Most organizations underestimate this need.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: For example, an insurer that is setting up an AI innovation team may initially s
nodes.text: For example, an insurer that is setting up an AI innovation team may initially staff it with only the existing data-scientist team; however, it will quickly realize it needs domain experts who understand claims processing in depth and must embed them into the team to gain traction.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Delivering Early Value to Build Momentum.
nodes.text: Delivering Early Value to Build Momentum.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Large AI programs often stall because they aim too high at the start.
nodes.text: Large AI programs often stall because they aim too high at the start.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Executives want to see tangible benefits quickly; without early proof points, in
nodes.text: Executives want to see tangible benefits quickly; without early proof points, investment and enthusiasm can fade.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: One company tried to launch an enterprise-wide “AI assistant for every employee”
nodes.text: One company tried to launch an enterprise-wide “AI assistant for every employee” campaign, but the scope was too broad, and progress was slow.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: As a result, the company pivoted to a much narrower first win: an AI agent that
nodes.text: As a result, the company pivoted to a much narrower first win: an AI agent that automated vendor onboarding.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Within three months, it had cut onboarding time by 40%, giving leadership the co
nodes.text: Within three months, it had cut onboarding time by 40%, giving leadership the confidence to fund broader use cases.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Integrating with Legacy Technologies.
nodes.text: Integrating with Legacy Technologies.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Many companies run on complex, decades-old infrastructure that was not designed
nodes.text: Many companies run on complex, decades-old infrastructure that was not designed to support autonomous AI agents.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Integration with such legacy technology can result in infrastructure that is bri
nodes.text: Integration with such legacy technology can result in infrastructure that is brittle, expensive, and slow.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Companies should therefore use AI as a smart middleware layer that translates be
nodes.text: Companies should therefore use AI as a smart middleware layer that translates between modern agent interfaces and legacy systems; for example, as large-language-model-powered connectors that auto-generate APIs from old codebases.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: In some cases, businesses might wrap legacy workflows with AI-driven automation
nodes.text: In some cases, businesses might wrap legacy workflows with AI-driven automation so that agents can operate through existing user interfaces and processes, delivering quick wins without the need for deep re-platforming.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Real Transformation Means AI-First
nodes.text: Real Transformation Means AI-First
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: AI doesn’t just automate workflows—it transforms them. As enterprises move beyon
nodes.text: AI doesn’t just automate workflows—it transforms them. As enterprises move beyond AI-augmented workflows and toward AI-orchestrated execution, they’ll set goals such as autonomously managed operations, real-time adaptation, and continuously optimized processes with minimal human oversight.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: However, effective transformation requires businesses to integrate AI-first work
nodes.text: However, effective transformation requires businesses to integrate AI-first workflow execution into their business operations and fully embrace the transformation.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This means treating AI as a product—assigning someone design authority over the
nodes.text: This means treating AI as a product—assigning someone design authority over the agents’ processes, implementing control mechanisms, and creating human-in-the-loop fallbacks.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Accomplishing this will require structural and cultural change, including:
nodes.text: Accomplishing this will require structural and cultural change, including:
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Platform Re-Architecture. Moving from static APIs to event-driven or agent-compa
nodes.text: Platform Re-Architecture. Moving from static APIs to event-driven or agent-compatible infrastructure, such as adopting a software framework or open agent architectures to help integrate AI agents and legacy technologies.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Operating Model Shift. Embedding agents into core value chain operations, not ju
nodes.text: Operating Model Shift. Embedding agents into core value chain operations, not just the edge (such as the helpdesk).
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: AI Talent Strategy. Hiring or training teams that can design agent ecosystems, n
nodes.text: AI Talent Strategy. Hiring or training teams that can design agent ecosystems, not just models.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: AI Orchestration Is the Future
nodes.text: AI Orchestration Is the Future
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: AI-assisted processes are no longer enough.
nodes.text: AI-assisted processes are no longer enough.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The future should be AI-orchestrated—with AI agents learning, adapting, and runn
nodes.text: The future should be AI-orchestrated—with AI agents learning, adapting, and running enterprise processes in real time.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Companies that embrace this change decisively will gain a competitive edge in pr
nodes.text: Companies that embrace this change decisively will gain a competitive edge in productivity, responsiveness, and innovation, leading in a landscape where AI no longer just informs decisions, but makes them.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Leading in the Age of AI Agents: Managing the Machines That
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Stay ahead with BCG insights on artificial intelligence Manage Subscriptions Age
nodes.text: Stay ahead with BCG insights on artificial intelligence Manage Subscriptions Agentic AI isn’t just a new technology, it’s the future—and it’s already here.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Systems based on agentic AI can plan, act, and learn on their own, thereby accel
nodes.text: Systems based on agentic AI can plan, act, and learn on their own, thereby accelerating efficiency, innovation, and growth .
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: But to seize this potential, companies must navigate uncharted ground, managing
nodes.text: But to seize this potential, companies must navigate uncharted ground, managing a technology that combines capabilities typical of tools with others characteristic of people.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The task involves reimagining workflows, roles, governance, and learning, and de
nodes.text: The task involves reimagining workflows, roles, governance, and learning, and devising investment strategies to provide the flexibility necessary to create value with agentic AI.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: This insight comes from the ninth annual global research study on artificial int
nodes.text: This insight comes from the ninth annual global research study on artificial intelligence and business strategy conducted by MIT Sloan Management Review and BCG.
nodes.author: BCG Global
