nodes.label: A total of 2,102 respondents spanning 21 industries and 116 countries participat
nodes.text: A total of 2,102 respondents spanning 21 industries and 116 countries participated in the survey, and the results reveal that agentic AI is rapidly emerging in enterprises: 35% of organizations have begun to use it; and another 44% plan to join their ranks soon.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: On the other hand, 47% indicate that they don’t have a strategy for what they ar
nodes.text: On the other hand, 47% indicate that they don’t have a strategy for what they are going to do with AI.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The dual nature of agentic AI further complicates the situation.
nodes.text: The dual nature of agentic AI further complicates the situation.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Blending qualities of tool and human, agentic AI doesn’t fit neatly into traditi
nodes.text: Blending qualities of tool and human, agentic AI doesn’t fit neatly into traditional management frameworks.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Managing it purely as a tool or purely as a worker creates four strategic tensio
nodes.text: Managing it purely as a tool or purely as a worker creates four strategic tensions: scalability versus adaptability, investment versus employment, supervision versus autonomy, and process retrofitting versus process reimagining.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Yet few organizations are restructuring to manage the competing demands.
nodes.text: Yet few organizations are restructuring to manage the competing demands.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Although substantial effort is necessary, companies that embrace agentic AI’s du
nodes.text: Although substantial effort is necessary, companies that embrace agentic AI’s dual nature put themselves in a position to unlock the technology’s full potential.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: The path forward starts by clarifying the underlying value thesis.
nodes.text: The path forward starts by clarifying the underlying value thesis.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Agentic AI systems have the ability to appreciate with use, getting smarter and
nodes.text: Agentic AI systems have the ability to appreciate with use, getting smarter and more capable as time passes.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Companies that treat agentic AI solely as a cost-saving tool risk missing its gr
nodes.text: Companies that treat agentic AI solely as a cost-saving tool risk missing its greater value as an engine for scalable learning, adaptability, and innovation.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: So organizations should first ask, “What are we trying to optimize for?” Then th
nodes.text: So organizations should first ask, “What are we trying to optimize for?” Then they should take five practical steps:
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Redesign work around agentic-first workflows. Rather than automating isolated ta
nodes.text: Redesign work around agentic-first workflows. Rather than automating isolated tasks, reimagine processes to integrate agentic AI’s tool-like scalability and human-like adaptability. Almost two-thirds of agentic AI leaders expect to see changes to their operating model. (See the exhibit.) A best practice: build workflows that can shift smoothly between efficiency optimization and innovative problem solving—rather than focusing on one or the other.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Upgrade governance and redesign decision rights. Since agentic AI systems fall s
nodes.text: Upgrade governance and redesign decision rights. Since agentic AI systems fall somewhere between tools (owned and predictable) and people (autonomous and requiring supervision), governance must be adaptive. Our survey results indicate that 58% of leading organizations anticipate changes to governance and decision-making rights. Companies should create governance hubs and equip them with enterprise-wide guardrails. But they should also treat decision rights as dynamic—tailored by workflow—and build system-level oversight mechanisms to monitor and manage varying degrees of AI autonomy.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Redefine roles for human-agentic interaction. When AI agents coordinate workflow
nodes.text: Redefine roles for human-agentic interaction. When AI agents coordinate workflows, traditional spans of control widen. This calls for a flatter organization (45% of agentic AI leaders expect to see a reduction in middle management layers) in which managerial roles evolve to orchestrate hybrid human-AI teams. With this in mind, companies should consider creating dual career paths for generalist orchestrators and AI-augmented specialists. Both are critical.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Prioritize continuous learning for humans and for AI agents. Organizations that
nodes.text: Prioritize continuous learning for humans and for AI agents. Organizations that fail to care for their AI agents risk seeing their systems become outdated, inaccurate, or unpredictable. Employee training programs should cover not only how to use AI systems, but also how to supervise, critique, and direct them. Like their human counterparts, agentic AI benefits from continuous learning and support. This can take different forms, including fine-tuning agents through retraining as more data becomes available and conducting AI performance reviews to evaluate the system’s accuracy, adaptability, and bias—and when necessary, to trigger intervention.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Anchor investment decisions in value. Agentic AI can scale exponentially, evolve
nodes.text: Anchor investment decisions in value. Agentic AI can scale exponentially, evolve quickly, and create value in multiple ways simultaneously. As such, investment strategies should combine scale, adaptability, and focus. Agentic AI leaders ensure that investments remain anchored to the value thesis, whether it be efficiency, innovation, revenue growth, or some combination of these. They also build adaptable investment plans that possess the flexibility to support ongoing training over the AI agent’s life cycle, as well as to respond to shifts in technology and evolving infrastructure needs.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: A mix of tool and collaborator, agentic AI is unlike any other technology.
nodes.text: A mix of tool and collaborator, agentic AI is unlike any other technology.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Businesses that recognize its dual nature and align their processes, governance,
nodes.text: Businesses that recognize its dual nature and align their processes, governance, talent, and investments accordingly can capture its full value.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: Although agentic AI will inevitably challenge established management paradigms,
nodes.text: Although agentic AI will inevitably challenge established management paradigms, with the right strategy, imagination, and execution, it won’t just offer promise—it will deliver impact.
nodes.author: BCG Global

nodes.label: 📖 Technology Architecture of Tomorrow   | Insights
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: From rigid roadmaps to real-time systems, 25 years of enterprise IT evolution ha
nodes.text: From rigid roadmaps to real-time systems, 25 years of enterprise IT evolution have brought us to a defining moment — but the architecture of tomorrow is still taking shape.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The last quarter century proved that good architecture morphs as customer expect
nodes.text: The last quarter century proved that good architecture morphs as customer expectations evolve, but now it needs to be more adaptive, agile, and future-focused than ever before.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: As part of this shift, architecture has become a strategic boardroom topic, no l
nodes.text: As part of this shift, architecture has become a strategic boardroom topic, no longer relegated to an ‘IT for IT’ backroom discussion, and concepts like AI-powered self-optimization are actively debated and embraced.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Organizations that have excelled are those that refuse to be victims of their ar
nodes.text: Organizations that have excelled are those that refuse to be victims of their architecture, instead taking control of their future evolution by shaping the architecture that drives their business.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: We are seeing several promising architectural patterns that have emerged and  em
nodes.text: We are seeing several promising architectural patterns that have emerged and  embraced at scale.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: For instance, self-healing and event-driven architectures are increasing in popu
nodes.text: For instance, self-healing and event-driven architectures are increasing in popularity, and AI-enabled predictive maintenance is widespread.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Microservices are no longer treated as the answer to every problem, and modular
nodes.text: Microservices are no longer treated as the answer to every problem, and modular monoliths are coming back into fashion as a viable alternative.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: IT landscapes will increasingly be governed by AI, enabling architectural ineffi
nodes.text: IT landscapes will increasingly be governed by AI, enabling architectural inefficiencies to be spotted and complex application portfolios to be optimized.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: AI-enabled security will also characterize the coming years of architecture inno
nodes.text: AI-enabled security will also characterize the coming years of architecture innovation, with use cases including real-time threat detection and mitigation.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: At BCG Platinion, our tech experts are engaged in helping some of the world’s le
nodes.text: At BCG Platinion, our tech experts are engaged in helping some of the world’s leading organizations navigate this new and often technically overwhelming era, and they are gathering valuable insights along the way.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Building on Part 1’s journey from the 2000s to today’s AI-driven, cloud-native e
nodes.text: Building on Part 1’s journey from the 2000s to today’s AI-driven, cloud-native era, and marking our 25 th anniversary, in Part 2 our experts spotlight the next chapter of enterprise architecture—new tools of the trade, self-healing systems, and architects’ evolving role.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: ‍ New Tools of the Trade
nodes.text: ‍ New Tools of the Trade
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: From AI to blockchain, transformative technologies are bursting onto the scene t
nodes.text: From AI to blockchain, transformative technologies are bursting onto the scene that must be integrated without compromising stability.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This means balancing innovation with architectural discipline while keeping an e
nodes.text: This means balancing innovation with architectural discipline while keeping an eye on technical debt and IT sprawl.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Without proper integration, AI adds complexity, increases risk, and obscures dec
nodes.text: Without proper integration, AI adds complexity, increases risk, and obscures decision logic.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The solution is to ensure transparency with the right techniques, keep humans in
nodes.text: The solution is to ensure transparency with the right techniques, keep humans in-the-loop, and manage AI workloads with Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) and robust observability tools.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: By using hybrid blockchain architectures, organizations can increase transparenc
nodes.text: By using hybrid blockchain architectures, organizations can increase transparency and decentralization without putting data at risk.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: This is achieved by combining permissioned private layers and permissionless pub
nodes.text: This is achieved by combining permissioned private layers and permissionless public layers, with smart contracts in place to enforce rules across both.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: While blockchain is expected to be used in more niche use cases, quantum computi
nodes.text: While blockchain is expected to be used in more niche use cases, quantum computing is on track to have broader, more transformative implications.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: For example, it has the potential to let users assess the efficiency of multiple
nodes.text: For example, it has the potential to let users assess the efficiency of multiple solutions within their systems simultaneously, while also taking AI capabilities to the next level.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Enterprise Architecture Management (EAM) frameworks are a major advantage when i
nodes.text: Enterprise Architecture Management (EAM) frameworks are a major advantage when it comes to adopting new technologies in a structured, thoughtful way.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Using them can be especially effective when combined with industry-specific fram
nodes.text: Using them can be especially effective when combined with industry-specific frameworks, such as the Banking Industry Architecture Network (BIAN).
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: ‍ As organizations become increasingly comfortable with using technologies like
nodes.text: ‍ As organizations become increasingly comfortable with using technologies like AI to enhance their approach to IT architecture, groundbreaking new capabilities will be realized—the ability for systems to self-heal is a prime example. ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Self-Healing Systems
nodes.text: Self-Healing Systems
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: As modern IT environments become ever more complex, those using automation to ma
nodes.text: As modern IT environments become ever more complex, those using automation to manage them will gain a significant competitive advantage.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Mechanisms for self-healing will be essential if organizations are to maintain h
nodes.text: Mechanisms for self-healing will be essential if organizations are to maintain high availability and security while cutting costs.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: When a system can detect, diagnose, and repair issues in a predictive way, costl
nodes.text: When a system can detect, diagnose, and repair issues in a predictive way, costly downtime is reduced immediately.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Just as importantly, the precious capacity of technical talent can be redeployed
nodes.text: Just as importantly, the precious capacity of technical talent can be redeployed to more value-adding tasks.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Resilience mechanisms can also be implemented to defend against single points of
nodes.text: Resilience mechanisms can also be implemented to defend against single points of failure and prevent costly system-wide outages.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: From a security standpoint, self-healing capabilities have become crucial now th
nodes.text: From a security standpoint, self-healing capabilities have become crucial now that sophisticated cyberattacks are often autonomous.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The right AI-enabled security capabilities will simultaneously detect, isolate,
nodes.text: The right AI-enabled security capabilities will simultaneously detect, isolate, patch, and remediate a threat in real time—whereas a human operator may not react quickly enough to mitigate damage and downtime.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: As hybrid, cloud-native architectures become increasingly distributed, failures
nodes.text: As hybrid, cloud-native architectures become increasingly distributed, failures can simply no longer be traced as easily as they could when systems were monolithic.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Because of this, self-healing systems will become the new standard for efficienc
nodes.text: Because of this, self-healing systems will become the new standard for efficiency, security, and overall resilience.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The development process is also ripe for AI transformation, from generating code
nodes.text: The development process is also ripe for AI transformation, from generating code for developers and building test cases for production and monitoring, to supporting the work of business analysts.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: BCG benchmarks show that AI can increase the efficiency of development by up to
nodes.text: BCG benchmarks show that AI can increase the efficiency of development by up to 50%, and potentially even more in the future depending on the use case. With AI-enabled efficiency increases in mind, what will the future role of the architect look like?
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: From Designers to Orchestrators
nodes.text: From Designers to Orchestrators
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Once gatekeepers of rigid standards and long-term roadmaps, enterprise architect
nodes.text: Once gatekeepers of rigid standards and long-term roadmaps, enterprise architects are becoming collaborative technology advisors that design and oversee modular systems in real-time, Gen AI tooling blurs lines between Enterprise and Solution architecture.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: As software development becomes increasingly like building with blocks (think cl
nodes.text: As software development becomes increasingly like building with blocks (think cloud services and ready-made Application Programming Interface (APIs), architects will embed themselves in agile teams and help to manage living systems.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: The once marginalized skill of software testing is making a comeback; as softwar
nodes.text: The once marginalized skill of software testing is making a comeback; as software development becomes more automated, focusing on observability and testing is a no-regret move that gives organizations the ability to safely deploy software at the greater speeds.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: GenAI has the potential to significantly enhance reliability through predictive
nodes.text: GenAI has the potential to significantly enhance reliability through predictive maintenance, which could reduce system downtime by 50 to 60%. 4 The technology will also handle things like continuous value tracking, serving as a strong link between IT and the business by producing holistic insights across functions. 5 With greater capacity at their disposal, architects will focus more heavily on:
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: ‍ Leading AI system design including managing data pipelines and model operation
nodes.text: ‍ Leading AI system design including managing data pipelines and model operations ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Translating business vision into scalable, composable architectures ‍
nodes.text: Translating business vision into scalable, composable architectures ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Piloting new technologies and architectural experiments ‍
nodes.text: Piloting new technologies and architectural experiments ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Coaching teams on applying patterns, trade-offs and managing complexity ‍
nodes.text: Coaching teams on applying patterns, trade-offs and managing complexity ‍
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Embedding architectural literacy across the organization to raise decision quali
nodes.text: Embedding architectural literacy across the organization to raise decision quality
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Making critical decisions regarding the trade-off between incremental modernizat
nodes.text: Making critical decisions regarding the trade-off between incremental modernization and the rapid adoption of new technologies will remain a core focus for architects, with hype-driven decisions presenting a significant risk.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Proven technical experts will be needed to navigate options thoughtfully and def
nodes.text: Proven technical experts will be needed to navigate options thoughtfully and define the right course of action for the business.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: We can expect this new breed of architect to be more aligned with the business i
nodes.text: We can expect this new breed of architect to be more aligned with the business in terms of strategy and value delivery, while also taking on a more cross-functional, embedded role.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Architects won’t just design systems—they will architect platforms for autonomy,
nodes.text: Architects won’t just design systems—they will architect platforms for autonomy, adaptability, and sustained strategic advantage.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Conclusion
nodes.text: Conclusion
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Architecture is a strategic enabler of resilience, speed, and long-term value.
nodes.text: Architecture is a strategic enabler of resilience, speed, and long-term value.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: It needs to be data-centric, cloud-native, increasingly autonomous, and above al
nodes.text: It needs to be data-centric, cloud-native, increasingly autonomous, and above all, modular.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Distributed architectures built on APIs are bringing the era of static, monolith
nodes.text: Distributed architectures built on APIs are bringing the era of static, monolithic systems to an end.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Microservices, event-driven patterns, and low-code platforms will increasingly b
nodes.text: Microservices, event-driven patterns, and low-code platforms will increasingly become central architectural features, promoting changeability and scalability.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: AI is set to take over the operating layer, with Artificial Intelligence for IT
nodes.text: AI is set to take over the operating layer, with Artificial Intelligence for IT Operations (AIOps) platforms supporting self-healing systems, managing infrastructure requirements, and detecting anomalies.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Prescriptive and predictive analytics, powered by machine learning and Large Lan
nodes.text: Prescriptive and predictive analytics, powered by machine learning and Large Language Models (LLMs), have a central role to play in the continuous optimization we foresee.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Architects’ priorities will continue to include translating business strategy in
nodes.text: Architects’ priorities will continue to include translating business strategy into tech capabilities and driving change when it comes to real-world concerns like the environmental impact of IT operations. 6 We believe Green IT is a significant opportunity to create smarter, more efficient systems.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: At BCG Platinion, we are passionate about tailoring architecture transformations
nodes.text: At BCG Platinion, we are passionate about tailoring architecture transformations end-to-end and moving this crucial conversation out of the backroom.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: Our mission is to build systems that shape the next wave of technology, because
nodes.text: Our mission is to build systems that shape the next wave of technology, because the coming era won’t be defined by tools, but by the architectures behind them.
nodes.author: BCG Platinion

nodes.label: For help blueprinting the future of your business, get in touch with our expert
nodes.text: For help blueprinting the future of your business, get in touch with our expert team.

nodes.label: The year of truth for AI
nodes.text: The year of truth for AI
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: AI becomes the backbone of the digital economy, shifting from isolated proofs of
nodes.text: AI becomes the backbone of the digital economy, shifting from isolated proofs of concept to coherent, adaptive, and trusted value systems. This transformation demands not just technology, but governance and cultural readiness to embed AI into the very fabric of enterprise decision-making.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: AI is eating software
nodes.text: AI is eating software
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: The paradigm moves from “writing code” to “expressing intent.” Developers articu
nodes.text: The paradigm moves from “writing code” to “expressing intent.” Developers articulate desired outcomes, and AI autonomously delivers, integrating and maintaining systems behind the scenes.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: As software becomes self-assembling and self-healing, the competitive edge will
nodes.text: As software becomes self-assembling and self-healing, the competitive edge will hinge on mastering orchestration and governance rather than manual coding.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Cloud 3.0: all flavors of cloud
nodes.text: Cloud 3.0: all flavors of cloud
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Cloud is entering its next evolution.
nodes.text: Cloud is entering its next evolution.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: After a decade focused on migration and cost efficiency, cloud is now becoming t
nodes.text: After a decade focused on migration and cost efficiency, cloud is now becoming the operational backbone for AI and AI assisted apps.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: AI cannot scale only on the classical public cloud architectures.
nodes.text: AI cannot scale only on the classical public cloud architectures.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: The need to fine-tune models on proprietary data, manage data sensitivity, and d
nodes.text: The need to fine-tune models on proprietary data, manage data sensitivity, and deploy low-latency inference is pushing organizations toward hybrid, private, multi and sovereign cloud models, and not by exception.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Cloud ceases to be a passive infrastructure layer and becomes an active enabler
nodes.text: Cloud ceases to be a passive infrastructure layer and becomes an active enabler of AI-driven architectures, ensuring portability, sovereignty.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: The rise of intelligent ops
nodes.text: The rise of intelligent ops
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Monolithic enterprise backbones evolve into living ecosystems of intelligent, mo
nodes.text: Monolithic enterprise backbones evolve into living ecosystems of intelligent, modular, and continuously learning applications, blending human oversight with autonomous AI agents and putting the process back at the core.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: This shift turns operations into adaptive engines of value creation, where resil
nodes.text: This shift turns operations into adaptive engines of value creation, where resilience and agility become structural rather than aspirational.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Intelligent operations position enterprises not just to run better, but to reinv
nodes.text: Intelligent operations position enterprises not just to run better, but to reinvent themselves continuously.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: The borderless paradox of tech sovereignty
nodes.text: The borderless paradox of tech sovereignty
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Tech sovereignty returns to at the top of the agenda, but the race is now for re
nodes.text: Tech sovereignty returns to at the top of the agenda, but the race is now for resilient interdependence—balancing open collaboration with strategic self-reliance.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Success will depend on designing systems that remain globally connected yet cont
nodes.text: Success will depend on designing systems that remain globally connected yet controllable, embedding sovereignty principles into architecture rather than isolationist strategies.

nodes.text: More than half of organizations are committing to sustained investment, with many planning over a five-year horizon – reflecting the belief that consistent, long-term investment in AI builds cumulative benefits that late adopters cannot replicate.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: The latest research brief from the Capgemini Research Institute, The multi-year
nodes.text: The latest research brief from the Capgemini Research Institute, The multi-year AI advantage: Building the enterprise of tomorrow , explores how businesses are accelerating AI adoption, balancing near-term priorities with long-term vision, and building the foundations for enterprise-wide intelligence.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Key findings, based on a global survey of over 1,500 leaders across 15 countries
nodes.text: Key findings, based on a global survey of over 1,500 leaders across 15 countries, include:
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: AI budgets are rising: Organizations expect to allocate 5% of annual business bu
nodes.text: AI budgets are rising: Organizations expect to allocate 5% of annual business budgets to AI by 2026, up from 3% in 2025. Leaders recognize that failing to scale AI quickly could mean missed opportunities and lost strategic advantage.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: AI is now everywhere: More than half of organizations are prioritizing AI invest
nodes.text: AI is now everywhere: More than half of organizations are prioritizing AI investments in sales, marketing, and IT operations. Gen AI has crossed the threshold from pilots to scaled use, with 38% already operationalizing use cases. Agentic AI and edge AI are gaining traction, signaling a broader spectrum of adoption.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Enterprise-wide AI adoption is a new discipline, and the playbook is still evolv
nodes.text: Enterprise-wide AI adoption is a new discipline, and the playbook is still evolving: Strong executive sponsorship (highlighted by 67% of surveyed organizations), external partnerships (59%), governance and ethical frameworks (53%), and scalable data infrastructure (51%) are viewed as critical enablers for moving from pilots to enterprise-wide deployment.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Human–AI collaboration is key: AI is amplifying – not replacing – human capabili
nodes.text: Human–AI collaboration is key: AI is amplifying – not replacing – human capabilities. 66% of organizations report measurable improvements in productivity and decision quality through human–AI collaboration. Six in 10 are redefining skillsets and investing in workforce upskilling.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.label: Sovereignty and trust are strategic priorities: Data and AI sovereignty are top
nodes.text: Sovereignty and trust are strategic priorities: Data and AI sovereignty are top concerns, with 54% prioritizing data control. Organizations are balancing build-versus-buy strategies to combine speed with differentiation.
nodes.author: Capgemini

nodes.text: The next decade won’t be defined by incremental digital upgrades, but by a profound shift toward autonomous, adaptive, and self-optimizing systems powered by agentic AI.

nodes.text: This is the promise of agentic AI: transitioning from simple task execution to autonomous value creation at enterprise scale.

nodes.label: How agentic AI redefines the enterprise
nodes.text: How agentic AI redefines the enterprise
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: Anticipation over reaction: With agentic AI, businesses gain systems that can de
nodes.text: Anticipation over reaction: With agentic AI, businesses gain systems that can detect change before it impacts operations. From supply chain shifts to evolving customer behaviors, AI agents can help enterprises anticipate what’s next, rather than react when it’s too late.
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: Real-time operational adaptation: Agentic systems can adjust workflows, reroute
nodes.text: Real-time operational adaptation: Agentic systems can adjust workflows, reroute processes, and optimize operations in real time. Whether it’s resolving issues before they surface or dynamically reallocating resources, enterprises gain responsiveness that traditional systems simply can’t match.
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: Accelerated decision cycles: Decision-making transforms from long, manual cycles
nodes.text: Accelerated decision cycles: Decision-making transforms from long, manual cycles into minutes or seconds. Instead of waiting for reports, enterprises receive continuous, actionable intelligence that supports confident, timely choices.
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: Empowering people, not replacing them: At the heart of this shift is augmentatio
nodes.text: Empowering people, not replacing them: At the heart of this shift is augmentation, not displacement. Agentic AI frees employees from complexity and repetitive tasks so they can focus on innovation, problem solving, and creativity. It strengthens human contribution by reducing friction and elevating purpose-driven work.
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: Building an autonomous enterprise: The move to autonomous business models will d
nodes.text: Building an autonomous enterprise: The move to autonomous business models will define the next generation of transformation. But achieving this requires the right balance of strategy, governance, technology, and execution.
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: Capgemini and AWS are committed to helping organizations adopt agentic AI respon
nodes.text: Capgemini and AWS are committed to helping organizations adopt agentic AI responsibly and effectively, with robust operating models, secure foundations, and scalable architectures that support continuous evolution.
nodes.author: Alex MirZabeigi

nodes.label: This is about creating enterprises that operate with intelligence, adapt with ag
nodes.text: This is about creating enterprises that operate with intelligence, adapt with agility, and innovate with clarity.

nodes.label: Inside the process: how EY navigated its own AI
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: EY set ambitious goals to drive AI transformation across three key areas:
nodes.text: EY set ambitious goals to drive AI transformation across three key areas:
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Enhancing service offerings with AI-enabled solutions for growth and efficiency
nodes.text: Enhancing service offerings with AI-enabled solutions for growth and efficiency
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Redefining internal work, functions and technology for optimal operations in the
nodes.text: Redefining internal work, functions and technology for optimal operations in the AI era
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Shaping public policy, ethics and social agendas to promote the responsible use
nodes.text: Shaping public policy, ethics and social agendas to promote the responsible use of AI globally
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: These objectives were designed to deliver long-term value and growth while addre
nodes.text: These objectives were designed to deliver long-term value and growth while addressing the comprehensive needs of clients, internal capabilities and societal impact.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: To meet these goals – considering the challenges to support all dimensions of th
nodes.text: To meet these goals – considering the challenges to support all dimensions of the AI journey – the team focused on six facets through which they could navigate the complexities of AI transformation with confidence and precision.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: “From our own process, EY has uncovered challenges and learned lessons across si
nodes.text: “From our own process, EY has uncovered challenges and learned lessons across six key areas for AI: strategy, people, technology, governance, clients and society,” explains Beatriz Sanz Sáiz, EY Global AI Sector Leader.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: The functional transformation strategically mapped AI use cases against business
nodes.text: The functional transformation strategically mapped AI use cases against business goals and our entire value chain, reimagining workflows and operations around AI.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: This approach mitigated the risk of launching piecemeal AI initiatives that migh
nodes.text: This approach mitigated the risk of launching piecemeal AI initiatives that might fail to deliver anticipated value, or potentially waste resources on low-impact projects that could result in stalled or abandoned efforts.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: In the realm of technology and data, EY centralized AI to streamline firm-wide t
nodes.text: In the realm of technology and data, EY centralized AI to streamline firm-wide technology consumption, foster the integration of innovative solutions, and encourage responsible exploration.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Developers were empowered with advanced tools and platforms to accelerate the AI
nodes.text: Developers were empowered with advanced tools and platforms to accelerate the AI development lifecycle, while data and systems integration facilitated architectural harmony and robust data governance in alignment with the EY responsible AI framework.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: The teams furthered this agenda by collaborating with strategic technology allia
nodes.text: The teams furthered this agenda by collaborating with strategic technology alliances to help position the global organization at the forefront of the AI revolution.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: With a focus on augmenting people, EY adopted a human-centered, transparent appr
nodes.text: With a focus on augmenting people, EY adopted a human-centered, transparent approach to transformation that fosters AI literacy, drives consistent integration into daily workflows, and addresses employee reservations toward new technologies and tools.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Internally, EY embraced this philosophy to prepare its workforce for an AI-drive
nodes.text: Internally, EY embraced this philosophy to prepare its workforce for an AI-driven future, closing skills gaps through targeted upskilling and efficient change management.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: “We have an opportunity to reshape the industry from a technology perspective an
nodes.text: “We have an opportunity to reshape the industry from a technology perspective and with the domain knowledge we have within EY,” says Pablo Cebro, EY Global Technology Platforms Lead.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: The commitment to responsible AI reflects a dedication to building confidence in
nodes.text: The commitment to responsible AI reflects a dedication to building confidence in AI, aligning technology solutions, enterprise processes, and AI infrastructure with core values of ethical governance and risk mitigation.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: As responsible AI is critical to transformation, EY established firm-wide AI def
nodes.text: As responsible AI is critical to transformation, EY established firm-wide AI definitions to ensure consistency and clarity throughout all business functions.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Risk management processes were designed to evolve alongside the pace of AI innov
nodes.text: Risk management processes were designed to evolve alongside the pace of AI innovation, striking a balance between speed and emerging risks.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Additionally, budget prioritization was organized to coincide with AI governance
nodes.text: Additionally, budget prioritization was organized to coincide with AI governance to promote effective, responsible AI deployment.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: “A Responsible AI framework is critical to our AI journey and is designed to gui
nodes.text: “A Responsible AI framework is critical to our AI journey and is designed to guide the ethical development, deployment and governance of AI technologies within the firm,” notes Alexei Ivanov, EY Global Risk Management – Transformation and Innovation Leader. “Reflecting on our commitment to ethical leadership and readiness, our primary AI ethos is making sure AI is ethical and doing the right thing for the right purpose.” Focusing on service disruption, EY is actively redefining its services and solutions offered to clients.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Centered on elevating the client experience, EY is embracing alternative commerc
nodes.text: Centered on elevating the client experience, EY is embracing alternative commercial models to reflect the true value delivered.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Client labs have been established to co-invest with clients to build capabilitie
nodes.text: Client labs have been established to co-invest with clients to build capabilities and establish market leadership, consistently aligning services and solutions with the forefront of industry needs.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: “Our approach to transformation isn’t just a roadmap for our organization’s evol
nodes.text: “Our approach to transformation isn’t just a roadmap for our organization’s evolution, but an activator for industry-wide change, empowering clients worldwide with the lessons from our 400,000 strong workforce’s AI journey,” says Hanne Jesca Bax, EY Global Vice Chair – Markets.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: Finally, the commitment to “AI for good” is central to the transformation strate
nodes.text: Finally, the commitment to “AI for good” is central to the transformation strategy, emphasizing a focus on ethical and responsible use of AI.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: The profound impact of AI necessitates a persistent dedication to preserving pub
nodes.text: The profound impact of AI necessitates a persistent dedication to preserving public confidence through well-defined safeguards, policies and responsible development.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: EY transformation efforts demonstrate that responsible, practical and strategic
nodes.text: EY transformation efforts demonstrate that responsible, practical and strategic AI applications can lead to a more equitable and sustainable future.
nodes.author: EY

nodes.label: AI and Transformations: The enterprise power couple
nodes.text: AI and Transformations: The enterprise power couple
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Discover how AI can streamline operations, reduce costs, and accelerate value cr
nodes.text: Discover how AI can streamline operations, reduce costs, and accelerate value creation, while addressing common transformation challenges.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: The AI revolution has arrived—and enterprise transformations will never be the s
nodes.text: The AI revolution has arrived—and enterprise transformations will never be the same.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: AI’s powerful suite of technologies has quickly become a force-multiplier for di
nodes.text: AI’s powerful suite of technologies has quickly become a force-multiplier for digital transformation initiatives and is delivering value for most companies, according to the KPMG US Technology Survey Report , which found that 9 in 10 companies are now delivering improvements in profitability and performance from digital transformation initiatives.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: However, AI is also revealing the weaknesses in many existing transformation pro
nodes.text: However, AI is also revealing the weaknesses in many existing transformation projects by exposing cracks in their cloud and data foundations. These revelations are compelling innovation and transformation leaders to reevaluate their playbooks.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Indeed, when embedded seamlessly and intentionally into transformation projects
nodes.text: Indeed, when embedded seamlessly and intentionally into transformation projects from the start, AI’s game-changing technologies can help organizations streamline the notoriously complex “details” of enterprise-change operations, reduce costs, as well as accelerate value creation and speed to market.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Clearly, AI technologies can play a starring role in enabling successful digital
nodes.text: Clearly, AI technologies can play a starring role in enabling successful digital transformation initiatives—as our research and work with clients has shown.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: But getting this power couple of AI and transformation to work in harmony will r
nodes.text: But getting this power couple of AI and transformation to work in harmony will require companies to reimagine their approach, empower their people, and put some long-standing assumptions about how to deliver enterprise change in their rearview mirror.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Here’s a closer look at how AI technologies can enhance your digital transformat
nodes.text: Here’s a closer look at how AI technologies can enhance your digital transformation efforts.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Enhancing the transformation hierarchy
nodes.text: Enhancing the transformation hierarchy
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: For many leading companies, reimagining the enterprise-change playbook starts wi
nodes.text: For many leading companies, reimagining the enterprise-change playbook starts with a commitment to making transformations that are AI-enabled from the start.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: They are using AI and GenAI tools to power key components at each level of the d
nodes.text: They are using AI and GenAI tools to power key components at each level of the digital transformation journey—from cloud-based-platforms to modern-data taking it all the way through to build AI-powered user experiences—and tightly connecting these essential services at every level using modern composable architecture.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: At KPMG, we think of this approach as an enhanced new transformation hierarchy :
nodes.text: At KPMG, we think of this approach as an enhanced new transformation hierarchy : A framework for executing enterprise digital initiatives that leverages multiple cutting-edge capabilities, including Cloud, Data & AI (CDAI), to produce breakthrough efficiencies from the bottom up and the top down, powered by a fully connected Intelligent Cloud.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: For leading enterprises, the goal of any business-led tech transformation is to
nodes.text: For leading enterprises, the goal of any business-led tech transformation is to deliver differentiated business outcomes.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: These leading enterprises leverage this modern transformation hierarchy and sign
nodes.text: These leading enterprises leverage this modern transformation hierarchy and significantly optimize their user experiences via expanded customization for customers, employees, and their business and ecosystem partners.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: In our experience with client transformations, when they are done methodically b
nodes.text: In our experience with client transformations, when they are done methodically by meticulously injecting AI and with hyper focus on value, they deliver not incremental but exponential business outcomes.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Here’s a closer look at how this dynamic new transformation hierarchy works:
nodes.text: Here’s a closer look at how this dynamic new transformation hierarchy works:
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: The Engagement layer (Connectivity & Experience)
nodes.text: The Engagement layer (Connectivity & Experience)
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: This layer leverages the platforms and data to deliver robust user experiences—c
nodes.text: This layer leverages the platforms and data to deliver robust user experiences—customer-facing apps, predictive modeling for the finance team, and perhaps even a GenAI-powered executive dashboard to provide a near real-time view of how specific transformation initiatives are progressing.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: The Cognitive Intelligence layer (Data & Integration)
nodes.text: The Cognitive Intelligence layer (Data & Integration)
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: This data and integration layer houses content, APIs, microservices, data pipeli
nodes.text: This data and integration layer houses content, APIs, microservices, data pipelines and event-driven technologies.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: The Engine Room (Platforms)
nodes.text: The Engine Room (Platforms)
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: This foundational layer includes essential platforms like virtual ERPs, CRMs, da
nodes.text: This foundational layer includes essential platforms like virtual ERPs, CRMs, data storage, and more.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: At this level, AI can help automate data extraction from legacy systems, as just
nodes.text: At this level, AI can help automate data extraction from legacy systems, as just one example, while GenAI can generate dynamic customer segmentations that support highly customized content and insights.

nodes.text: Opportunity is knocking
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: For transformation leaders, help from AI isn’t “on the way”—it’s here, today.
nodes.text: For transformation leaders, help from AI isn’t “on the way”—it’s here, today.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: It’s already delivering real value and accelerating access to new capabilities f
nodes.text: It’s already delivering real value and accelerating access to new capabilities for early adopters of their complex technology initiatives.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: And it’s upending stubborn, long-standing perceptions that most enterprise trans
nodes.text: And it’s upending stubborn, long-standing perceptions that most enterprise transformations are moonshots that, more often than not, fall short of their lofty goals.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: To be sure, AI’s powerful (and still expanding) suite of technologies on their o
nodes.text: To be sure, AI’s powerful (and still expanding) suite of technologies on their own aren’t some Rosetta Stones that will suddenly unlock the mysteries of successful transformations. But they can dramatically elevate the people, processes, and technologies involved.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Further, the debut of this new power couple doesn’t need to wait for some future
nodes.text: Further, the debut of this new power couple doesn’t need to wait for some future enterprise change initiative. Indeed, many companies are achieving significant business value sooner by weaving AI into existing transformations.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: With its power to turn complexity into clarity and moonshots into milestones, AI
nodes.text: With its power to turn complexity into clarity and moonshots into milestones, AI is proving that transforming the results from enterprise transformation doesn’t require a crystal ball … you just need to answer the opportunity knocking at your door.

nodes.label: Is your infrastructure ready for your AI aspirations?
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Is your infrastructure ready for your AI aspirations?
nodes.text: Is your infrastructure ready for your AI aspirations?
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Your business may be ready, but can your IT infrastructure handle the extra dema
nodes.text: Your business may be ready, but can your IT infrastructure handle the extra demands of AI?
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Over the last decade, IT organizations have worked tirelessly to modernize their
nodes.text: Over the last decade, IT organizations have worked tirelessly to modernize their infrastructures to support the evolving needs of the business, driven primarily by the transition to the cloud and the software-as-a-service (SaaS) model.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: But the impact of the cloud now seems practically glacial compared to that of ar
nodes.text: But the impact of the cloud now seems practically glacial compared to that of artificial intelligence (AI), which is transforming business—and putting pressure on IT to respond—at an unprecedented rate.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Traditional IT systems must quickly evolve to accommodate the immense processing
nodes.text: Traditional IT systems must quickly evolve to accommodate the immense processing power, storage, and data management requirements demanded by AI applications.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Companies that hesitate to capitalize on these trends risk falling behind their
nodes.text: Companies that hesitate to capitalize on these trends risk falling behind their peers in innovation, efficiency, and competitiveness.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Without the ability to leverage AI, organizations risk missing out on data-drive
nodes.text: Without the ability to leverage AI, organizations risk missing out on data-driven decision-making and automation opportunities and fail to deliver the experiences that modern consumers demand.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: A recent Cisco report highlights significant infrastructure bottlenecks as organ
nodes.text: A recent Cisco report highlights significant infrastructure bottlenecks as organizations strive to integrate AI technologies, revealing that only 14% of companies are "fully prepared" for AI adoption due to deficiencies in six key business areas: strategy, infrastructure, data, governance, talent, and culture.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Infrastructure is identified as a particularly critical obstacle; although 95% o
nodes.text: Infrastructure is identified as a particularly critical obstacle; although 95% of businesses acknowledge that AI will increase infrastructure demands, only 17% have networks capable of handling such complexities.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: A substantial portion of companies, 23%, report limited or absent scalability in
nodes.text: A substantial portion of companies, 23%, report limited or absent scalability in their current IT frameworks, while 30% experience issues with network latency and throughput.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Additionally, 54% of respondents admit their infrastructure has only "moderate o
nodes.text: Additionally, 54% of respondents admit their infrastructure has only "moderate or limited scalability," necessitating updates to support AI's computational requirements.
nodes.author: KPMG

nodes.label: Notably, only 31% of companies describe their infrastructure as "highly scalable

nodes.label: Organisations across industries are realising the transformational power of AI a
nodes.text: Organisations across industries are realising the transformational power of AI agents—streamlining operations and tackling complex business tasks at scale. However, many deployments encounter issues with managing state, ensuring scalability, and maintaining operational stability once complexity increases. This post outlines an event-driven framework that leverages Apache Kafka for messaging and BPMN-based process orchestration to deliver the resilience, efficiency, and compliance modern enterprises require.

nodes.label: Instead of direct calls between AI services, each event is published to Kafka, a
nodes.text: Instead of direct calls between AI services, each event is published to Kafka, allowing agents and services to operate asynchronously. Key advantages:
nodes.author: PricewaterhouseCoopers

nodes.label: Seamless scalability Individual components (e.g., speech-to-text or fraud detect
nodes.text: Seamless scalability Individual components (e.g., speech-to-text or fraud detection services) scale independently based on message throughput. If a sudden spike occurs (peak trading day, e-commerce flash sale), only the relevant components need to scale.
nodes.author: PricewaterhouseCoopers

nodes.label: Resilience and fault tolerance With Kafka’s event log, the system can replay eve
nodes.text: Resilience and fault tolerance With Kafka’s event log, the system can replay events after downtime or disruptions. This is vital in regulated areas like finance and healthcare, where data loss is unacceptable.
nodes.author: PricewaterhouseCoopers

nodes.label: Clear separation of concerns BPMN-based orchestration handles the business proce
nodes.text: Clear separation of concerns BPMN-based orchestration handles the business process flow, while AI agents focus on analysis and decision-making. This modular design reduces complexity and improves maintainability.

nodes.text: A long-running business process is modeled in BPMN (for example, loan origination or complex voice-based customer support).

nodes.text: Each process step is mapped to events flowing through Kafka topics.

nodes.text: AI agents subscribe to relevant Kafka topics, process the events (e.g., run a machine learning model), and publish results back to Kafka.

nodes.text: The BPMN engine listens for specific events to move the workflow to the next stage—fully decoupled from the AI services themselves.

nodes.text: By using this loose coupling , enterprises can adapt individual AI tasks, business logic, or compliance steps without affecting the entire system.

nodes.label: Conversational AI at scale A conversational AI application uses Azure Speech Ser
nodes.text: Conversational AI at scale A conversational AI application uses Azure Speech Services for real-time voice interactions. Audio data streams into Kafka, which triggers separate services for speech-to-text conversion, context-aware response generation, and text-to-speech transformation. Each result is published as an event, so microservices can react asynchronously. By decoupling each step, the system scales horizontally to thousands of concurrent sessions while preserving low latency.
nodes.author: PricewaterhouseCoopers

nodes.label: Direct voice-to-voice AI models In advanced setups, AI models process voice inpu
nodes.text: Direct voice-to-voice AI models In advanced setups, AI models process voice inputs directly and generate synthetic voice outputs in real-time—bypassing text intermediaries. For example, frameworks like Google’s WaveNet, OpenAI’s Whisper, or Meta’s SeamlessM4T handle audio in and audio out. Event-driven flow : Kafka topics manage each stage (voice input processing, intent recognition, response generation, voice synthesis) asynchronously. Scaling and latency : Edge computing, GPU acceleration, and WebRTC-based streaming can further reduce network delays. Context management : Redis or similar in-memory stores provide a “session memory,” so the AI agent can recall user context, enabling human-like back-and-forth conversations. This design accommodates thousands of concurrent conversations with ultra-low latency—ideal for customer support, voice-based digital assistants, and hands-free IoT applications.

nodes.text: Context management : Redis or similar in-memory stores provide a “session memory,” so the AI agent can recall user context, enabling human-like back-and-forth conversations. This design accommodates thousands of concurrent conversations with ultra-low latency—ideal for customer support, voice-based digital assistants, and hands-free IoT applications.
nodes.author: PricewaterhouseCoopers

nodes.label: Regulatory compliance in banking A global bank applies the same architectural pr
nodes.text: Regulatory compliance in banking A global bank applies the same architectural principles for Basel III/IV reporting. Trading systems publish large volumes of data to Kafka in diverse formats. A BPMN workflow orchestrator (Camunda 8) coordinates AI agents that standardise and validate data. Redis ensures idempotent processing, preventing duplicate handling of events. Meanwhile, container orchestration (Kubernetes) auto-scales infrastructure based on trading volumes, ensuring cost optimisation. The entire pipeline is fully auditable—critical for meeting regulatory requirements.
