nodes.text: Implementation includes role-based permissions, adversarial input detection, supply chain validation, and behavioral monitoring.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: API gateways equipped with authentication frameworks and threat detection capabi
nodes.text: API gateways equipped with authentication frameworks and threat detection capabilities control all AI models and tool interactions, while circuit breakers automatically prevent cascade failures and maintain system availability through graceful degradation.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: The monitoring infrastructure at this level proves critical for enterprise adopt
nodes.text: The monitoring infrastructure at this level proves critical for enterprise adoption.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Organizations must track API costs, token usage, and security events from the ou
nodes.text: Organizations must track API costs, token usage, and security events from the outset.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Many enterprises discover post-deployment that inadequate cost tracking led to b
nodes.text: Many enterprises discover post-deployment that inadequate cost tracking led to budget overruns or that insufficient security monitoring exposed them to novel attack vectors.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Reasoning Transparency with Continuous Evaluation addresses the accountability r
nodes.text: Reasoning Transparency with Continuous Evaluation addresses the accountability requirements that distinguish enterprise AI from experimental deployments.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: This pattern structures AI decision-making into auditable processes with integra
nodes.text: This pattern structures AI decision-making into auditable processes with integrated bias detection, hallucination monitoring, and confidence scoring.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Automated quality assessment continuously tracks reasoning consistency while cap
nodes.text: Automated quality assessment continuously tracks reasoning consistency while capturing decision rationale, alternative approaches, and demographic impact indicators. This capability proves essential for regulatory compliance and model risk management.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: In enterprise environments, explainability consistently outweighs raw performanc
nodes.text: In enterprise environments, explainability consistently outweighs raw performance in determining deployment success. Systems that clearly demonstrate their reasoning processes earn broader organizational adoption than more accurate but opaque alternatives.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Data Lifecycle Governance with Ethical Safeguards completes the foundational fra
nodes.text: Data Lifecycle Governance with Ethical Safeguards completes the foundational framework by implementing systematic information protection. This pattern manages data through classification schemes, encryption protocols, purpose limitation, and automated consent management.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Public information remains accessible while personally identifiable information
nodes.text: Public information remains accessible while personally identifiable information (PII) and PHI receive differential privacy protection. Highly sensitive data undergoes pseudonymization techniques that facilitate compliance verification without exposing underlying information.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Automated retention enforcement is critical to long-term success.
nodes.text: Automated retention enforcement is critical to long-term success.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Manual processes for right-to-deletion and data lifecycle management cannot scal
nodes.text: Manual processes for right-to-deletion and data lifecycle management cannot scale with enterprise AI deployments.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Systems must think about data relationships without retaining sensitive informat
nodes.text: Systems must think about data relationships without retaining sensitive information in active memory, ensuring both functional capability and regulatory compliance.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Together, these foundation tier patterns help lay the governance infrastructure
nodes.text: Together, these foundation tier patterns help lay the governance infrastructure with embedded security monitoring, continuous quality assessment, and ethical safeguards. This is essential to enable all subsequent AI capabilities that we cover next
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Workflow Tier: Implementing Structured Autonomy
nodes.text: Workflow Tier: Implementing Structured Autonomy
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Once the Foundation Tier has established trust and demonstrated value, organizat
nodes.text: Once the Foundation Tier has established trust and demonstrated value, organizations can advance to Workflow Tier implementations where meaningful business transformation begins.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: In this tier, orchestration patterns manage multiple AI interactions across flex
nodes.text: In this tier, orchestration patterns manage multiple AI interactions across flexible execution paths, while preserving the determinism and oversight needed for complex business operations.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Tier 2: Implementing Structured Autonomy Here, Constrained Autonomy Zones with C
nodes.text: Tier 2: Implementing Structured Autonomy Here, Constrained Autonomy Zones with Change Management bridges foundational controls with business process automation.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: This approach defines secure operational boundaries where AI systems can operate
nodes.text: This approach defines secure operational boundaries where AI systems can operate independently while leveraging the cost controls, performance monitoring, and governance frameworks established in the Foundation Tier.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Workflows tier incorporate mandatory checkpoints for validation, compliance veri
nodes.text: Workflows tier incorporate mandatory checkpoints for validation, compliance verification, and human oversight, with automated escalation procedures that account for organizational change resistance patterns.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Between these checkpoints, AI systems optimize their approaches, retry failed op
nodes.text: Between these checkpoints, AI systems optimize their approaches, retry failed operations, and adapt to changing conditions within predefined constraints for cost, ethics, and performance.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: The key insight gained is to perform gradual autonomy expansion based on measure
nodes.text: The key insight gained is to perform gradual autonomy expansion based on measured outcomes and demonstrated user confidence, while tracking adoption rates alongside technical performance metrics.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Workflow Orchestration with Comprehensive Monitoring represents the operational
nodes.text: Workflow Orchestration with Comprehensive Monitoring represents the operational core of this tier, decomposing complex business processes into coordinated components with real-time quality assessment.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: This orchestration enables independent optimization of individual steps while en
nodes.text: This orchestration enables independent optimization of individual steps while ensuring proper sequencing, error handling, and bias detection throughout the complete workflow.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Five essential orchestration patterns emerge within this workflow tier :
nodes.text: Five essential orchestration patterns emerge within this workflow tier :
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Prompt Chaining extends the reasoning transparency from Foundation Tier across m
nodes.text: Prompt Chaining extends the reasoning transparency from Foundation Tier across multi-step task sequences. Complex work decomposes into predictable steps with validation gates, accuracy verification, and bias assessments between each component. Continuous monitoring tracks output quality and reasoning consistency across the complete execution chain, ensuring reliability and maintaining auditability.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Routing leverages established security and governance frameworks to classify inp
nodes.text: Routing leverages established security and governance frameworks to classify inputs using confidence thresholds and fairness criteria. Tasks route to specialized agents while monitoring systems track demographic disparities and ensure optimal cost-capability matching with equitable treatment across user populations. This pattern enables organizations to balance expensive, capable models with efficient, targeted solutions.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Parallelization utilizes the robust monitoring infrastructure to process indepen
nodes.text: Parallelization utilizes the robust monitoring infrastructure to process independent subtasks simultaneously with sophisticated result aggregation, conflict resolution, and consensus validation. Bias detection prevents systematic discrimination while load balancing ensures efficient resource utilization.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Evaluator-Optimizer extends continuous evaluation capabilities into iterative re
nodes.text: Evaluator-Optimizer extends continuous evaluation capabilities into iterative refinement processes. Self-correction loops operate with convergence detection, cost controls, and quality improvement tracking while preventing infinite iterations and ensuring productive outcomes that justify computational investment.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Orchestrator-Workers employs the comprehensive monitoring framework for dynamic
nodes.text: Orchestrator-Workers employs the comprehensive monitoring framework for dynamic planning with load balancing, failure handling, and adaptive replanning based on intermediate results. This pattern provides efficient resource utilization while maintaining visibility into distributed decision-making processes.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: This orchestrated approach transforms solid foundational infrastructure into dyn
nodes.text: This orchestrated approach transforms solid foundational infrastructure into dynamic business capability, enabling AI systems to handle complex processes while operating within governance boundaries that maintain enterprise confidence. And, this naturally brings us to the final tier.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Autonomous Tier: Enabling Dynamic Intelligence
nodes.text: Autonomous Tier: Enabling Dynamic Intelligence
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: The progression from structured workflows leads naturally to the Autonomous Tier
nodes.text: The progression from structured workflows leads naturally to the Autonomous Tier (i.e., advanced implementations that allow agentic AI systems to determine their own execution strategies based on high-level objectives).
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: This autonomy becomes feasible only through the sophisticated monitoring, safety
nodes.text: This autonomy becomes feasible only through the sophisticated monitoring, safety constraints, and ethical boundaries established in previous tiers.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Tier 3: Enabling Dynamic Intelligence Goal-Directed Planning with Ethical Bounda
nodes.text: Tier 3: Enabling Dynamic Intelligence Goal-Directed Planning with Ethical Boundaries represents the culmination of Foundation Tier ethical safeguards and Workflow Tier orchestration capabilities.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Systems receive strategic objectives and operate within ethical constraints, saf
nodes.text: Systems receive strategic objectives and operate within ethical constraints, safety boundaries, cost budgets, and performance targets established through lower-tier implementations.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Planning processes incorporate uncertainty quantification, alternative strategy
nodes.text: Planning processes incorporate uncertainty quantification, alternative strategy development, and comprehensive stakeholder impact assessment while continuous monitoring ensures autonomous decisions align with organizational values and regulatory requirements.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Adaptive Learning with Bias Prevention extends the continuous evaluation framewo
nodes.text: Adaptive Learning with Bias Prevention extends the continuous evaluation frameworks from previous tiers into self-improvement capabilities.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Systems refine their approaches based on environmental feedback including tool e
nodes.text: Systems refine their approaches based on environmental feedback including tool execution results, user satisfaction metrics, and fairness indicators across demographic groups.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Learning mechanisms incorporate active bias correction to enhance performance wi
nodes.text: Learning mechanisms incorporate active bias correction to enhance performance without amplifying existing inequalities or creating new forms of discrimination.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Collaboration with Conflict Resolution coordinates specialized agent
nodes.text: Multi-Agent Collaboration with Conflict Resolution coordinates specialized agents through the structured communication protocols established in Workflow Tier implementations, enhanced with sophisticated conflict resolution, consensus mechanisms, and ethical arbitration.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Agents manage planning, execution, testing, and analysis while maintaining share
nodes.text: Agents manage planning, execution, testing, and analysis while maintaining shared context and synchronized ethical standards that prevent echo chambers or biased consensus formation.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: In short, autonomous tier require the sophisticated monitoring, cost controls, a
nodes.text: In short, autonomous tier require the sophisticated monitoring, cost controls, and governance frameworks that Foundation and Workflow tiers provide.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: They operate most effectively in controlled environments with strict resource li
nodes.text: They operate most effectively in controlled environments with strict resource limits, comprehensive safety monitoring, and explicit regulatory approval, demanding robust exception handling and clear escalation procedures that only mature foundational infrastructure can support.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Industry-Specific Implementation Approaches
nodes.text: Industry-Specific Implementation Approaches
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Our three-tier progression manifests differently across industries, reflecting u
nodes.text: Our three-tier progression manifests differently across industries, reflecting unique regulatory environments, risk tolerances, customer expectations and operational requirements.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Understanding these industry-specific approaches enables organizations to tailor
nodes.text: Understanding these industry-specific approaches enables organizations to tailor their implementation strategies while maintaining systematic capability development.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Let’s look at some industry examples: Financial services represents, perhaps the
nodes.text: Let’s look at some industry examples: Financial services represents, perhaps the most challenging environment for agentic AI deployment.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Financial institutions leverage AI capabilities for fraud detection, risk assess
nodes.text: Financial institutions leverage AI capabilities for fraud detection, risk assessment, and customer service while operating under increasingly strict regulatory oversight focused on algorithmic fairness and discriminatory impact prevention.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: This creates a natural emphasis on Foundation Tier implementations with comprehe
nodes.text: This creates a natural emphasis on Foundation Tier implementations with comprehensive Tool Orchestration providing strict governance, threat detection, and bias monitoring for all financial system interactions.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Reasoning transparency becomes critical for defensible decision-making with demo
nodes.text: Reasoning transparency becomes critical for defensible decision-making with demographic impact tracking, while Data Lifecycle Governance incorporates aggressive tokenization, consent management, and fairness verification protocols.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: For example, Workflow Tier advancement for loan underwriting and algorithmic tra
nodes.text: For example, Workflow Tier advancement for loan underwriting and algorithmic trading requires mandatory human checkpoints, comprehensive bias testing, and equitable outcome monitoring.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Autonomous patterns remain largely experimental due to regulatory constraints th
nodes.text: Autonomous patterns remain largely experimental due to regulatory constraints that demand the transparency and control only mature Foundation implementations provide.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Healthcare Agentic deployment carries the highest stakes, where patient safety a
nodes.text: Healthcare Agentic deployment carries the highest stakes, where patient safety and health equity concerns make the systematic tier progression essential.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Healthcare organizations must ensure AI systems augment clinical judgment while
nodes.text: Healthcare organizations must ensure AI systems augment clinical judgment while maintaining strict compliance with privacy regulations and ethical standards.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Where, Foundation Tier implementation prioritizes Data Lifecycle Governance for
nodes.text: Where, Foundation Tier implementation prioritizes Data Lifecycle Governance for PHI with FHIR compliance and comprehensive consent management, Tool Orchestration with stringent access controls for electronic health records (EHRs) and medical devices, and Reasoning Transparency for AI-assisted diagnosis with clinical evidence tracking and fairness validation.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Then, Workflow Tier progression focuses on administrative automation and clinica
nodes.text: Then, Workflow Tier progression focuses on administrative automation and clinical workflow support with mandatory human oversight, health equity assessments, and patient safety checkpoints that leverage Foundation monitoring capabilities.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Autonomous tier remain highly restricted, requiring the mature governance framew
nodes.text: Autonomous tier remain highly restricted, requiring the mature governance frameworks that comprehensive Foundation and Workflow implementations provide.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Retail organizations demonstrate how tier progression enables personalization at
nodes.text: Retail organizations demonstrate how tier progression enables personalization at scale while ensuring customer fairness across diverse populations.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Retailers must balance intimate personalization with global optimization while p
nodes.text: Retailers must balance intimate personalization with global optimization while preventing discriminatory practices that could harm brand reputation or violate emerging regulations.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Implementation leverages Foundation Tier for comprehensive PII protection and se
nodes.text: Implementation leverages Foundation Tier for comprehensive PII protection and secure access with bias detection throughout customer-facing systems.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Workflow Tier provides sophisticated customer service routing with fairness vali
nodes.text: Workflow Tier provides sophisticated customer service routing with fairness validation, order processing with integrated fraud detection, personalized content generation with demographic equity verification, and inventory management with demand forecasting capabilities.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Autonomous pattern exploration in dynamic pricing and supply chain optimization
nodes.text: Autonomous pattern exploration in dynamic pricing and supply chain optimization becomes viable within controlled contexts because Foundation-level fairness monitoring ensures equitable treatment across customer segments and geographic regions.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Manufacturing showcases how systematic tier progression manages the intersection
nodes.text: Manufacturing showcases how systematic tier progression manages the intersection of AI capabilities with physical safety requirements and workforce impact considerations.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Manufacturing organizations must maintain absolute precision and safety while ma
nodes.text: Manufacturing organizations must maintain absolute precision and safety while managing workforce transitions as AI augments human capabilities.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Foundation Tier focuses on operational technology/information technology (OT/IT)
nodes.text: Foundation Tier focuses on operational technology/information technology (OT/IT) security integration with comprehensive threat detection and workforce impact and safety monitoring, Tool Orchestration for machinery and sensor integration with safety protocols, creating the safety framework required for advanced automation.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Workflow Tier enables production sequence automation with quality validation, co
nodes.text: Workflow Tier enables production sequence automation with quality validation, computer vision quality control with bias and anomaly detection systems, and predictive maintenance coordination with workforce planning considerations.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Autonomous patterns supporting predictive maintenance and dynamic scheduling bec
nodes.text: Autonomous patterns supporting predictive maintenance and dynamic scheduling become feasible within strict safety boundaries because Foundation monitoring capabilities ensure comprehensive workforce impact assessments and ethical automation guidelines that consider broader community effects.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Implementation Strategy and Guiding Principles
nodes.text: Implementation Strategy and Guiding Principles
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Successful deployment of these three-tier progression depends on combining techn
nodes.text: Successful deployment of these three-tier progression depends on combining technical excellence with ethical responsibility and strong change management. These four implementation phases help move safely through each capability tier while keeping security, governance and trust at the center.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Establish Foundation Tier Patterns
nodes.text: Establish Foundation Tier Patterns
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Implement Tool Orchestration with Enterprise Security, Reasoning Transparency wi
nodes.text: Implement Tool Orchestration with Enterprise Security, Reasoning Transparency with Continuous Evaluation, and Data Lifecycle Governance with Ethical Safeguards. Include threat modeling, bias testing, and human-AI collaboration rules to earn trust early.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Demonstrate Foundation Tier Value
nodes.text: Demonstrate Foundation Tier Value
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Execute controlled pilots using foundation infrastructure to prove security comp
nodes.text: Execute controlled pilots using foundation infrastructure to prove security compliance, cost visibility, and trust building. Begin in non-critical areas, train teams, and measure adoption alongside technical performance before scaling.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Expand Workflow Tier Patterns
nodes.text: Expand Workflow Tier Patterns
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Deploy Constrained Autonomy Zones and the five core orchestration patterns we di
nodes.text: Deploy Constrained Autonomy Zones and the five core orchestration patterns we discussed in this article earlier (Prompt Chaining, Routing, Parallelization, Evaluator-Optimizer, Orchestrator-Workers) for business integration.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Advance only when foundation value is proven, maintaining comprehensive monitori
nodes.text: Advance only when foundation value is proven, maintaining comprehensive monitoring.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Explore Autonomous Tier Capabilities
nodes.text: Explore Autonomous Tier Capabilities
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Test Goal-Directed Planning with Ethical Boundaries, Adaptive Learning with Bias
nodes.text: Test Goal-Directed Planning with Ethical Boundaries, Adaptive Learning with Bias Prevention, and Multi-Agent Collaboration in controlled environments with regulatory approval. Require comprehensive safety monitoring while planning for emerging regulations like the EU AI Act.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Agentic AI Implementation Roadmap
nodes.text: Agentic AI Implementation Roadmap
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Act Now, Build Sustainably
nodes.text: Act Now, Build Sustainably
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: The enterprise agentic AI landscape is at an inflection point.
nodes.text: The enterprise agentic AI landscape is at an inflection point.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Early implementations reveal a clear pattern: organizations that prioritize gove
nodes.text: Early implementations reveal a clear pattern: organizations that prioritize governance foundations consistently outperform those chasing autonomous capabilities first.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Our three-tier progression isn't theoretical, it reflects the successful deploym
nodes.text: Our three-tier progression isn't theoretical, it reflects the successful deployment patterns emerging across industries.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: We strongly recommend progressing deliberately through each tier.
nodes.text: We strongly recommend progressing deliberately through each tier.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: Prove security compliance and stakeholder trust before expanding scope.
nodes.text: Prove security compliance and stakeholder trust before expanding scope.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: The companies building systematic capabilities now will dominate the next phase
nodes.text: The companies building systematic capabilities now will dominate the next phase of enterprise AI, while those rushing to autonomy face increasing regulatory scrutiny and operational risk.
nodes.author: InfoQ

nodes.label: The competitive advantage of Agentic AI belongs to organizations that master gov
nodes.text: The competitive advantage of Agentic AI belongs to organizations that master governance-enabled autonomy, not ungoverned automation.

nodes.label: How Agentic AI Empowers Architecture Governance
nodes.text: How Agentic AI Empowers Architecture Governance
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: By Neal Ford and Mark Richards November 19, 2025 • 9 minute read One of the prin
nodes.text: By Neal Ford and Mark Richards November 19, 2025 • 9 minute read One of the principles in our upcoming book Architecture as Code is the ability for architects to design automated governance checks for important architectural concerns, creating fast feedback loops when things go awry.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: This idea isn’t new—Neal and his coauthors Rebecca Parsons and Patrick Kua espou
nodes.text: This idea isn’t new—Neal and his coauthors Rebecca Parsons and Patrick Kua espoused this idea back in 2017 in the first edition of Building Evolutionary Architectures , and many of our clients adopted these practices with great success.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: However, our most ambitious goals were largely thwarted by a common problem in m
nodes.text: However, our most ambitious goals were largely thwarted by a common problem in modern architectures: brittleness .
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Fortunately, the advent of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and agentic AI have
nodes.text: Fortunately, the advent of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and agentic AI have largely solved this problem for enterprise architects.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Fitness Functions
nodes.text: Fitness Functions
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Building Evolutionary Architectures defines the concept of an architectural fitn
nodes.text: Building Evolutionary Architectures defines the concept of an architectural fitness function: any mechanism that provides an objective integrity check for architectural characteristics. Architects can think of fitness functions sort of like unit tests, but for architectural concerns.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: While many fitness functions run like unit tests to test structure (using tools
nodes.text: While many fitness functions run like unit tests to test structure (using tools like ArchUnit , NetArchTest , PyTestArch , arch-go , and so on), architects can write fitness functions to validate all sorts of important checks…like tasks normally reserved for relational databases.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Fitness functions and referential integrity
nodes.text: Fitness functions and referential integrity
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Consider the architecture illustrated in Figure 1.
nodes.text: Consider the architecture illustrated in Figure 1.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Figure 1: Strategically splitting a database in a distributed architecture
nodes.text: Figure 1: Strategically splitting a database in a distributed architecture
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In Figure 1, the team has decided to split the data into two databases for bette
nodes.text: In Figure 1, the team has decided to split the data into two databases for better scalability and availability.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: However, the common disadvantage of that approach lies with the fact that the te
nodes.text: However, the common disadvantage of that approach lies with the fact that the team can no longer rely on the database to enforce referential integrity.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In this situation, each ticket must have a corresponding customer to model this
nodes.text: In this situation, each ticket must have a corresponding customer to model this workflow correctly.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: While many teams seem to think that referential integrity is only possible withi
nodes.text: While many teams seem to think that referential integrity is only possible within a relational database, we separate the governance activity (data integrity) from the implementation (the relational database) and realize we can create our own check using an architectural fitness function, as shown in Figure 2.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Figure 2: Implementing referential integrity as a fitness function
nodes.text: Figure 2: Implementing referential integrity as a fitness function
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In Figure 2, the architect has created a small fitness function that monitors th
nodes.text: In Figure 2, the architect has created a small fitness function that monitors the queue between customer and ticket .
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: When the queue depth drops to zero (meaning that the system isn’t processing any
nodes.text: When the queue depth drops to zero (meaning that the system isn’t processing any messages), the fitness function creates a set of customer keys from the customer service and a set of customer foreign keys from the ticket service and asserts that all of the ticket foreign keys are contained within the set of customer keys.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Why not just query the databases directly from the fitness function?
nodes.text: Why not just query the databases directly from the fitness function?
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Abstracting them as sets allows flexibility—querying across databases on a const
nodes.text: Abstracting them as sets allows flexibility—querying across databases on a constant basis introduces overhead that may have negative side effects.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Abstracting the fitness function check from the mechanics of how the data is sto
nodes.text: Abstracting the fitness function check from the mechanics of how the data is stored to an abstract data structure has at least a couple of advantages.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: First, using sets allows architects to cache nonvolatile data (like customer key
nodes.text: First, using sets allows architects to cache nonvolatile data (like customer keys), avoiding constant querying of the database.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Many solutions exist for write-through caches in the rare event we do add a cust
nodes.text: Many solutions exist for write-through caches in the rare event we do add a customer.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Second, using sets of keys abstracts us from actual data items.
nodes.text: Second, using sets of keys abstracts us from actual data items.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Data engineers prefer synthetic keys to using domain data; the same is true for
nodes.text: Data engineers prefer synthetic keys to using domain data; the same is true for architects.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: While the database schema might change over time, the team will always need the
nodes.text: While the database schema might change over time, the team will always need the relationship between customers and tickets , which this fitness function validates in an abstract way.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Who executes this code?
nodes.text: Who executes this code?
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: As this problem is typical in distributed architectures such as microservices, t
nodes.text: As this problem is typical in distributed architectures such as microservices, the common place to execute this governance code is within the service mesh of the microservices architecture.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Service mesh is a general pattern for handling operational concerns in microserv
nodes.text: Service mesh is a general pattern for handling operational concerns in microservices, such as logging, monitoring, naming, service discovery, and other nondomain concerns.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In mature microservices ecosystems, the service mesh also acts as a governance m
nodes.text: In mature microservices ecosystems, the service mesh also acts as a governance mesh , applying fitness functions and other rules at runtime.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: This is a common way that architects at the application level can validate data
nodes.text: This is a common way that architects at the application level can validate data integrity, and we’ve implemented these types of fitness functions on hundreds of projects.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: However, the specificity of the implementation details makes it difficult to exp
nodes.text: However, the specificity of the implementation details makes it difficult to expand the scope of these types of fitness functions to the enterprise architect level because they include too many implementation details about how the project works.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Brittleness for metadomains
nodes.text: Brittleness for metadomains
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: One of the key lessons from domain-driven design was the idea of keeping impleme
nodes.text: One of the key lessons from domain-driven design was the idea of keeping implementation details as tightly bound as possible, using anticorruption layers to prevent integration points from understanding too many details. Architects have embraced this philosophy in architectures like microservices.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Yet we see the same problem here at the metalevel, where enterprise architects w
nodes.text: Yet we see the same problem here at the metalevel, where enterprise architects would like to broadly control concerns like data integrity yet are hampered by the distance and specificity of the governance requirement.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Distance refers to the scope of the activity.
nodes.text: Distance refers to the scope of the activity.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: While application and integration architects have a narrow scope of responsibili
nodes.text: While application and integration architects have a narrow scope of responsibility, enterprise architects by their nature sit at the enterprise level.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Thus, for an enterprise architect to enforce governance such as referential inte
nodes.text: Thus, for an enterprise architect to enforce governance such as referential integrity requires them to know too many specific details about how the team has implemented the project.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: One of our biggest global clients has a role within their enterprise architectur
nodes.text: One of our biggest global clients has a role within their enterprise architecture group called evolutionary architect , whose job is to identify global governance concerns, and we have other clients who have tried to implement this level of holistic governance with their enterprise architects.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: However, the brittleness defeats these efforts: As soon as the team needs to cha
nodes.text: However, the brittleness defeats these efforts: As soon as the team needs to change an implementation detail, the fitness function breaks.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Even though we often couch fitness functions as “unit tests for architecture,” i
nodes.text: Even though we often couch fitness functions as “unit tests for architecture,” in reality, they break much less often than unit tests.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: (How often do changes affect some fundamental architectural concern versus a cha
nodes.text: (How often do changes affect some fundamental architectural concern versus a change to the domain?)
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: However, by exposing implementation details outside the project to enterprise ar
nodes.text: However, by exposing implementation details outside the project to enterprise architects, these fitness functions do break enough to limit their value.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: We’ve tried a variety of anticorruption layers for metaconcerns, but generative
nodes.text: We’ve tried a variety of anticorruption layers for metaconcerns, but generative AI and MCP have provided the best solution to date.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: MCP and Agentic Governance
nodes.text: MCP and Agentic Governance
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: MCP defines a general integration layer for agents to query and consume capabili
nodes.text: MCP defines a general integration layer for agents to query and consume capabilities within a particular metascope.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: For example, teams can set up an MCP server at the application or integration ar
nodes.text: For example, teams can set up an MCP server at the application or integration architecture level to expose tools and data sources to AI agents.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: This provides the perfect anticorruption layer for enterprise architects to stat
nodes.text: This provides the perfect anticorruption layer for enterprise architects to state the intent of governance without relying on implementation details.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: This allows teams to implement the type of governance that the strategically min
nodes.text: This allows teams to implement the type of governance that the strategically minded enterprise architects want but create a level of indirection for the details. For example, see the updated referential integrity check illustrated in Figure 3.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Figure 3. Using MCP for indirection to hide the fitness function implementation
nodes.text: Figure 3. Using MCP for indirection to hide the fitness function implementation details
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In Figure 3, the enterprise architect issues the general request to validate ref
nodes.text: In Figure 3, the enterprise architect issues the general request to validate referential integrity to the MCP server for the project. It in turn exposes fitness functions via tools (or data sources such as log files) to carry out the request.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: By creating an anticorruption layer between the project details and enterprise a
nodes.text: By creating an anticorruption layer between the project details and enterprise architect, we can use MCP to handle implementation details so that when the project evolves in the future, it doesn’t break the governance because of brittleness , as shown in Figure 4.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Figure 4. Using agentic AI to create metalevel indirection
nodes.text: Figure 4. Using agentic AI to create metalevel indirection
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In Figure 4, the enterprise architect concern ( validate referential integrity )
nodes.text: In Figure 4, the enterprise architect concern ( validate referential integrity ) hasn’t changed, but the project details have.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: The team added another service for experts , who work on tickets, meaning we now
nodes.text: The team added another service for experts , who work on tickets, meaning we now need to validate integrity across three databases.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: The team changes the internal MCP tool that implements the fitness function, and
nodes.text: The team changes the internal MCP tool that implements the fitness function, and the enterprise architect request stays the same.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: This allows enterprise architects to effectively state governance intent without
nodes.text: This allows enterprise architects to effectively state governance intent without diving into implementation details, removing the brittleness of far-reaching fitness functions and enabling much more proactive holistic governance by architects at all levels.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Defining the Intersections of Architecture
nodes.text: Defining the Intersections of Architecture
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In Architecture as Code , we discuss nine different intersections with software
nodes.text: In Architecture as Code , we discuss nine different intersections with software architecture and other parts of the software development ecosystem (data representing one of them), all expressed as architectural fitness functions (the “code” part of architecture as code).
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: In defining the intersection of architecture and enterprise architect, we can us
nodes.text: In defining the intersection of architecture and enterprise architect, we can use MCP and agents to state intent holistically, deferring the actual details to individual projects and ecosystems.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: This solves one of the nagging problems for enterprise architects who want to bu
nodes.text: This solves one of the nagging problems for enterprise architects who want to build more automated feedback loops within their systems.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: MCP is almost ideally suited for this purpose, designed to expose tools, data so
nodes.text: MCP is almost ideally suited for this purpose, designed to expose tools, data sources, and prompt libraries to external contexts outside a particular project domain. This allows enterprise architects to holistically define broad intent and leave it to teams to implement (and evolve) their solutions.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: X as code (where X can be a wide variety of things) typically arises when the so
nodes.text: X as code (where X can be a wide variety of things) typically arises when the software development ecosystem reaches a certain level of maturity and automation.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Teams tried for years to make infrastructure as code work, but it didn’t until t
nodes.text: Teams tried for years to make infrastructure as code work, but it didn’t until tools such as Puppet and Chef came along that could enable that capability.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: The same is true with other “as code” initiatives (security, policy, and so on):
nodes.text: The same is true with other “as code” initiatives (security, policy, and so on): The ecosystem needs to provide tools and frameworks to allow it to work.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.label: Now, with the combination of powerful fitness function libraries for a wide vari
nodes.text: Now, with the combination of powerful fitness function libraries for a wide variety of platforms and ecosystem innovations such as MCP and agentic AI, architecture itself has enough support to join the “as code” communities.
nodes.author: Neal Ford, Mark Richards

nodes.text: How to build an AI-ready organization: the Enterprise Intelligence Architecture
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: In this interview with IDC's Marlanna Bozicevich, we learn about how organizatio
nodes.text: In this interview with IDC's Marlanna Bozicevich, we learn about how organizations need to prioritize data management to take advantage of AI as a transformative platform. We also hit on how AI can support data management.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: I spoke with IDC’s Marlanna Bozicevich, Research Analyst, Data Platforms , at ID
nodes.text: I spoke with IDC’s Marlanna Bozicevich, Research Analyst, Data Platforms , at IDC’s 60th annual Directions conference .
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Marlanna and her analyst colleagues advise senior IT decision makers around tech
nodes.text: Marlanna and her analyst colleagues advise senior IT decision makers around technologies such as agentic AI. In our conversation we get the benefit of that advice as we discuss how organizations need to manage data to take advantage of agentic and generative AI.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Marlanna proposes what she calls the Enterprise Intelligence Architecture, in wh
nodes.text: Marlanna proposes what she calls the Enterprise Intelligence Architecture, in which data is considered across four planes, laddering up to business activity.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.text: You can watch our conversation here , or via the YouTube player below.

nodes.label: AI and data management
nodes.text: AI and data management
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: I asked Marlanna what is top of mind for the IT leaders with whom she works?
nodes.text: I asked Marlanna what is top of mind for the IT leaders with whom she works?
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: She said that in the data-management space, there are three general areas where
nodes.text: She said that in the data-management space, there are three general areas where IT buyers are thinking about AI:
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Productivity: Bringing AI automation into data management to improve operational
nodes.text: Productivity: Bringing AI automation into data management to improve operational efficiency (including automating repetitive tasks, and natural language interactions).
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Responsible AI: Properly managing intelligence about your data.
nodes.text: Responsible AI: Properly managing intelligence about your data.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Agentic AI: The next phase of AI maturity, another tool for bringing automation
nodes.text: Agentic AI: The next phase of AI maturity, another tool for bringing automation into data management now in the form of autonomous action. In the agentic age real-time data becomes crucial.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: AI for data / data for AI
nodes.text: AI for data / data for AI
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: When we think about data and AI, we tend to focus on the impact AI can have when
nodes.text: When we think about data and AI, we tend to focus on the impact AI can have when fueled with quality data. Marlanna is keen that organizations also consider AI as a means of improving data quality.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: I asked her in what ways she sees AI being used to improve data processes and bo
nodes.text: I asked her in what ways she sees AI being used to improve data processes and boost productivity among data teams, and how does this impact other parts of the organization?
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: She said there are two sides to boosting productivity in data-management practic
nodes.text: She said there are two sides to boosting productivity in data-management practices:
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: AI for data: Finding ways to improve the processes of data engineers, data scien
nodes.text: AI for data: Finding ways to improve the processes of data engineers, data scientists, data stewards (adding automated features to improve the data personas workflow).
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Data for AI: How the cleaned, AI-ready data can be used internally in other part
nodes.text: Data for AI: How the cleaned, AI-ready data can be used internally in other parts of the organization to boost productivity (natural language interface for business personas, data democratization, data access, enhanced collaboration between data and business teams).
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Treat data as a product
nodes.text: Treat data as a product
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: That’s managing data within an organization to drive efficiency. I asked Marlann
nodes.text: That’s managing data within an organization to drive efficiency. I asked Marlanna how can IT buyers use their now efficiently managed data to power AI workloads?
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: She said it is important to treat data as a product.
nodes.text: She said it is important to treat data as a product.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Data products are a key success factor for AI initiatives, including agents.
nodes.text: Data products are a key success factor for AI initiatives, including agents.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: In Marlanna’s view data products will be key to facilitating data exchange among
nodes.text: In Marlanna’s view data products will be key to facilitating data exchange among agents.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Challenges will arise in standardization.
nodes.text: Challenges will arise in standardization.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: (See also: Can AI solve your technical debt problem? )
nodes.text: (See also: Can AI solve your technical debt problem? )
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Building the AI-ready organization
nodes.text: Building the AI-ready organization
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: For any business to generate ROI in AI, inside or out, organizations need to be
nodes.text: For any business to generate ROI in AI, inside or out, organizations need to be AI ready.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Data and the management of and access to data, is critical for the successful im
nodes.text: Data and the management of and access to data, is critical for the successful implementation of AI projects.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: I know from my own conversations with IT buyers that there is some tension betwe
nodes.text: I know from my own conversations with IT buyers that there is some tension between what AI can do conceptually, and what AI can do for a living organization, warts and all.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: IT leaders need to get their organizations AI ready.
nodes.text: IT leaders need to get their organizations AI ready.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Here Marlanna introduces the concept of Enterprise Intelligence Architecture for
nodes.text: Here Marlanna introduces the concept of Enterprise Intelligence Architecture for the AI fueled business. She says that organizations need to think about data in four planes: The four planes of the Enterprise Intelligence Architecture
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Data plane.
nodes.text: Data plane.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Data control plane.
nodes.text: Data control plane.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Data synthesis plane.
nodes.text: Data synthesis plane.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Business activity plane.
nodes.text: Business activity plane.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: Each plane requires focus and resource so that the organization is capturing, st
nodes.text: Each plane requires focus and resource so that the organization is capturing, storing and accessing data in a way that is useful to business outputs. Functions across the architecture – the four planes – come together to deliver AI-ready data in the enterprise.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: This structure and approach then supports continuous and collective learning, an
nodes.text: This structure and approach then supports continuous and collective learning, and the delivery of insights at scale.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: This in turn promotes data literacy and data culture, supporting continuous deve
nodes.text: This in turn promotes data literacy and data culture, supporting continuous development of the Architecture.
nodes.author: CIO

nodes.label: AI Architecture: Building Enterprise AI Systems with Governa
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Proven Architectural Design Patterns
nodes.text: Proven Architectural Design Patterns
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Established architectural design patterns help organizations build reliable syst
nodes.text: Established architectural design patterns help organizations build reliable systems. These patterns address common challenges around data management, model serving, and system integration. By following proven patterns, architects can avoid pitfalls and accelerate implementation across projects.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Key patterns include lakehouse approaches for unified data management, feature s
nodes.text: Key patterns include lakehouse approaches for unified data management, feature stores for reusable model inputs, and microservices for scalable deployments.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Each architectural design pattern solves specific problems and can be combined t
nodes.text: Each architectural design pattern solves specific problems and can be combined to create comprehensive solutions.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Architects should understand multiple patterns and select appropriate ones for t
nodes.text: Architects should understand multiple patterns and select appropriate ones for their needs.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The architectural design process benefits from documented patterns that capture
nodes.text: The architectural design process benefits from documented patterns that capture best practices. Organizations can explore different styles of implementation and choose approaches that align with their requirements. This structured approach to design reduces risk and improves outcomes.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Real-Time AI Applications
nodes.text: Real-Time AI Applications
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Many use cases require real-time responses to user input or streaming data. Syst
nodes.text: Many use cases require real-time responses to user input or streaming data. Systems must support low-latency model serving with the ability to scale based on demand. Real-time architecture involves monitoring infrastructure, automatic failover capabilities, and request routing across model versions.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations implement real-time processing to enable interactive applications.
nodes.text: Organizations implement real-time processing to enable interactive applications. This requires architectural design that minimizes latency while maintaining reliability. The architecture must process input quickly, generate predictions efficiently, and deliver results to users without delay.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Collaboration and Data Sharing
nodes.text: Collaboration and Data Sharing
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Effective architecture enables collaboration across teams and organizations.
nodes.text: Effective architecture enables collaboration across teams and organizations.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The design process should facilitate knowledge sharing and reuse of components a
nodes.text: The design process should facilitate knowledge sharing and reuse of components across different projects.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Modern platforms provide capabilities for secure data sharing without compromisi
nodes.text: Modern platforms provide capabilities for secure data sharing without compromising governance or creating duplication.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations can build applications collaboratively by establishing shared reso
nodes.text: Organizations can build applications collaboratively by establishing shared resources and common standards. This collaborative approach accelerates innovation and improves quality. Teams can leverage work from other groups, building on proven concepts and avoiding redundant efforts.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Cost Management Strategies
nodes.text: Cost Management Strategies
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Workloads consume significant compute resources, particularly during model train
nodes.text: Workloads consume significant compute resources, particularly during model training. Efficient architecture includes mechanisms to manage costs through automation and optimization. Organizations should explore different styles of compute provisioning to balance cost with performance needs.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The architectural design should provide visibility into resource utilization and
nodes.text: The architectural design should provide visibility into resource utilization and make it easy to identify optimization opportunities.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: By actively managing the lifecycle and implementing cost controls, organizations
nodes.text: By actively managing the lifecycle and implementing cost controls, organizations can build applications while controlling expenses.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: This includes automating resource scaling and implementing policies for resource
nodes.text: This includes automating resource scaling and implementing policies for resource allocation.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Monitoring and Maintenance
nodes.text: Monitoring and Maintenance
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Once deployed, models require continuous monitoring to maintain effectiveness. M
nodes.text: Once deployed, models require continuous monitoring to maintain effectiveness. Monitoring solutions track performance, data drift, and system health over time. These systems generate alerts when infrastructure deviates from expected behavior, enabling proactive maintenance.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The architecture must support updating models, rolling back changes, and managin
nodes.text: The architecture must support updating models, rolling back changes, and managing multiple versions simultaneously.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: This operational flexibility ensures applications remain reliable as conditions
nodes.text: This operational flexibility ensures applications remain reliable as conditions evolve.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations need processes to regularly review performance and plan improvemen
nodes.text: Organizations need processes to regularly review performance and plan improvements to their design.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Security in AI Architecture
nodes.text: Security in AI Architecture
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Security represents a critical pillar of any architecture. Organizations must pr
nodes.text: Security represents a critical pillar of any architecture. Organizations must protect data, models, and infrastructure from unauthorized access and misuse. The design should implement defense-in-depth with multiple layers of security controls throughout the system.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Key security considerations include access controls, encryption, network isolati
nodes.text: Key security considerations include access controls, encryption, network isolation, and audit logging. Architecture must address security at every layer, from data storage through model serving. Organizations should regularly assess security posture and update controls as threats evolve.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Future Developments
nodes.text: Future Developments
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence is reshaping architecture requi
nodes.text: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence is reshaping architecture requirements.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations integrate new capabilities while addressing evolving challenges.
nodes.text: Organizations integrate new capabilities while addressing evolving challenges.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Future architecture will incorporate specialized tools for managing advanced wor
nodes.text: Future architecture will incorporate specialized tools for managing advanced workflows and maintaining governance as technology develops.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: As artificial intelligence becomes central to operations, patterns will evolve t
nodes.text: As artificial intelligence becomes central to operations, patterns will evolve toward greater automation and intelligence.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The world continues to transform as innovations enable more sophisticated soluti
nodes.text: The world continues to transform as innovations enable more sophisticated solutions.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations must build flexible architecture that can adapt to incorporate fut
nodes.text: Organizations must build flexible architecture that can adapt to incorporate future developments while maintaining stability.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Building Your Strategy
nodes.text: Building Your Strategy
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Creating robust architecture requires balancing performance, governance, securit
nodes.text: Creating robust architecture requires balancing performance, governance, security, and cost.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations that invest in solid foundations position themselves to extract ma
nodes.text: Organizations that invest in solid foundations position themselves to extract maximum value from artificial intelligence investments.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Start by assessing current infrastructure and identifying gaps that could limit
nodes.text: Start by assessing current infrastructure and identifying gaps that could limit initiatives.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Establish governance policies and security controls from the beginning.
nodes.text: Establish governance policies and security controls from the beginning.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Choose platforms that support the entire lifecycle and enable collaboration acro
nodes.text: Choose platforms that support the entire lifecycle and enable collaboration across teams.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Consider how to integrate data files and assets into the design.
nodes.text: Consider how to integrate data files and assets into the design.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The architecture should handle diverse data types and support various use cases
nodes.text: The architecture should handle diverse data types and support various use cases across the organization.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Remember that architecture evolves as needs change and technology advances.
nodes.text: Remember that architecture evolves as needs change and technology advances.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: The transformation enabled by well-designed systems empowers organizations to au
nodes.text: The transformation enabled by well-designed systems empowers organizations to automate processes and create new solutions.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: With proper architectural design, organizations harness artificial intelligence
nodes.text: With proper architectural design, organizations harness artificial intelligence to drive competitive advantage in their industries.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Organizations can explore free resources to enhance their knowledge of best prac
nodes.text: Organizations can explore free resources to enhance their knowledge of best practices.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: By following proven architectural design patterns, you can build systems that de
nodes.text: By following proven architectural design patterns, you can build systems that deliver immediate value while providing flexibility for future innovations.
nodes.author: Databricks

nodes.label: Whether working on complex machine learning models or building applications at s
nodes.text: Whether working on complex machine learning models or building applications at scale, sound principles remain essential.

nodes.label: AI Agent Security: How to Safely Run Autonomous Agents (The 2026 Playbook)
nodes.text: AI Agent Security: How to Safely Run Autonomous Agents (The 2026 Playbook)
nodes.author: Stormap

nodes.label: Executive Summary: As AI agents gain the ability to browse the web, make payment
nodes.text: Executive Summary: As AI agents gain the ability to browse the web, make payments, and execute code autonomously, security becomes critical. This playbook covers the "treat agent as adversary" security model, sandboxing strategies, programmable guardrails, and lessons from real incidents — including agents extracting $40M from Polymarket. Includes practical OpenClaw security configurations.
nodes.author: Stormap

nodes.label: Source: Nate B Jones
nodes.text: Source: Nate B Jones
nodes.author: Stormap

nodes.label: The New Threat Model: Agents as Adversaries
nodes.text: The New Threat Model: Agents as Adversaries
nodes.author: Stormap

nodes.label: Traditional software security assumes programs do exactly what they're told. AI
nodes.text: Traditional software security assumes programs do exactly what they're told. AI agents are different — they're probabilistic, creative, and sometimes unpredictable. The new security model, advocated by Cloudflare and other infrastructure providers, treats every agent as a potential adversary .
